body:
## DJ BoBo at Ostravar Aréna — A Complete Concert Guide

Are you heading to DJ BoBo Arena Tour 2026 at Ostravar Aréna? Whether you're from Ostrava or traveling from the other side of the country, this guide will help you plan the entire evening stress-free. From transport to parking to tips on where to have dinner — everything in one place.

### Basic Event Information

- **What:** DJ BoBo Arena Tour 2026
- **When:** Friday, October 17, 2026
- **Where:** Ostravar Aréna, Ruská 3077/135, 700 30 Ostrava-Vítkovice
- **Doors open:** 6:30 PM
- **Concert starts:** 8:00 PM
- **Tickets:** [Buy at AKCE OSTRAVA](/akce/dj-bobo-arena-tour-2026-ostrava)

### How to Get to Ostravar Aréna

Ostravar Aréna is located in the Vítkovice district, in close proximity to Dolní oblast Vítkovice. It is easily accessible by both public transport and car.

**By tram (recommended):**
The most comfortable option, especially if you don't want to deal with parking. The following lines will take you to the arena:

- **Tram no. 1** — from the city center (Elektra or Náměstí Republiky stop)
- **Tram no. 2** — from Poruba and other parts of the city
- **Tram no. 11** — alternative connection

Get off at the **Městský stadion** or **Dolní oblast Vítkovice** stop. From the stop, it's a 3–5 minute walk directly to the arena. After the concert, extra services are usually provided.

**By bus:**
The Městský stadion stop is also served by several bus lines. Check the current timetables on the DPO (Ostrava Transport Company) website.

**By car:**
- From the D1 highway, take the Ostrava-Vítkovice exit and continue along Ruská street
- From Ostrava city center, the arena is approximately a 10-minute drive
- GPS coordinates: 49.8158N, 18.2789E

**By train:**
If you're traveling from a greater distance, both Ostrava-Svinov and Ostrava hlavní nádraží (main station) are connected to the tram network. From the main station, you can reach the arena by tram in approximately 15 minutes.

### Parking at Ostravar Aréna

Parking is one of the most common visitor questions. Here are your options:

| Parking Lot | Distance | Capacity | Note |
|---|---|---|---|
| P1 — directly at the arena | 0 min | approx. 500 spaces | Fills up first, arrive early |
| P2 — Dolní oblast Vítkovice | 5 min walk | approx. 800 spaces | Good alternative |
| P3 — Závodní street | 8 min walk | approx. 300 spaces | Less known, usually available |
| Street parking in surrounding areas | 5–15 min | variable | Free, but limited |

**Tip:** For major concerts, we recommend arriving at least an hour before doors open (around 5:30 PM) if you want to park in the immediate vicinity of the arena. Alternatively, use public transport — you'll avoid the hassle of parking and potential traffic jams after the concert.

### What to Bring and What to Leave at Home

Ostravar Aréna has standard security rules for concert events:

**Allowed:**
- Small bags and purses
- Mobile phone (photos and video recording allowed)
- Wallet, keys, personal belongings

**Prohibited:**
- Professional cameras with interchangeable lenses
- Outside food and drinks (refreshment stands are available inside the arena)
- Larger backpacks and bags
- Selfie sticks
- Any dangerous items

A security check takes place at entry — expect queues, especially just before the show starts. That's why we recommend arriving early.

### Refreshments Inside and Outside the Arena

**Inside the arena** you'll find several refreshment stands offering:
- Beer, wine, non-alcoholic drinks
- Hot dogs, nachos, popcorn
- Coffee and hot drinks (October can be chilly)

Prices inside the arena are typically higher than outside. If you'd like to have dinner before the concert, the surrounding area offers numerous restaurants:

- **Dolní oblast Vítkovice** — modern restaurants and cafés in an industrial setting
- **Ostrava city center** (10 min by tram) — wide selection of dining options
- **Karolina Park** — shopping center with a food court, accessible by tram

### Accommodation for Out-of-Town Visitors

If you're traveling from a greater distance and want to enjoy the evening stress-free, consider staying overnight in Ostrava. The concert ends around 10:30 PM, and you'll still have the journey home ahead of you.

**Accommodation tips by budget:**

- **Budget (up to 1,500 CZK):** Hostels in Ostrava center, Airbnb apartments near Vítkovice
- **Standard (1,500–3,000 CZK):** Hotels in the center — Imperial, Mercure, Ruby Blue
- **Premium (3,000+ CZK):** Clarion Congress Hotel, Park Inn by Radisson

Book in advance — during major concerts, hotels near the arena fill up quickly.

### Ideal Evening Schedule

For maximum comfort, we recommend the following schedule:

- **4:30–5:00 PM** — Arrival in Ostrava (if traveling from another city)
- **5:00–6:00 PM** — Dinner near the arena or in the center
- **6:00–6:30 PM** — Travel to the arena, parking
- **6:30 PM** — Doors open, entry, orientation in the venue
- **7:00–7:45 PM** — Finding your seats, refreshments, pre-show atmosphere
- **8:00–10:30 PM** — DJ BoBo concert
- **10:30–11:00 PM** — Leaving the arena, departure home or to hotel

### What to Do in Ostrava the Next Day

If you stay overnight in Ostrava, take advantage of Saturday to explore the city:

- **Dolní oblast Vítkovice** — industrial heritage site with the Bolt Tower and science center
- **Slezskoostravský hrad** — a venue that also hosts other great events like [Kollárovci](/akce/kollarovci-slezskoostravsky-hrad) or [Hradní Oldies Festival](/akce/hradni-oldies-festival)
- **Stodolní ulice** — the legendary entertainment district
- **ZOO Ostrava** — ideal for families with children

A complete overview of [events in Ostrava](/akce) can be found on the [AKCE OSTRAVA](/akce) portal.

### Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)

**Where can I park at Ostravar Aréna?**
Directly at the arena is the P1 parking lot with a capacity of approx. 500 spaces. Additional parking is available at P2 near Dolní oblast Vítkovice (800 spaces). We recommend arriving early or using public transport.

**Which tram goes to Ostravar Aréna?**
Trams no. 1, 2, or 11 to the Městský stadion stop. From the city center, it takes approximately 10 minutes.

**What time do the doors open?**
Ostravar Aréna doors open at 6:30 PM, the concert starts at 8:00 PM.

**Can I bring my own drinks into the arena?**
No, outside food and drinks are not allowed. Refreshment stands are available inside the arena.

**Where can I buy tickets for DJ BoBo in Ostrava?**
Tickets are on sale at [AKCE OSTRAVA](/akce/dj-bobo-arena-tour-2026-ostrava) in three categories: Stands (975 CZK), VIP Stands (2,490 CZK), and Golden Seating (3,990 CZK).

**Is there any accommodation near the arena?**
Yes, there are numerous hotels and guesthouses near the arena and in Ostrava city center. We recommend booking in advance.

title: DJ BoBo Great Adventure Tour — Europe's Most Spectacular Show
metaDescription: DJ BoBo Great Adventure Tour — behind the scenes of Europe's most spectacular show. Lasers, LED walls, pyrotechnics, and 16 dancers on one stage.
excerpt: In October 2025, DJ BoBo launched what critics and fans unanimously called his most ambitious tour yet — the Great Adventure Tour. A show that transcends the boundaries of a regular concert and enters...
body:
## DJ BoBo Great Adventure Tour — Europe's Most Spectacular Show

In October 2025, DJ BoBo launched what critics and fans unanimously called his most ambitious tour yet — the **Great Adventure Tour**. A show that transcends the boundaries of a regular concert and enters the territory of a grand theatrical spectacle. If you've ever wondered what goes on behind the scenes of such a production, this article reveals the backstage of one of the most technically demanding shows the European pop-dance scene has ever seen.

### Concept — A Great Adventure as a Story

Unlike most concert tours, the Great Adventure Tour has a clearly defined narrative. DJ BoBo doesn't conceive a concert as merely a sequence of hits, but as a journey — an adventure he experiences together with the audience. The show is divided into several acts, each with its own visual identity, musical atmosphere, and emotional charge.

The individual acts explore themes such as:
- **Adventure and discovery** — an energetic opening with classic hits
- **Romance and emotion** — ballads and slower songs
- **Futurism and technology** — the most visually demanding part with LED projections
- **Celebration and unity** — a spectacular finale with pyrotechnics

This dramaturgical approach distinguishes DJ BoBo from virtually all other dance performers. The audience doesn't watch a concert — they experience a story.

### LED Walls and Visual Technology

The central visual element of the Great Adventure Tour is a giant LED wall measuring over 20 meters wide and 8 meters tall. However, it's not a single surface — the setup includes several moving segments that reconfigure during the show to create three-dimensional compositions.

Technical specifications of the visual production:

| Element | Specification |
|---|---|
| Main LED wall | 20 x 8 m, 4K resolution |
| Side panels | 2 x moving segments 6 x 4 m |
| Floor LED | 80 m2 interactive LED floor |
| Projection surfaces | 3D mapping on scenic elements |
| Control system | Disguise media server, synchronized with music |

Content for the LED walls is created by a team of visual artists over several months before the tour. Each song has its own visual accompaniment — from abstract geometric patterns to realistic landscapes and cinematic sequences.

### A Laser Show on Par with Cirque du Soleil

Lasers have been an integral part of DJ BoBo concerts since the 1990s, but the Great Adventure Tour took their use to an entirely new level. The show uses:

- **24 laser units** distributed throughout the entire arena
- **Full-color RGB lasers** with power up to 30 W per unit
- **Beam effects** creating geometric patterns above the audience's heads
- **Laser scanners** synchronized with the music in real time
- **Atmospheric effects** — lasers combined with haze create three-dimensional light objects

Safety is strictly controlled — every laser configuration undergoes certification, and strict limits for audience eye protection are maintained during the show.

### Pyrotechnics — Fire and Confetti

The Great Adventure Tour uses pyrotechnics in a way that is unmatched in European concert touring:

- **Flame throwers** at the edges of the stage with flame heights up to 4 meters
- **Gerbs and fountains** — cold pyrotechnic effects creating waterfalls of sparks
- **CO2 cannons** — for dramatic moments and bass drops
- **Confetti cannons** — thousands of confetti pieces in the show finale
- **Spark machines** — controlled spark generators on moving arms

A specialized team responsible for pyrotechnics travels with the tour and conducts safety checks and calibration of all effects before every concert.

### Dance Ensemble — 16 Professionals on Stage

DJ BoBo has always emphasized choreography. The Great Adventure Tour works with an ensemble of **16 professional dancers** from across Europe. Each of them went through an audition that attracted hundreds of applicants.

The choreography combines:
- Modern dance and contemporary
- Street dance and hip hop elements
- Acrobatic passages
- Synchronized formations
- Duet and solo performances

The dance component of the show is prepared six months before the tour begins. Choreographer Jessica Redmann, a long-time DJ BoBo collaborator, created over 90 minutes of original choreography for the Great Adventure Tour.

### Costumes and Transformations

During a single concert, DJ BoBo and the dance ensemble go through several complete costume changes. The Great Adventure Tour includes:

- **5 costume sets** for DJ BoBo
- **4 costume sets** for the dance ensemble
- **Quick changes** (some under 60 seconds) with the help of the backstage team
- Costumes designed by a Swiss fashion designer specifically for the tour

### Tour Logistics — Numbers That Impress

Behind the spectacular show stands an impressive logistical operation:

- **14 trucks** transport the set, sound, and lighting
- **120+ members** make up the entire production team (technicians, sound engineers, lighting designers, makeup artists, hairdressers)
- **2 days** are needed to build the set in each city
- **18 hours** for the complete soundcheck and technical rehearsal
- **4,000 kg** is the weight of the LED construction alone

### From Great Adventure to Arena Tour 2026

The Great Adventure Tour was a huge success, and its production elements carry over into the follow-up **Arena Tour 2026**, which will visit Ostrava in October. Fans at Ostravar Aréna can look forward to a show that builds on the Great Adventure experience and adds new elements.

[DJ BoBo Arena Tour 2026 in Ostrava](/akce/dj-bobo-arena-tour-2026-ostrava) — October 17, 2026, Ostravar Aréna. Tickets in the categories Stands (975 CZK), VIP Stands (2,490 CZK), and Golden Seating (3,990 CZK) are on sale at [AKCE OSTRAVA](/akce).

### How DJ BoBo's Show Compares to the Competition

In a European context, very few artists invest as much in their live show as DJ BoBo. A comparison of production value:

| Artist | LED Walls | Lasers | Pyro | Dancers |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| DJ BoBo | 20m main + side | 24 units | Flame throwers + gerbs | 16 |
| Scooter | Standard backdrop | Basic | Minimal | 2–4 |
| David Guetta | DJ booth with LED | Medium | CO2 | 0 |
| Schiller | Projection | Atmospheric | No | 0 |

DJ BoBo clearly leads — his show is on the level of mega-productions like Cirque du Soleil or Madonna concerts.

### What to Take Away for Your Visit to Ostrava

If you're planning to see DJ BoBo at Ostravar Aréna, know that much more than a regular concert awaits you. Prepare yourself for:

- A complete sensory experience — sound, visuals, light, the heat of pyrotechnics
- A show that draws you in from the very first second
- Moments when you'll just stand with your mouth open, watching

On the [AKCE OSTRAVA](/akce) portal, you'll find other major events besides DJ BoBo — from [Kollárovci at Slezskoostravský hrad](/akce/kollarovci-slezskoostravsky-hrad) through the [Hradní Oldies Festival](/akce/hradni-oldies-festival) with Village People and Michal David to the family-friendly [Fesťáček](/akce/festacek) in July.

### Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)

**How much does one DJ BoBo concert production cost?**
Exact figures are not public, but expert estimates suggest costs of 200,000–400,000 EUR per concert including logistics, technology, and personnel.

**Is the DJ BoBo show the same in every city?**
The basic structure and setlist remain consistent, but DJ BoBo adapts certain moments to the local audience. Audience interaction varies from concert to concert.

**Are the laser effects safe for eyes?**
Yes, all laser effects comply with strict EU safety standards. The lasers are set so they never aim directly at spectators at dangerous intensity.

**How long does it take to build the stage?**
The complete stage setup takes approximately two days. The crew works continuously in shifts.

**Where can I see the DJ BoBo show in the Czech Republic?**
DJ BoBo will perform on October 17, 2026, at Ostravar Aréna in Ostrava. Tickets can be purchased at [AKCE OSTRAVA](/akce/dj-bobo-arena-tour-2026-ostrava).

title: Eurodance Concerts in Ostrava 2026 — DJ BoBo, Scooter, and More
metaDescription: Overview of eurodance concerts in Ostrava 2026. DJ BoBo, Scooter, and other dance legends heading to the Moravian-Silesian metropolis. Complete event and ticket guide.
excerpt: In 2026, Ostrava is becoming one of the main destinations for eurodance fans in the Czech Republic. This genre, which defined the dance scene of the 90s and still has millions of loyal listeners around...
body:
## Eurodance Concerts in Ostrava 2026 — DJ BoBo, Scooter, and More

In 2026, Ostrava is becoming one of the main destinations for eurodance fans in the Czech Republic. This genre, which defined the dance scene of the 90s and still has millions of loyal listeners around the world, is returning to the stage in a big way. And the Moravian-Silesian metropolis is part of it.

### Why Eurodance Is Experiencing a Renaissance in 2026

Nostalgia for the 90s is stronger than ever before. The generation that grew up on dance floor hits is now seeking authentic concert experiences. At the same time, younger listeners are discovering eurodance through viral videos on social media or their parents' playlists.

Eurodance never completely disappeared — it was just waiting for its moment. And that moment is now. Major arena tours, festival headliners, and special retro events prove that the energy of tracks like *Everybody*, *Hyper Hyper*, or *Chihuahua* is timeless.

### DJ BoBo Arena Tour — The Main Eurodance Event of the Year in Ostrava

The most prominent eurodance event of 2026 in Ostrava is undoubtedly the **DJ BoBo Arena Tour**, which will visit **Ostravar Aréna on October 17, 2026**. The Swiss showman, born René Peter Baumann, will bring his legendary spectacular show full of pyrotechnics, dancers, visual effects, and of course, immortal hits.

DJ BoBo ranks among the absolute elite of the European dance scene. Throughout his career, he has sold over 25 million records, and his concerts are known as some of the most elaborate shows in the entire genre. Fans can look forward to classics such as:

- **Everybody** — an anthem for an entire generation
- **Chihuahua** — one of the most famous dance hits of the new millennium
- **Love Is All Around** — an emotional ballad with a dance foundation
- **Freedom** — an energetic celebration of life
- **Somebody Dance with Me** — a song that truly everyone knows

[Tickets for DJ BoBo in Ostrava can be found here](/akce).

### Scooter — The Techno Kings Who Conquered the World

When someone mentions eurodance and techno in the same breath, most people think of **Scooter**. The Hamburg-based group led by charismatic **H.P. Baxxter** is one of the longest-running projects in the entire genre. Their energetic live shows are legendary, and fans in the Czech Republic always respond with enormous enthusiasm.

Scooter regularly performs in Czech cities, and their shows are among the most attended dance events in the country. Follow our pages for current information about their potential concerts in Ostrava and the surrounding area in 2026.

### Other Eurodance Events in Ostrava and Surroundings

Ostrava offers additional opportunities to enjoy retro dance music in 2026. At **Slezskoostravský hrad**, the **Hradní Oldies Festival** takes place (May 16, 2026), featuring, among others:

| Artist | Origin | Famous Hits |
|-----------|-------|------------|
| Village People | USA | Y.M.C.A., In the Navy |
| Michal David | CZ | Disco příběh, Nonstop |
| East 17 | UK | Stay Another Day, House of Love |
| Tublatanka | SK | Pravda víťazí, Dnes |

Although these are more oldies and pop acts, Village People and East 17 are very close to eurodance, and the entire festival will offer a great dance atmosphere. [Check out the complete program](/akce).

### Why Ostrava for Dance Concerts

Ostrava has several advantages that make it an important point on the eurodance concert map:

- **Ostravar Aréna** — a modern multifunctional hall with a capacity of over 10,000 spectators, ideal for large arena shows
- **Slezskoostravský hrad** — a unique open-air venue with an unrepeatable atmosphere
- **Transport accessibility** — easy access from Poland and Slovakia expands the audience
- **Strong fan base** — Ostrava residents are known for their energy and passion for concerts

### How to Enjoy a Eurodance Concert to the Fullest

If you're heading to your first (or next) eurodance concert, here are a few tips:

1. **Buy tickets early** — major dance shows sell out quickly
2. **Dress comfortably** — you'll be dancing all evening
3. **Arrive on time** — opening acts and warm-up DJ sets are often great
4. **Let yourself go** — eurodance concerts are all about energy and collective experience
5. **Share the experience** — take photos and videos as souvenirs

### What to Expect from Eurodance Concerts in the Coming Years

The retro dance concert trend is definitely not ending. On the contrary — promoters report growing interest and capacities are increasing. Ostrava, as the third largest city in the Czech Republic, will continue to play a key role in the concert calendar. Follow [our current listings](/akce) so you don't miss any event.

---

## Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)

### When is DJ BoBo performing in Ostrava?

DJ BoBo Arena Tour visits Ostravar Aréna on **October 17, 2026**. It is an indoor show with full production.

### Where can I buy tickets for eurodance concerts in Ostrava?

Tickets for all eurodance events in Ostrava can be found at [akceostrava.cz](/akce). We recommend purchasing in advance — popular events sell out quickly.

### Will Scooter perform in Ostrava in 2026?

A specific date for a Scooter concert in Ostrava has not yet been confirmed for 2026. Follow our pages for current information about all dance concerts in the region.

### What other dance events are taking place in Ostrava?

In addition to DJ BoBo, Ostrava hosts the Hradní Oldies Festival (May 16) at Slezskoostravský hrad with Village People, Michal David, East 17, and Tublatanka. [A complete event overview can be found here](/akce).

### Is eurodance suitable for younger audiences?

Absolutely. Eurodance is positive, energetic music that appeals to all age groups. For families with children, we also recommend [Fesťáček](/akce) — a children's festival taking place on July 11, 2026.

title: DJ BoBo vs Scooter — The Battle of Two Eurodance Legends
metaDescription: DJ BoBo vs Scooter — the ultimate comparison of two eurodance icons. Who has better hits, shows, and career? Find out what sets them apart and what they share.
excerpt: Imagine a ring. In one corner stands an elegant Swiss showman with a perfectly choreographed show. In the other corner, an energetic German with a baseball bat and the immortal cry "Hyper! Hyper!" DJ B...
body:
## DJ BoBo vs Scooter — The Battle of Two Eurodance Legends

Imagine a ring. In one corner stands an elegant Swiss showman with a perfectly choreographed show. In the other corner, an energetic German with a baseball bat and the immortal cry "Hyper! Hyper!" DJ BoBo versus Scooter — two titans of eurodance who shaped dance music in the 90s and still fill arenas across Europe to this day.

Who is the greater legend? Let's break it down.

### Basic Profile of Both Icons

| Category | DJ BoBo | Scooter |
|-----------|---------|---------|
| Real name | René Peter Baumann | H.P. Baxxter (Hans Peter Geerdes) |
| Origin | Switzerland | Germany (Hamburg) |
| Career start | 1992 | 1993 |
| Number of albums | 14+ | 20+ |
| Records sold | 25+ million | 30+ million |
| Main genre | Eurodance / pop | Happy hardcore / techno |
| Signature element | Spectacular show | Energetic chants |

### Career and Longevity

Both projects emerged at the beginning of the 90s and are still active today — that's more than three decades of continuous activity. In itself, this is an extraordinary achievement in the music industry.

**DJ BoBo** started as a breakdancer and DJ in Zurich clubs. His breakthrough single *Somebody Dance with Me* (1992) catapulted him to the top of the European charts. Since then, he has released a series of successful albums and, above all, became famous for his spectacular concert shows, which resemble musicals or Cirque du Soleil rather than traditional concerts.

**Scooter** were formed in Hamburg when H.P. Baxxter left the project Celebrate the Nun. Together with Rick Jordan, they created a sound that was rawer, harder, and more straightforward than anything on the dance scene at the time. The hit *Hyper Hyper* (1994) defined an entire era and became one of the most recognizable dance tracks of all time.

### Musical Style — Elegance vs. Energy

This is where the two projects differ fundamentally, and that's precisely what makes them such an interesting pair to compare.

**DJ BoBo** relies on:
- Melodic, singable songs with catchy choruses
- Pop-dance production with emotional impact
- Lyrics about love, freedom, and life themes
- Professional vocals and harmonic arrangements
- Accessibility for a wide audience

**Scooter** bet on:
- Hard basslines and aggressive techno-dance beats
- Iconic chants and H.P. Baxxter's shout-outs
- Minimalistic but extremely effective lyrics
- Raw energy and directness
- A rave party atmosphere

### Greatest Hits Compared

| DJ BoBo | Scooter |
|---------|---------|
| Everybody (1994) | Hyper Hyper (1994) |
| Chihuahua (2002) | How Much Is the Fish (1998) |
| Love Is All Around (1994) | Maria (I Like It Loud) (2003) |
| Freedom (2001) | Nessaja (2002) |
| It's My Life (1997) | Fire (1997) |
| Somebody Dance with Me (1992) | Posse (I Need You on the Floor) (2001) |
| Let the Dream Come True (1994) | The Logical Song (2002) |
| There Is a Party (1994) | Jumping All Over the World (2007) |

Both camps have their loyal supporters. While DJ BoBo fans appreciate the melodiousness and complexity of his music, Scooter enthusiasts love the adrenaline and uncompromising energy.

### Concert Shows — Two Different Worlds

This is perhaps the biggest difference between the two projects.

**DJ BoBo** is known as the "king of show business." His concerts are multimedia spectacles with:
- Dozens of dancers and acrobats
- Elaborate pyrotechnics and laser effects
- Costume changes and set design
- A storyline that runs through the entire concert
- Precise choreography for every moment

DJ BoBo's performances resemble a Broadway musical set in a dance context. His Arena Tour, which visits **Ostravar Aréna in Ostrava on October 17, 2026**, promises to be once again one of the most spectacular concert experiences of the year. [Tickets can be found on our website](/akce).

**Scooter** take the opposite approach. Their show is:
- Raw and straightforward — minimal set design, maximum energy
- Built on H.P. Baxxter's interaction with the audience
- Unpredictable and spontaneous
- Focused on making the audience dance from the first to the last minute
- Authentic — no playback, no pretense

### Cultural Impact — Who Influenced More

Both projects have left an indelible mark on European pop culture.

**DJ BoBo** showed that dance music can be sophisticated and can appeal to the mainstream. His success opened the door for other dance projects in traditional media. At the same time, he raised the bar for concert production to an entirely new level.

**Scooter**, on the other hand, defined an entire subculture. Their music became the soundtrack of countless rave parties, discos, and student events. H.P. Baxxter's chants entered the pop-cultural lexicon — who doesn't know "How much is the fish?" or "Hyper! Hyper!"?

### Influence on the Czech Scene

In the Czech Republic, both projects have an enormous fan base. Scooter perform here regularly, and their shows are among the most attended foreign dance events. DJ BoBo comes less often, which makes each of his appearances all the more exclusive — that's why the October concert in Ostrava is such an exceptional opportunity.

### The Verdict — Who Wins?

Honestly? Both. And that's not a diplomatic answer — it's reality. DJ BoBo and Scooter represent two different philosophies of the same genre. They are like the yin and yang of eurodance:

- Want **elegance, visual spectacle, and melodies**? Go to DJ BoBo.
- Want **adrenaline, energy, and rave atmosphere**? Choose Scooter.
- Want both? Attend both concerts. You won't regret it.

And if you must choose just one — DJ BoBo at Ostravar Aréna on October 17, 2026, is a sure bet. [Secure your tickets early](/akce).

---

## Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)

### Who is more popular — DJ BoBo or Scooter?

In terms of records sold, Scooter leads with over 30 million. DJ BoBo has over 25 million. Both belong to the absolute elite of the European dance scene. Popularity varies by region — Scooter dominate in Germany and Central Europe, while DJ BoBo is stronger in Switzerland and Southern Europe.

### When can I see DJ BoBo in Ostrava?

DJ BoBo Arena Tour takes place on **October 17, 2026, at Ostravar Aréna**. [Tickets are available at akceostrava.cz](/akce).

### Have DJ BoBo and Scooter ever collaborated?

A direct collaboration on a joint track between DJ BoBo and Scooter has not yet taken place. However, both projects meet at festivals and joint concert evenings across Europe.

### Which concert is more suitable for families with children?

DJ BoBo, thanks to his visual show and more accessible music, is generally more suitable for a wider audience including families. Scooter concerts tend to be more energetic and louder. For the youngest children, we recommend [Fesťáček](/akce) — a children's festival on July 11, 2026.

### Are DJ BoBo and Scooter still making new music?

Yes, both projects are still active and releasing new albums. At the same time, they play a mix of older classics and newer songs at concerts, so both nostalgia seekers and fans of current work will be satisfied.

title: Scooter — The Story and Career of the Techno Kings from Hamburg
metaDescription: The complete story of the band Scooter from their founding in 1993 to the present. H.P. Baxxter, Rick Jordan, and the road to 30+ million records sold.
excerpt: Very few manage to stay at the top for three decades. Scooter did it. From their first single in 1993 to their current arena tours — the Hamburg-based group led by the charismatic H.P. Baxxter is one...
body:
## Scooter — The Story and Career of the Techno Kings from Hamburg

Very few manage to stay at the top for three decades. Scooter did it. From their first single in 1993 to their current arena tours — the Hamburg-based group led by the charismatic H.P. Baxxter is one of the most successful dance projects in the history of European music. This is their story.

### The Beginnings — From Celebrate the Nun to Scooter

The Scooter story begins even before the band was officially founded. Hans Peter Geerdes — later known as **H.P. Baxxter** — was born on December 6, 1964, in Lengerich, northern Germany. From a young age, he was fascinated by electronic music and a punk approach to creation.

In the late 80s, he co-founded the project **Celebrate the Nun**, which had minor success on the German synth-pop scene. However, the project soon fell apart, and Baxxter began looking for a new direction.

He found it in 1993 when he teamed up with DJ **Rick Jordan** (real name Jens Thele) and MC **Ferris** (Sören Bühler). The trio founded the project **Scooter** and immediately headed to the studio.

### Breakthrough — Hyper Hyper and the Rise to Fame (1994–1996)

The debut album *...and the Beat Goes On!* was released in 1994 and contained the single that changed everything — **Hyper Hyper**. The track with its unmistakable synthetic riff and Baxxter's chant became a massive hit across Europe.

Key moments of the early period:
- **1994** — *Hyper Hyper* conquers charts in Germany, Austria, Switzerland, and other countries
- **1994** — The follow-up single *Move Your Ass!* confirms that Scooter are not one-hit wonders
- **1995** — The album *Our Happy Hardcore* defines their sound and delivers hits *Friends*, *Back in the U.K.*, and *Let Me Be Your Valentine*
- **1996** — *Wicked!* continues the momentum, Scooter become a fixture of European dance

Ferris left in 1998, but that didn't change the band's success. Scooter continued as the duo Baxxter–Jordan, and their popularity continued to grow.

### The Golden Era — Mega-Hits at the Turn of the Millennium (1998–2004)

The turn of the millennium brought Scooter their most commercially successful period. One hit followed another, and the band became one of the best-selling dance acts in the world.

| Year | Single | Position in DE |
|-----|-------|-------------|
| 1998 | How Much Is the Fish | #1 |
| 1999 | Faster Harder Scooter | #2 |
| 2001 | Posse (I Need You on the Floor) | #3 |
| 2002 | Nessaja | #1 |
| 2002 | The Logical Song | #1 |
| 2003 | Maria (I Like It Loud) | #1 |
| 2003 | The Night | #2 |
| 2004 | Jigga Jigga! | #1 |

This period was defined by several factors:

- **Catchy covers** — Scooter weren't afraid to transform well-known songs into their own style (*The Logical Song* by Supertramp, *Nessaja* by Peter Maffay)
- **Higher production value** — the sound shifted from raw happy hardcore to more polished dance-techno
- **H.P. Baxxter's charisma** — the frontman became an icon thanks to his eccentric style, sunglasses, and unmistakable chants
- **Relentless touring** — Scooter played hundreds of concerts per year across Europe

### Sound Evolution — From Hardcore to Mainstream Techno

One of the reasons for Scooter's longevity is their ability to adapt to changing trends without losing their identity.

**Early phase (1993–1997):** Raw happy hardcore with simple but effective beats. The sound was hard, straightforward, and made for the dance floor.

**Middle period (1998–2005):** More sophisticated production, more melodic elements, catchier choruses. Scooter moved closer to the mainstream but maintained their energy.

**Modern era (2006–present):** Integration of EDM elements, big room house, and modern techno. The sound is more powerful and production-rich, but Scooter's DNA — Baxxter's voice, energetic beats, and unmistakable atmosphere — remains.

### Rick Jordan and the Departure of a Key Member

In 2014, co-founder **Rick Jordan** left Scooter. For many fans, this was a shock — Jordan was not only a DJ and producer but also a key part of the band's identity. His departure marked the end of an era.

H.P. Baxxter, however, was undeterred. He brought in new members — first **Michael Simon** (known as "Michael" or Phil Speiser) and later other collaborators. The band continued performing and recording, proving that Scooter is primarily about the vision and energy of H.P. Baxxter.

### Scooter and the Czech Republic — A Special Relationship

The Czech Republic has a specific relationship with Scooter. The band performs here regularly and always sells out large venues. The Czech audience is one of Scooter's most loyal fan bases in Europe.

The reasons are obvious:
- The dance and techno scene in the Czech Republic has always been strong
- The 90s left a deep mark on Czech pop culture
- Czech audiences appreciate authentic energy and directness
- Geographic proximity to Germany facilitates regular concerts

Ostrava, as the third largest city in the Czech Republic and the gateway to industrial Silesia, naturally connects with Scooter's energetic music. Follow [our pages](/akce) for current information about dance concerts in the region.

### H.P. Baxxter — The Man Behind the Sunglasses

Hans Peter Geerdes, alias H.P. Baxxter, is one of the most recognizable figures on the European music scene. His style is unmistakable:

- **Visual identity** — sunglasses, eccentric clothing, blonde hair
- **Vocal delivery** — screams, chants, and a specific intonation that cannot be mistaken
- **Stage energy** — even past sixty, Baxxter jumps and runs across the stage like a twenty-year-old
- **Privacy** — off stage, he lives a relatively quiet life and is known for his love of exotic animals

Baxxter is proof that age is just a number. His enthusiasm for music and performing is still authentic and infectious after more than thirty years.

### Scooter in Numbers

- **30+ million** records sold worldwide
- **20+ studio albums**
- **80+ singles**
- **23x** German Top 10 — more than any other dance project
- **1,000+** concerts performed
- **30+ years** of continuous activity

### What the Future Holds

Scooter show no signs of slowing down. H.P. Baxxter has repeatedly stated that he will keep performing as long as his health allows. And his energy on stage suggests that will be for quite some time yet.

For fans in Ostrava and the surrounding area, this is great news. Dance concerts in the Moravian-Silesian region are booming — just check the [current event program](/akce), which includes DJ BoBo Arena Tour at Ostravar Aréna (October 17, 2026) and the Hradní Oldies Festival at Slezskoostravský hrad (May 16, 2026).

---

## Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)

### When were Scooter formed?

Scooter were founded in 1993 in Hamburg. The founding members were H.P. Baxxter, Rick Jordan, and Ferris.

### How old is H.P. Baxxter?

H.P. Baxxter (real name Hans Peter Geerdes) was born on December 6, 1964. In 2026, he is 61 years old, but his energy on stage certainly doesn't show it.

### How many albums have Scooter released?

Scooter have released over 20 studio albums. Their discography is one of the most extensive in the entire electronic dance music genre.

### Why did Rick Jordan leave Scooter?

Rick Jordan left Scooter in 2014 for personal reasons. After his departure, H.P. Baxxter continued with new collaborators, and the band has been successfully operating ever since.

### Where can I see dance concerts in Ostrava?

An overview of current dance and eurodance concerts in Ostrava can be found at [akceostrava.cz](/akce). In 2026, events include DJ BoBo Arena Tour (October 17) at Ostravar Aréna.

title: Scooter Greatest Hits — Hyper Hyper, How Much Is The Fish, and More
metaDescription: Overview of Scooter's greatest hits from Hyper Hyper to the present. A chronological guide to the songs that defined the European dance scene.
excerpt: Scooter have released over 80 singles and 20 albums throughout their career. Dozens of those songs became massive hits that everyone who has ever stepped on a dance floor knows. Let's go through the bi...
body:
## Scooter Greatest Hits — Hyper Hyper, How Much Is The Fish, and More

Scooter have released over 80 singles and 20 albums throughout their career. Dozens of those songs became massive hits that everyone who has ever stepped on a dance floor knows. Let's go through the biggest ones — chronologically, with context, and with an explanation of why they are so exceptional.

### Early Hits — The Birth of a Phenomenon (1994–1997)

#### Hyper Hyper (1994)

The song that changed everything. **Hyper Hyper** is the definition of happy hardcore — a simple yet brilliant synthetic riff, a hard beat, and Baxxter's immortal chant. The single climbed to the top of the charts across Europe and became one of the most recognizable dance tracks of the 90s.

Why it's so iconic:
- The synthetic riff is instantly recognizable from the very first second
- The chant "Hyper! Hyper!" became a cultural phenomenon
- The minimalistic lyrics are paradoxically its strength — they're universal

#### Move Your Ass! (1994)

The second single confirmed that Scooter were no one-hit wonders. *Move Your Ass!* brought an even harder beat and more aggressive sound. On the dance floors, it worked as a guaranteed way to get people moving.

#### Friends (1995)

From the album *Our Happy Hardcore* — one of the few more melodic tracks of the early period. *Friends* showed that Scooter can deliver a more emotional register without losing their energy.

#### Fire (1997)

A dark, intense track with a Jimi Hendrix sample. *Fire* is among the darkest songs in Scooter's repertoire and always provokes a massive response at concerts.

### The Golden Era of Hits (1998–2004)

#### How Much Is the Fish (1998)

If Hyper Hyper opened the door, **How Much Is the Fish** kicked it wide open. The single with the absurd title and even more absurd lyrics became number one in Germany and a hit across Europe.

| Facts about How Much Is the Fish |  |
|-----|-----|
| Year of release | 1998 |
| Album | No Time to Chill |
| Position in Germany | #1 |
| Duration | 3:47 |
| Fun fact | The title has no deeper meaning — Baxxter simply wanted an absurd phrase |

The song is proof that in dance music, the meaning of the lyrics doesn't matter — it's about energy and groove. And *How Much Is the Fish* has both in abundance.

#### Faster Harder Scooter (1999)

The title says it all. Faster, harder — and unmistakably Scooter. The track became a manifesto of the band and their approach to music. No compromises, no unnecessary ornaments, just pure energy.

#### Posse (I Need You on the Floor) (2001)

The beginning of the new millennium brought a shift toward a more polished sound. *Posse* combines traditional Scooter energy with more modern production and a catchy chorus. At concerts, it ranks among the absolute highlights of the setlist.

#### Nessaja (2002)

A cover of Peter Maffay's song that Scooter transformed into a dance anthem. **Nessaja** is one of the most emotional songs by the band — the epic chorus "Don't stop believing" combined with a hard beat creates an unforgettable contrast.

This song is proof of Scooter's ability to take an existing melody and completely reshape it into their identity. The result? Number one in Germany and one of the most played dance tracks of 2002.

#### The Logical Song (2002)

Another brilliant cover — this time of Supertramp. **The Logical Song** is possibly Scooter's most commercially successful song ever. The combination of the nostalgic melody with Scooter's production appealed even to listeners who wouldn't normally touch dance music.

- Number one in 8 countries
- Platinum single in several European countries
- One of the most played dance tracks of 2002

#### Maria (I Like It Loud) (2003)

**Maria** is exactly the song you picture when someone says "Scooter." The chant "I like it loud!" became Baxxter's second most famous catchphrase right after "Hyper! Hyper!"

The song has everything that makes Scooter Scooter:
- An instantly memorable hook
- A hard, uncompromising beat
- Baxxter's energetic delivery
- An atmosphere that gets even the most stubborn wallflower moving

#### Jigga Jigga! (2004)

Another number one in Germany. *Jigga Jigga!* closed the golden era of Scooter with a proper bang — literally. The song is one of the most aggressive in their repertoire and works as a reliable detonator at concerts.

### Modern Classics (2005–Present)

#### Jumping All Over the World (2007)

The title track from the album that launched a new phase of Scooter's career. *Jumping All Over the World* is anthemic, positive, and perfect for large arena shows. This track often opens their concerts.

#### J'adore Hardcore (2009)

Scooter returned to their roots, and the result is excellent. *J'adore Hardcore* is a declaration of love for the genre that birthed them, wrapped in a modern production coat.

#### Bück dich (2017)

Controversial, provocative, and typically Scooter. The title comes from a Rammstein song, and the track shows that Baxxter isn't afraid to provoke even after twenty years in the business.

#### FCK 2020 (2020)

A pandemic anthem that perfectly expressed the frustration of the year that cancelled all concerts. Scooter turned collective anger into an energetic dance track.

### Complete Overview of Top Hits in a Table

| Song | Year | Position DE | Album |
|---------|-----|-----------|-------|
| Hyper Hyper | 1994 | #5 | ...and the Beat Goes On! |
| Move Your Ass! | 1994 | #7 | ...and the Beat Goes On! |
| Friends | 1995 | #4 | Our Happy Hardcore |
| Fire | 1997 | #3 | Wicked! |
| How Much Is the Fish | 1998 | #1 | No Time to Chill |
| Faster Harder Scooter | 1999 | #2 | Back to the Heavyweight Jam |
| Posse | 2001 | #3 | We Bring the Noise! |
| Nessaja | 2002 | #1 | The Stadium Techno Experience |
| The Logical Song | 2002 | #1 | The Stadium Techno Experience |
| Maria (I Like It Loud) | 2003 | #1 | Mind the Gap |
| Jigga Jigga! | 2004 | #1 | Mind the Gap |
| Jumping All Over the World | 2007 | #5 | Jumping All Over the World |

### Scooter Hits Live — An Unforgettable Experience

One thing is hearing Scooter through speakers. An entirely different thing is experiencing them live. Songs like *Hyper Hyper*, *How Much Is the Fish*, or *Maria* take on a completely new dimension at concerts — thousands of people chanting the choruses, bass you feel in your chest, and H.P. Baxxter, who even after thirty years overflows with energy.

If you want to experience a similar atmosphere in Ostrava, follow the [current dance event program](/akce). In 2026, **DJ BoBo Arena Tour** (October 17) is heading to Ostravar Aréna, among other events — another eurodance legend who guarantees a top-tier concert experience.

---

## Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)

### What is Scooter's biggest hit?

Commercially, the most successful is probably **The Logical Song** (2002) — a Supertramp cover that reached number one in 8 countries. However, the most well-known song is undoubtedly **Hyper Hyper** (1994), which defined the entire genre.

### How many number one hits do Scooter have in Germany?

Scooter have at least 5 number one singles in Germany — *How Much Is the Fish*, *Nessaja*, *The Logical Song*, *Maria (I Like It Loud)*, and *Jigga Jigga!*.

### Does H.P. Baxxter write the lyrics himself?

H.P. Baxxter is Scooter's main lyricist and is the author of most iconic chants and phrases. He collaborates with other musicians on production, but the creative vision is primarily his.

### Where can I hear Scooter live in the Czech Republic?

Scooter regularly perform in the Czech Republic. For current information about dance concerts in Ostrava and the surrounding area, follow [akceostrava.cz](/akce).

### Do Scooter play new songs at concerts, or just old hits?

Scooter's setlist combines classic hits with newer songs. The core of the performance consists of immortal classics, supplemented by current material. Fans will enjoy themselves regardless of which Scooter era they prefer.

title: Scooter Concerts in the Czech Republic 2026 — Where to See Them Live
metaDescription: Where to see Scooter live in the Czech Republic in 2026? Concert overview, history of performances in the country, and tips for fans. A complete guide.
excerpt: Scooter and the Czech Republic — it's a love story that's been going on for over three decades. The Hamburg-based techno-dance group led by H.P. Baxxter is among the foreign artists who visit the coun...
body:
## Scooter Concerts in the Czech Republic 2026 — Where to See Them Live

Scooter and the Czech Republic — it's a love story that's been going on for over three decades. The Hamburg-based techno-dance group led by H.P. Baxxter is among the foreign artists who visit the country most frequently and always sell out. 2026 should be no exception. Let's look at where and when you can see Scooter live.

### Scooter and the Czech Republic — History of the Relationship

The Czech Republic is one of Scooter's key markets in Central Europe. The band started performing here regularly in the second half of the 90s and has been returning almost every year since.

**Why do Scooter have such a strong fan base in the Czech Republic?**

- **Cultural context of the 90s** — The Czech Republic experienced a massive boom in dance music in the 90s. Discos, rave parties, and techno events were everywhere, and Scooter were one of the main soundtracks of this era
- **Accessibility** — Germany is a neighboring country, so Scooter could travel to the Czech Republic easily and frequently
- **Universal music** — dance and techno need no translation; Scooter's energy works everywhere
- **Fan loyalty** — the generation that grew up on Hyper Hyper and How Much Is the Fish now goes to concerts with their children

### Where Scooter Traditionally Perform in the Czech Republic

Scooter have several favorite destinations in the Czech Republic that they regularly return to:

| City | Typical Venue | Capacity |
|-------|--------------|----------|
| Prague | O2 arena, Forum Karlín | 12,000–17,000 |
| Brno | DRFG Arena, Výstaviště | 7,500–10,000 |
| Ostrava | Ostravar Aréna | 12,500 |
| Plzeň | Home Monitoring Arena | 7,000 |
| Liberec | Home Credit Arena | 7,000 |

The biggest concerts traditionally take place at Prague's O2 arena, where Scooter regularly sell out. But Ostrava's Ostravar Aréna and Brno's DRFG Arena have also hosted legendary Scooter shows.

### Scooter Concerts in the Czech Republic 2026 — Current Information

As of March 2026, no specific dates for Scooter concerts in the Czech Republic have been officially confirmed for this year. However, this doesn't mean none will take place — Scooter often announce concerts with relatively short notice, and Czech stops are a regular part of their tours.

**Where to follow current information:**
- [akceostrava.cz](/akce) — for events in Ostrava and surroundings
- Scooter's official website and social media
- Ticketmaster, Ticketportal, and other ticket sellers

We recommend regularly checking these sources, as Scooter tickets in the Czech Republic traditionally sell out very quickly.

### Scooter at Czech Festivals

Besides standalone concerts, Scooter regularly appear as headliners at Czech festivals. Their festival performances have a specific energy — the open-air setting, summer night, and thousands of fans create an unforgettable atmosphere.

Festivals where Scooter have performed in the past include:
- **Beats for Love** (Ostrava) — one of the largest dance festivals in Central Europe
- **Let It Roll** — a drum and bass festival where Scooter surprised as special guests
- **Colours of Ostrava** — a multi-genre festival that occasionally features dance legends

### What to Expect from a Scooter Concert

If you haven't been to a Scooter show yet, here's what awaits you:

#### Setlist

A typical Scooter concert lasts 90–120 minutes and includes a mix of old classics and newer songs. The core of the setlist consists of immortal hits:

1. **Hyper Hyper** — usually as the opening or closing track
2. **How Much Is the Fish** — always provokes a massive response
3. **Maria (I Like It Loud)** — the audience sings every word
4. **The Logical Song** — one of the most emotional moments of the show
5. **Nessaja** — an epic chorus performed by thousands
6. **Fire** — a dark and intense moment in the concert
7. **Jumping All Over the World** — an anthemic track for the finale

#### Atmosphere

A Scooter concert is **an experience for all senses**:
- Massive bass you feel throughout your body
- Light show with lasers and strobes
- H.P. Baxxter tirelessly running across the stage
- An audience that dances and chants from the first to the last minute
- Energy that cannot be described — you have to experience it

#### Practical Tips

- **Buy tickets immediately after they go on sale** — Scooter sell out quickly in the Czech Republic
- **Come in comfortable clothing** — you'll be dancing and jumping all evening
- **Bring earplugs** — the sound at Scooter concerts is intense; protect your hearing
- **Hydration** — don't forget to drink enough fluids
- **Transport** — plan your way home in advance; there's heavy demand on public transport and parking after concerts

### Alternative Dance Concerts in Ostrava 2026

Even if Scooter don't appear in Ostrava this year, there's no need to be disappointed. The Moravian-Silesian metropolis offers a number of other excellent dance and retro events in 2026:

**DJ BoBo Arena Tour — October 17, 2026, Ostravar Aréna**

The Swiss eurodance legend will bring his spectacular show full of hits like *Everybody*, *Chihuahua*, or *Somebody Dance with Me*. DJ BoBo is known as the "king of show business," and his concerts are multimedia spectacles. [Tickets here](/akce).

**Hradní Oldies Festival — May 16, 2026, Slezskoostravský hrad**

A retro festival with Village People, Michal David, East 17, and Tublatanka. An ideal event for fans of dance and pop music from the 80s and 90s. [More information](/akce).

**Kollárovci — May 15, 2026, Slezskoostravský hrad**

For fans of other genres — a Slovak folk phenomenon on a unique castle stage. [Details here](/akce).

**Fesťáček — July 11, 2026**

A children's festival for the whole family. If you want to take the little ones to an event, this is the ideal choice. [Learn more](/akce).

### Scooter in Numbers — Concerts in the Czech Republic

Throughout their career, Scooter have performed dozens of concerts in the Czech Republic. Their performances in the country are characterized by:

- **Sold-out venues** — capacity is not an issue for Scooter in the Czech Republic
- **Enthusiastic audiences** — Czech audiences are among the loudest in Europe
- **Repeat visits** — fans go to Scooter again and again
- **Multi-generational audiences** — you'll meet both twenty-somethings and fifty-somethings at concerts

### Why Not to Miss Scooter in 2026

2026 is a special year for Scooter — the band is approaching the 33rd anniversary of their founding, and H.P. Baxxter is still in top form. Nobody knows how long Scooter will continue performing at full strength and with full energy. Every concert could be one of the last — or one of many more. Either way, it's worth being there.

Follow [akceostrava.cz](/akce) for the latest information on dance concerts in Ostrava and the Moravian-Silesian region. Whether it's Scooter, DJ BoBo, or anyone else — we'll make sure you don't miss a thing.

---

## Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)

### Will Scooter perform in the Czech Republic in 2026?

As of March 2026, no specific dates have been confirmed. However, Scooter travel to the Czech Republic regularly, and it's likely they will appear here during the year. Follow [akceostrava.cz](/akce) for current information.

### How much do Scooter tickets cost?

Scooter ticket prices in the Czech Republic typically range from 800 to 2,500 CZK depending on the seat category and venue. VIP tickets may cost more.

### How long does a Scooter concert last?

A typical Scooter performance lasts 90 to 120 minutes. At festivals, the set is usually shorter — around 60–75 minutes.

### Are Scooter concerts suitable for children?

Scooter concerts are very loud and energetic. For children over 12, they can be a great experience, but we recommend hearing protection. For younger children, [Fesťáček](/akce) — a children's festival on July 11, 2026 — is more suitable.

### Where can I find current dance concerts in Ostrava?

A complete overview of dance and eurodance concerts in Ostrava can be found at [akceostrava.cz](/akce). Currently, you can look forward to DJ BoBo Arena Tour (October 17, 2026) at Ostravar Aréna.

title: The Future of Live Music in Ostrava — Trends and Vision for 2026 and Beyond
metaDescription: Where is live music in Ostrava headed? Trends, new technologies, sustainability, and the vision for the concert scene in 2026 and beyond.
excerpt: Over the past decade, Ostrava has transformed from an industrial city into one of the most dynamic cultural centers in the Czech Republic. Live music here thrives not only thanks to traditional festivals — it grows through new v...
body:
## The Future of Live Music in Ostrava — Trends and Vision for 2026 and Beyond

Over the past decade, Ostrava has transformed from an industrial city into one of the most dynamic cultural centers in the Czech Republic. Live music here thrives not only thanks to traditional festivals — it grows through new venues, bold artistic directors, and audiences demanding ever more diverse experiences. The year 2026 brings a packed concert calendar, while also raising questions about where the live scene in Ostrava is heading. What trends are shaping the future of concerts? What can we expect from technology, sustainability, and new formats? And why is Ostrava the city setting the tone?

### Ostrava as a Cultural Hub — How We Got Here

Ostrava's music scene has undergone a remarkable transformation over the past twenty years. The city, long perceived primarily as industrial, now hosts events of international caliber. Several key factors are behind this:

- **Industrial heritage as a backdrop** — Dolní Vítkovice, Slezskoostravský hrad, and other post-industrial spaces have given concerts a unique atmosphere that no other Czech city can offer
- **Colours of Ostrava** — a world-class festival that gave the city international visibility
- **Ostravar Aréna** — a modern multi-purpose arena attracting big names
- **Local organizers** — companies like RESTARTSTAGE PRODUCTION bring events tailored to the Ostrava audience

### Concert Calendar 2026 — What Awaits Us

The year 2026 shows that Ostrava has no intention of slowing down. Here's an overview of key events:

| Event | Date | Venue | Genre |
|-------|------|-------|-------|
| Kollárovci | May 15 | Slezskoostravský hrad | Folk, pop |
| Hradní Oldies Festival | May 16 | Slezskoostravský hrad | Retro, disco, pop, rock |
| Fesťáček | July 11 | Ostrava | Children's, family |
| DJ BoBo | October 17 | Ostravar Aréna | Eurodance, show |

The diversity of genres and formats — from the folk-oriented Kollárovci through a retro festival with Village People and Michal David to the spectacular eurodance show by DJ BoBo — demonstrates that Ostrava's scene targets all age groups and musical preferences.

### Trend #1 — The Return of Nostalgia and Retro Concerts

One of the strongest trends in recent years is the return to music from past decades. The Hradní Oldies Festival featuring Village People, Michal David, East 17, and Tublatanka is a perfect example. Why is nostalgia so popular?

- **Generation X and boomers** have purchasing power and a desire to relive the experiences of their youth
- **Younger generations** are discovering retro music through social media — TikTok has revived dozens of hits from the 80s and 90s
- **Emotional connection** — nostalgic music triggers stronger emotional responses than new songs

This trend resonates particularly strongly in Ostrava. A city undergoing its own transformation naturally attracts events that connect the past with the present.

### Trend #2 — Technology Is Changing the Concert Experience

A concert in 2026 looks different than it did ten years ago — and in another five years, it will change even more. Technologies shaping the future of live music:

- **LED walls and immersive visuals** — giant screens and projections create a visual spectacle that complements the music. DJ BoBo is a pioneer of this approach — his show at Ostravar Aréna will be a showcase of top-tier production
- **Spatial audio** — systems like Dolby Atmos or d&b Soundscape place sound in three-dimensional space, where listeners hear music from all directions
- **Cashless payments** — festivals and venues are transitioning to cashless systems that speed up service and reduce queues
- **Digital tickets with NFC** — the end of paper tickets, entry by tapping your phone or wristband
- **Live streams and hybrid formats** — fans who can't attend in person watch the concert online in real time

### Trend #3 — Sustainability and Ecology

The music industry is becoming aware of its ecological footprint. In Ostrava and worldwide, we observe a shift toward more sustainable concerts:

| Area | Traditional Approach | Sustainable Approach |
|------|---------------------|---------------------|
| Power | Diesel generators | Grid connection, solar panels |
| Waste | Disposable cups | Deposit cups, compostable materials |
| Transport | Individual cars | Public transit support, shuttle buses, bike parking |
| Catering | Plastic cutlery | Compostable or reusable tableware |
| Merchandising | Fast fashion t-shirts | Organic cotton, local production |

Ostrava events have an advantage — most concert venues are well connected to public transit, and the city is compact, making it easy for attendees to reach the venue without a car.

### Trend #4 — Festivals Tailored to Specific Groups

The era of "one festival for everyone" is slowly coming to an end. The future belongs to events that precisely target specific audiences:

- **Family festivals** — Fesťáček on July 11 is an example of an event designed for parents with children, with a safe environment and programming for the youngest
- **Retro and nostalgic events** — Hradní Oldies Festival for the 40+ generation
- **Community concerts** — smaller events in clubs and alternative spaces for the local scene
- **Accessible events** — growing emphasis on accessibility for people with disabilities

This specialization doesn't mean big festivals will disappear — rather, an increasing number of niche events are appearing alongside them, appealing to specific communities.

### Trend #5 — Connecting Music with Other Experiences

A concert as a standalone event is no longer enough. Audiences expect a comprehensive experience that includes:

- **Gastronomy** — food trucks and local cuisine as part of the festival experience
- **Art and education** — installations, workshops, and panel discussions (the Colours of Ostrava model)
- **Social dimension** — charity concerts, support for local communities

### Vision for 2030 — Where Ostrava Is Heading

If current trends continue, Ostrava could become one of the most significant concert cities in the country by the end of the decade. Key prerequisites for further growth:

- **More medium-sized venues** — between clubs for 200 people and Ostravar Aréna for 12,000, there's a gap for venues holding 1,000–3,000 attendees
- **Connection with tourism** — concerts can be a reason to visit Ostrava
- **Support for young organizers** — a new generation of promoters brings fresh ideas
- **Music tourism destination** — people will travel to Ostrava specifically for concerts and festivals

### How to Be Part of the Future

The future of live music in Ostrava isn't created just by organizers — it's created by you, the audience. Every ticket purchased is a vote for concerts to keep happening. Start this year — find the current overview of all events at [akceostrava.cz/akce](/akce).

### Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)

**What concerts are taking place in Ostrava in 2026?**
In 2026, Ostrava will host, among others, a Kollárovci concert (May 15), Hradní Oldies Festival with Village People and Michal David (May 16), Fesťáček (July 11), and DJ BoBo (October 17). Full overview at [akceostrava.cz/akce](/akce).

**Is Ostrava a good city for concerts?**
Absolutely. Ostrava offers a unique combination of industrial spaces, a modern arena, and historic venues. A strong local community and quality organizers ensure a diverse range of events throughout the year.

**How is the concert experience changing thanks to technology?**
Modern concerts use LED walls, spatial audio, digital tickets, and cashless payments. Visual production is becoming as important as the music itself — an example is the DJ BoBo show at Ostravar Aréna.

**Are concerts in Ostrava eco-friendly?**
Ostrava events are gradually adopting sustainable practices — deposit cups, compostable materials, and public transit support. The city is compact, which naturally reduces the carbon footprint of concert transportation.

**Where can I find a current overview of concerts in Ostrava?**
A current and complete overview of concerts, festivals, and other music events in Ostrava can be found at [akceostrava.cz/akce](/akce).

title: Scooter Live Show — What to Expect from the Techno Kings' Performance
metaDescription: What to expect from a Scooter live show? Pyrotechnics, energy, and an unforgettable experience. Read about what a concert by the techno kings looks like from the front row.
excerpt: When you hear the name Scooter, most people immediately picture energetic beats, flashing lasers, and the unmistakable voice of H.P. Baxxter screaming "Hyper Hyper!" into a packed arena. A Scooter live show isn't just a concert...
body:
## Scooter Live Show — What to Expect from the Techno Kings' Performance

When you hear the name Scooter, most people immediately picture energetic beats, flashing lasers, and the unmistakable voice of H.P. Baxxter screaming "Hyper Hyper!" into a packed arena. A Scooter live show isn't just a concert — it's a complete audiovisual experience that engulfs you from the first second to the final fireworks. Let's take a look at everything you can expect from the techno kings' performance.

### Scooter on Stage — Energy That Can't Be Described in Words

Scooter have been touring non-stop for over thirty years and have played thousands of shows around the world. Yet every Scooter show is unique in its own way. The band is known for not holding back on stage — H.P. Baxxter jumps, runs, and interacts with the audience throughout the entire concert as if he were twenty years old.

A typical Scooter concert lasts approximately 90 to 120 minutes, during which time a cross-section of the group's entire career is performed. From early hits like "Hyper Hyper" and "Move Your Ass" through the golden era of "Maria (I Like It Loud)" and "The Logical Song" to newer material. The setlist is carefully crafted to alternate tempos — after an energetic opening, there's a moment to catch your breath before the arena shakes to its foundations again.

### World-Class Production

One of the main reasons a Scooter live show is so unforgettable is the production. The band invests enormous resources into:

- **Light show** — hundreds of moving lights, laser beams cutting through fog, and LED screens with dynamic visuals
- **Pyrotechnics** — pillars of flame, confetti explosions, and effects you can literally feel on your skin
- **Sound** — a top-tier sound system ensuring bass lines resonate through your entire body
- **Stage design** — spectacular stage constructions with ramps and elevated platforms

A Scooter show is designed to work in large arenas as well as at open-air festivals. Visual effects are adapted to the space — in an enclosed hall, the experience is more intense thanks to the reflection of sound and light, while under the open sky, pyrotechnics and the laser show stand out.

### What You'll Hear at a Scooter Concert — Overview of the Greatest Hits

Every fan gets their fill. A Scooter concert setlist typically includes these iconic tracks:

| Track | Release Year | Note |
|-------|-------------|------|
| Hyper Hyper | 1994 | Breakthrough hit, good old classic Scooter |
| Move Your Ass | 1995 | One of the first anthems on the rave scene |
| How Much Is the Fish? | 1998 | Iconic melody, the entire arena sings along |
| Maria (I Like It Loud) | 2003 | One of the band's best-selling singles |
| The Logical Song | 2002 | Supertramp cover, worldwide megahit |
| Nessaja | 2002 | Emotional moment of the concert |
| Fire | 1997 | Accompanied by actual flames on stage |
| Jumping All Over the World | 2007 | The entire arena jumps in unison |

### An Atmosphere You'll Fall in Love With

A Scooter performance is unique in that it creates an incredible atmosphere of togetherness. It doesn't matter if you're a die-hard fan from the nineties or a teenager who discovered Scooter on TikTok — the moment the first beats hit, everyone becomes part of one massive party.

H.P. Baxxter is a master at working the crowd. His phrases like "How much is the fish?!" or "Are you ready?!" have become part of the pop-cultural lexicon. The Scooter frontman can get tens of thousands of people moving with a single gesture and keep the energy at maximum throughout the entire concert.

### Practical Tips for Scooter Show Visitors

If you're planning to attend a Scooter concert, prepare yourself for an intense experience. A few tips:

- **Comfortable footwear** — you'll be jumping and dancing a lot, definitely leave the high heels at home
- **Hydration** — it gets hot in the crowd, don't forget to buy water before the show starts
- **Hearing protection** — if you're sensitive to loud music, earplugs are a smart investment that won't diminish the experience
- **Arrive early** — the opening DJs are usually great and will help you get into the right mood
- **Phone battery** — you'll want to capture at least a few moments, so charge your phone

### Scooter and the Czech Republic — A Long-Standing Relationship

Scooter have a strong connection to the Czech Republic. The band performs here regularly, and Czech audiences are among the most appreciative in Europe. H.P. Baxxter has repeatedly mentioned that concerts in Central Europe are something special for the band thanks to the energy and enthusiasm of the fans.

Ostrava, as a city with a rich tradition of live music and electronic scene, is an ideal destination for a Scooter show. If you're looking for more events in Ostrava, check out the [complete event overview](/akce) — besides electronic music, you can also look forward to the Hradní Oldies Festival at Slezskoostravský hrad (May 16) with Village People, Michal David, East 17, and Tublatanka, or the spectacular DJ BoBo Arena Tour at Ostravar Aréna (October 17).

### Why You Can't Miss a Scooter Show

A Scooter live show is more than a concert. It's a ritual, a celebration of music and energy that will stay in your memory for years. Whether you're a techno fan, a dance music lover, or simply want to enjoy an evening full of fun — Scooter won't disappoint. Everyone who has experienced a Scooter performance firsthand will confirm that it's one of the most intense concert experiences you can see in Europe.

## Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)

### How long does a Scooter concert last?

A typical Scooter concert lasts approximately 90 to 120 minutes, depending on the type of event. At festivals, the set tends to be shorter (60–75 minutes), while at standalone concerts, you'll enjoy the full version of the show.

### What hits will Scooter play at the concert?

The setlist always includes classics like "Hyper Hyper," "How Much Is the Fish?," "Maria (I Like It Loud)," "The Logical Song," and "Nessaja." The band combines old hits with newer material.

### Is a Scooter show suitable for children?

Scooter concerts are very loud and energetic. The environment may be too intense for younger children. If you're looking for a family-friendly event, we recommend [Fesťáček](/akce) — a children's festival taking place on July 11 in Ostrava.

### How much do Scooter tickets cost?

Ticket prices vary depending on the type of event and venue. The current ticket offerings for concerts in Ostrava can be found at [akceostrava.cz](/akce).

### Is it worth buying VIP tickets for a Scooter show?

VIP tickets usually include a better view of the stage, access to the VIP zone with a bar, and shorter queues. If you want maximum comfort while still being close to the action, VIP is definitely worth it.

title: H.P. Baxxter — Scooter Frontman, DJ, and Techno Scene Icon
metaDescription: Who is H.P. Baxxter? Biography of the Scooter frontman, his journey from Hannover to the top of the techno scene. DJ, showman, and electronic music icon.
excerpt: Hans Peter Geerdes, known worldwide as H.P. Baxxter, is one of the most prominent figures in European electronic music. As the frontman of Scooter, he has sold over 30 million records, played thousands of concerts, and bec...
body:
## H.P. Baxxter — Scooter Frontman, DJ, and Techno Scene Icon

Hans Peter Geerdes, known worldwide as H.P. Baxxter, is one of the most prominent figures in European electronic music. As the frontman of Scooter, he has sold over 30 million records, played thousands of concerts, and become synonymous with the energetic techno-dance sound. But who is the man behind the sunglasses and the distinctive voice?

### Youth and Path to Music

H.P. Baxxter was born on September 14, 1964, in Lennestadt, Germany, but grew up in Hannover. From a young age, he was fascinated by music — he listened to punk, new wave, and early electronic experiments. In the eighties, he played in several bands, but the real breakthrough came at the beginning of the nineties when electronic music began to break into the mainstream.

In 1993, together with Rick J. Jordan and Ferris Bueler, he founded the project Scooter. The original intention was to create dance music for the club scene, but the trio's ambitions quickly outgrew the boundaries of Hannover's clubs.

### The Rise of Scooter and H.P. Baxxter's Role

The breakthrough came in 1994 with the hit "Hyper Hyper," which climbed to the top of charts across Europe. H.P. Baxxter immediately became the face of the group — his energetic delivery, characteristic shouts, and unmistakable visual style made him one of the most recognizable figures in pop music.

Key moments in the career of H.P. Baxxter and Scooter:

| Year | Event |
|------|-------|
| 1993 | Founding of Scooter in Hannover |
| 1994 | Breakthrough with "Hyper Hyper" — #1 on the charts |
| 1998 | "How Much Is the Fish?" — iconic hit of the nineties |
| 2002 | "The Logical Song" — worldwide megahit, Supertramp cover |
| 2003 | "Maria (I Like It Loud)" — one of the most successful singles |
| 2008 | "Jumping All Over the World" — title track of the album of the same name |
| 2014 | 20 years on the scene, special anniversary tour |
| 2023 | 30 years of Scooter — the band still fills arenas across Europe |

### H.P. Baxxter as DJ and Producer

Although H.P. Baxxter is best known as the frontman and "announcer" of Scooter, his role in the band extends much further. He co-writes most of the lyrics and actively participates in production. His approach to creating music is pragmatic — the goal is always to create a track that works on a big stage and gets the audience moving.

As a DJ, H.P. Baxxter also performs outside of Scooter — he plays solo sets at festivals and clubs, combining classic Scooter hits with current dance tracks. His DJ sets are known for their high energy and ability to keep the crowd moving for hours.

### Visual Style and Image

H.P. Baxxter is known for his specific visual style, which has remained virtually unchanged for three decades:

- **Sunglasses** — his essential accessory, he wears them practically always on stage
- **Blond hair** — the iconic hairstyle that has become his trademark
- **Sportswear** — on stage most often in a t-shirt or jacket, always ready for action
- **Tattoos** — gradually accumulating tattoos document his life journey

His image is calculated yet authentic. H.P. Baxxter doesn't come across as a manufactured pop idol but as someone who genuinely enjoys his lifestyle.

### Personal Life of H.P. Baxxter

Outside of music, H.P. Baxxter is a passionate animal lover — he keeps snakes and other exotic animals, a fact that surprises many fans. He is also known for his love of fast cars and motorcycles.

Regarding his personal life, H.P. Baxxter guards it quite carefully. However, it is known that he has been married several times. Despite his age — he is 61 years old in 2026 — he is still just as energetic on stage as he was in the nineties. He himself says that performing keeps him young.

### H.P. Baxxter and His Philosophy

In interviews, H.P. Baxxter often emphasizes simplicity as the key to Scooter's success. "We don't make complicated music. We make music that people enjoy and that makes them dance. That's all," he said in one interview. This philosophy permeates everything Scooter does — from track production to setlist selection to show design.

Another fundamental principle is perseverance. While most dance projects from the nineties ended long ago, Scooter continue. H.P. Baxxter rejects the idea of retirement — as long as he enjoys performing and fans keep coming, there's no reason to stop.

### H.P. Baxxter and the Czech Fan Base

The Czech Republic is among the countries where Scooter and H.P. Baxxter have a particularly strong fan base. Czech audiences are known for their energy and enthusiasm, which Baxxter repeatedly appreciates. Concerts in the Czech Republic regularly rank among the most attended stops on European tours.

Ostrava, with its strong tradition of live music and electronic scene, is a natural venue for major concert events. If you're interested in upcoming events in the city, visit the [event overview at akceostrava.cz](/akce). In 2026, Ostrava will host, among others, the DJ BoBo Arena Tour at Ostravar Aréna (October 17) — another big show from the dance and electronic music world.

### H.P. Baxxter's Legacy for the Music Scene

H.P. Baxxter is more than just a dance band frontman. He is a symbol of an entire era of European electronic music. Through his approach — combining showmanship, humor, and relentless energy — he has influenced generations of artists and DJs. He proved that even in a genre many critics dismissed as superficial, one can build a thirty-year career and remain relevant.

For techno and dance music fans, H.P. Baxxter is a living legend. For everyone else, he is at the very least someone who knows how to get any crowd in the world moving.

## Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)

### How old is H.P. Baxxter?

H.P. Baxxter was born on September 14, 1964, making him 61 years old in 2026. Despite his age, he remains incredibly energetic on stage and fills arenas across Europe.

### What is H.P. Baxxter's real name?

H.P. Baxxter's real name is Hans Peter Geerdes. He chose the stage name H.P. Baxxter at the beginning of Scooter's career in 1993.

### Does H.P. Baxxter also perform solo as a DJ?

Yes, in addition to performing with Scooter, H.P. Baxxter occasionally plays solo DJ sets at festivals and clubs. His sets combine Scooter hits with current dance tracks.

### Where can I see Scooter or H.P. Baxxter live?

Scooter regularly tour across Europe, including the Czech Republic. A current overview of concerts and events in Ostrava can be found at [akceostrava.cz](/akce).

### Is H.P. Baxxter the author of Scooter's lyrics?

Yes, H.P. Baxxter co-writes most of Scooter's lyrics. He also participates in production and the overall direction of the band. He is the creative force behind the group's characteristic sound and style.

title: Techno and Dance Concerts in Ostrava 2026 — Complete Overview
