body:
# Michal David in Ostrava — Hradní Oldies Festival 2026

Slezskoostravský hrad will transform into the epicenter of nostalgia and entertainment on May 16, 2026. The Hradní Oldies Festival will bring a star-studded lineup to Ostrava, headlined by the king of Czech pop music Michal David alongside the legendary Village People, British boyband East 17, and Slovak rock icon Tublatanka. It is one of the biggest oldies events of 2026 in the Czech Republic — and you can be part of it.

## Hradní Oldies Festival — What Is It

The Hradní Oldies Festival is an all-day music festival focused on hits of the 80s and 90s, held in the unique setting of Slezskoostravský hrad. The festival is organized by RESTARTSTAGE PRODUCTION, and its ambition is to bring the best of the disco, pop music, and rock era to Ostrava — on an international scale.

### Date and Venue

- **Date:** May 16, 2026 (Saturday)
- **Venue:** Slezskoostravský hrad, Ostrava
- **Start:** Afternoon (exact schedule to be announced)

### Festival Lineup

| Artist | Genre | Greatest Hits |
|-----------|------|---------------|
| **Michal David** | Pop, disco | Nonstop, Poupata, Céčka céčka |
| **Village People** | Disco | Y.M.C.A., In the Navy, Macho Man |
| **East 17** | Pop, new jack swing | Stay Another Day, House of Love |
| **Tublatanka** | Rock | Pravda zvíťazí, Dnes, Žily |

## Michal David at the Hradní Oldies Festival

Michal David is undoubtedly the main Czech star of the festival. His presence guarantees that the Czech audience will get what they love — an energetic show full of unforgettable hits that the entire nation knows by heart.

### What to Expect from His Performance

Michal David always delivers an extraordinary performance on festival stages. His set typically includes:

- **Greatest hits** — "Nonstop", "Poupata", "Céčka, céčka", "Každý mi tě, brácho, závidí", "Discopříběh"
- **Energetic choreography** — Michal David is known for his movement on stage and interaction with the audience
- **Singing with the audience** — his concerts are a collective experience where the entire crowd sings
- **Professional sound and production** — backing band and sound engineering at the highest level

### Why This Is an Exceptional Opportunity

Seeing Michal David on one stage with Village People and East 17 is an experience that doesn't happen often. The Hradní Oldies Festival offers a unique combination of Czech and international pop music in a setting that gives the entire experience an unforgettable atmosphere. Slezskoostravský hrad with its view of the city and the industrial panorama of Ostrava creates a backdrop that no ordinary concert hall can offer.

## Village People, East 17, and Tublatanka — Star Guests

### Village People — A Disco Icon of Global Proportions

Village People need no introduction. Their "Y.M.C.A." is one of the most recognizable songs in the history of popular music. The group, which made costume performances and the disco era famous, is coming to Ostrava with their legendary show. For Czech fans, it is a rare opportunity to see this American icon live.

### East 17 — British Pop of the 90s

East 17 were one of the most successful British boybands of the 90s. Their ballad "Stay Another Day" became a worldwide hit and remains one of the most played songs of the era. East 17's performance at the Hradní Oldies Festival will delight everyone who looks back on the 90s fondly.

### Tublatanka — The Slovak Rock Legend

Tublatanka, led by Matúš Ďurinda, will bring energetic rock to the festival that shaped an entire generation of Czechoslovak listeners. The hits "Pravda zvíťazí" and "Dnes" are anthems that transcended the boundaries of genre and nation. Read more about Tublatanka in our [separate article](/blog/tublatanka-slovenska-rockova-legenda).

## Tickets — Categories and Prices

Tickets for the Hradní Oldies Festival are available in three categories to meet the needs of different types of visitors.

| Category | Price | What's Included |
|-----------|------|-------------|
| **Standard** | 999 CZK | Festival entry, standing in the main area |
| **VIP standing** | 1,490 CZK | Access to the VIP zone with a better view of the stage, separate refreshments |
| **VIP terrace** | 2,990 CZK | Exclusive access to the castle terrace with the best view, seating, premium refreshments |

### Where to Buy Tickets

Tickets are on sale at [akceostrava.cz](/akce). We recommend purchasing in advance — events with such a lineup sell out quickly. Online purchasing is simple, fast, and secure.

### Which Category Is Worth It

- **Standard for 999 CZK** is the ideal choice for those who want to be in the middle of the action, singing and dancing with the crowd. The festival atmosphere is most intense right in the main area.
- **VIP standing for 1,490 CZK** will be appreciated by those who want a better view and a bit more comfort, while still being close to the action.
- **VIP terrace for 2,990 CZK** is the premium experience — seating on the castle terrace with a panoramic view, where you can enjoy the festival in full comfort. An ideal choice for couples or groups who want to treat themselves to something extra.

## Slezskoostravský hrad — The Ideal Festival Venue

Slezskoostravský hrad is one of the most attractive concert venues in the entire Czech Republic. This Gothic fortress from the 13th century has undergone extensive reconstruction and now serves as a cultural center with a unique atmosphere.

### What the Castle Offers Visitors

- **Historic setting** — a concert in the backdrop of a medieval castle is an experience in itself
- **View of Ostrava** — the castle terraces open up a panorama of the city and the Beskydy mountains
- **Good facilities** — restrooms, refreshments, parking within walking distance
- **Public transport access** — tram and bus stops are a few minutes' walk from the castle

### How to Get to the Castle

- **Public transport:** Take the tram to the Výstaviště stop, then 10 minutes on foot
- **By car:** Parking is available near the castle; we recommend arriving early
- **On foot from the center:** The castle is approximately 15 minutes' walk from the center of Ostrava

## Practical Tips for Festival Visitors

### Before the Festival

- Buy your tickets early at [akceostrava.cz](/akce) — don't risk the event selling out
- Check the weather forecast — the festival is outdoors
- Charge your phone — you'll want to take photos and videos
- Arrange a meeting point with friends — it's easy to get lost in the crowd

### At the Festival

- Arrive early to choose a good spot
- Wear comfortable shoes — you'll be standing and dancing for several hours
- Stay hydrated — it can be warm even in May
- Enjoy the atmosphere — sing, dance, have fun

### After the Festival

- Expect thousands of people leaving at once — be patient
- Public transport is usually reinforced after large events
- If driving, expect traffic restrictions around the castle

## What's Happening the Day Before — Kollárovci at the Castle

It's worth mentioning that the day before the Hradní Oldies Festival — that is, May 15, 2026 — the Slovak band Kollárovci will perform at Slezskoostravský hrad. If you enjoy Slovak folk music in a modern arrangement, you can make your visit to Ostrava a whole weekend of music. More information can be found at [akceostrava.cz](/akce).

## Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)

### How much is the cheapest ticket to the Hradní Oldies Festival?

The cheapest ticket in the Standard category costs 999 CZK. VIP standing at 1,490 CZK and VIP terrace at 2,990 CZK are also available.

### Can I bring children to the festival?

The festival is suitable for all age groups. However, we recommend considering ear protection for smaller children, as sound at open-air festivals can be loud.

### Is refreshment available at the festival?

Yes, there will be food and drink stalls in the castle grounds. VIP categories include access to separate refreshments.

### What if it rains?

The festival takes place regardless of the weather. We recommend bringing a raincoat. Umbrellas are not advisable for safety reasons in the crowd.

### Will Michal David perform at other events in Ostrava in 2026?

The Hradní Oldies Festival is the main confirmed event with Michal David in Ostrava for 2026. Follow [akceostrava.cz](/akce) for current information about other concerts.

title: Czech Pop Hits of the 80s — Michal David, Hana Zagorová, and Other Legends

excerpt: The eighties were the golden era of Czech popular music. At a time when the world revolved around synthesizers, disco, and new wave, Czech artists were creating songs that survived decades and still play o...
body:
# Czech Pop Hits of the 80s — Michal David, Hana Zagorová, and Other Legends

The eighties were the golden era of Czech popular music. At a time when the world revolved around synthesizers, disco, and new wave, Czech artists were creating songs that survived decades and still play on radio waves, at celebrations, and on concert stages. Michal David, Hana Zagorová, Karel Gott, Helena Vondráčková, or Stanislav Hložek — these are names that defined the sound of an entire generation. Let's take a look at the phenomenon of Czech pop of the 80s, its key artists, and the hits that Czechs of all ages know.

## The Context of the Era — Music in Normalization-Era Czechoslovakia

Czech popular music of the 80s was created under specific conditions. The normalization regime restricted artistic freedom, but paradoxically created an environment in which pop music became one of the main escape valves for the entire society. Artists learned to write lyrics that worked on multiple levels — carefree and entertaining on the surface, sometimes carrying deeper meaning underneath.

### The Hit Parade as a Cultural Phenomenon

Television and radio hit parades were the main tool for spreading popular music in the 80s. Programs like Hitšaráda or music blocks on Czechoslovak Radio determined what people listened to. Unlike today's fragmented music scene, a kind of "shared soundtrack" existed in the 80s — songs that truly everyone knew.

### Synthesizers and a New Sound

The technological revolution in the form of more affordable synthesizers and drum machines fundamentally changed the sound of Czech pop music. Producers like Michal David, František Janeček, or Ondřej Soukup brought modern sound aesthetics to Czech music that could measure up to Western production.

## Key Artists of Czech 80s Pop Music

### Michal David — The Uncrowned King of Pop

Michal David is undoubtedly the most important figure of Czech 80s pop. As a songwriter, producer, and performer, he created dozens of hits that defined the sound of the entire decade. His ability to write catchy melodies with a dance rhythm had no competition in Czechoslovakia.

**Most significant hits:**

- "Nonstop" (1985) — perhaps the most famous Czech pop song ever
- "Poupata" — an emotional ballad that moves people to this day
- "Céčka, céčka" — a synonym for Czechoslovak disco
- "Každý mi tě, brácho, závidí" — a generational anthem
- "Discopříběh" — the title song of a cult film

Michal David is an active concert performer even in 2026. In Ostrava, he will perform on May 16 at the Hradní Oldies Festival at Slezskoostravský hrad — tickets are available at [akceostrava.cz](/akce).

### Hana Zagorová — Elegance and Voice

Hana Zagorová was among the most elegant figures on the Czech pop scene. Her crystal-clear voice and sophisticated approach to pop music made her one of the most respected Czech female singers. Although she left us forever in 2022, her legacy lives on through tribute concerts and constantly replayed hits.

**Unforgettable songs:**

- "Hej, mistře basů" — an energetic song with an unmistakable chorus
- "Já nemám strach" — a courageous song full of determination
- "Mimořádná linka" — a romantic ballad
- "Dál za obzor" — one of the most beautiful Czech pop ballads
- "To se nikdo nedoví" — a duet with Karel Gott

### Karel Gott — The Golden Voice of Prague

Karel Gott is the most famous Czech singer of all time. In the 80s, he was at the absolute peak of popularity, and his hits from this era are among the most played Czech songs ever. "Být stále mlád", "Když muž se ženou snídá", "Lady Carneval" (originally from the 60s but still popular), or "Čau, lásko" — these are just some of his immortal tracks.

### Helena Vondráčková — The Czech Diva

Helena Vondráčková represents the Czech answer to the great pop divas of the world. Her career dates back to the 60s, but in the 80s she experienced one of her most prolific creative periods. Hits like "Dlouhá noc", "Malovaný džbánku", or "Přelet nad kukaččím hnízdem" wrote her into the history of Czech music.

### Stanislav Hložek and Petr Kotvald — The Golden Boys

The duo of Hložek and Kotvald was a phenomenon in the 80s. Their joint hits "Holky z naší školky", "Náš vesmír", or "Šance" entertained all of Czechoslovakia. Stanislav Hložek also collaborated with Michal David on a number of other projects, and their creative synergy produced some of the most successful Czech pop songs.

## Overview of the Greatest Czech Pop Hits of the 80s

| Song | Artist | Year | Genre |
|-------|-----------|-----|------|
| Nonstop | Michal David | 1985 | Disco pop |
| Poupata | Michal David | 1986 | Pop ballad |
| Hej, mistře basů | Hana Zagorová | 1982 | Pop |
| Být stále mlád | Karel Gott | 1982 | Pop |
| Dlouhá noc | Helena Vondráčková | 1984 | Pop |
| Holky z naší školky | Kotvald & Hložek | 1983 | Pop |
| Céčka, céčka | Michal David | 1985 | Disco |
| To se nikdo nedoví | Gott & Zagorová | 1985 | Pop duet |
| Šance | Kotvald & Hložek | 1984 | Pop |
| Každý mi tě, brácho, závidí | Michal David | 1985 | Disco pop |

## The Legacy of the 80s in Today's Czech Music

The influence of Czech 80s pop music extends far beyond nostalgia. Contemporary Czech producers and artists regularly return to this period — whether through sampling, covers, or direct musical references.

### The Retro Wave on the Rise

In recent years, we have observed a significant increase in interest in retro aesthetics — and 80s music is at its core. Synth-wave, vaporwave, and other modern genres draw from the sound palette of the 80s and bring it to new generations.

### Tribute Concerts and Memorial Events

Tribute concerts dedicated to the legends of Czech 80s pop music enjoy enormous popularity. Memorial events for Hana Zagorová, Karel Gott, and other artists regularly fill halls across the country. In Ostrava, festivals at Slezskoostravský hrad are among the most significant events of this type.

### Oldies Festivals — Where to Hear 80s Hits Live

For fans of Czech 80s pop music, oldies festivals are key events where the legends of this era appear on one stage. In Ostrava, the main event in 2026 is the Hradní Oldies Festival (May 16, Slezskoostravský hrad) with Michal David headlining. A complete overview of events can be found at [akceostrava.cz](/akce).

## Why 80s Hits Are Still Relevant

The phenomenon of the enduring popularity of 80s hits has several explanations:

- **Melodic quality** — songs from the 80s have strong melodies that are easy to remember and don't tire even after hundreds of listens
- **Emotional resonance** — hits from this era are linked to strong memories and emotions of an entire generation
- **Universal themes** — love, youth, fun, and life optimism are timeless themes
- **Cultural heritage** — these songs are part of Czech identity and are passed down from generation to generation
- **Quality of performance** — singers of the 80s were professionals with exceptional vocal abilities

## Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)

### Who was the most successful Czech pop artist of the 80s?

In terms of the number of hits and records sold, it is Michal David, followed by Karel Gott and Hana Zagorová. Michal David also worked as a songwriter and producer for other artists.

### Which Czech pop hits of the 80s are most played today?

According to radio statistics, the most played include "Nonstop" and "Poupata" by Michal David, "Být stále mlád" by Karel Gott, and "Hej, mistře basů" by Hana Zagorová.

### Where can I hear Czech 80s pop hits live in 2026?

The best opportunities are oldies festivals and retro concerts. In Ostrava, the Hradní Oldies Festival with Michal David takes place on May 16, 2026 — tickets at [akceostrava.cz](/akce).

### Are there modern Czech bands inspired by the 80s sound?

Yes, a number of contemporary Czech producers and bands draw from the 80s sound. The synth-pop and retro-wave scene in the Czech Republic is alive and active.

### What influence did censorship have on Czech 80s pop music?

Censorship paradoxically contributed to the high quality of lyrics — authors learned to write multi-layered texts that on the surface met the requirements of the regime but simultaneously carried deeper meanings. This approach led to the creation of a unique literary style in Czech pop songwriting.

title: Michal David Discography — A Complete Overview of Albums and Singles

excerpt: Michal David is one of the most prolific Czech music creators. Over more than four decades on the scene, he has released dozens of albums, hundreds of singles, and written songs not only for himself but al...
body:
# Michal David Discography — A Complete Overview of Albums and Singles

Michal David is one of the most prolific Czech music creators. Over more than four decades on the scene, he has released dozens of albums, hundreds of singles, and written songs not only for himself but also for a number of other artists. His discography is a fascinating journey from the beginnings of Czechoslovak disco through the CD era and digital music to today's streaming platforms. In this article, you'll find a complete overview of his work — albums, singles, compilations, and film soundtracks.

## Career Beginnings — the 70s and Early 80s

Michal David, born Vladimír Štancl, was born on October 14, 1960 in Praha. Music accompanied him from childhood — he studied at the conservatory and began composing his own songs as a teenager. His professional career took off at the turn of the 70s and 80s when he began collaborating with producers and artists on the Czechoslovak pop scene.

### First Steps in Music

In the early stages of his career, Michal David worked as a keyboardist and arranger for various bands and artists. This experience gave him a deep understanding of music production, which he later put to use in creating his own hits. Even at this stage, his talent for creating catchy melodies and modern sound design was already evident.

## The Golden Era — the 80s

The eighties are without a doubt the most important period in Michal David's discography. In this decade, he created most of his best-known hits and established himself as the most successful Czech pop artist.

### Studio Albums of the 80s

| Album | Year | Note |
|-------|-----|----------|
| **Discopříběh** | 1987 | Soundtrack to the cult film, contains "Nonstop" and "Céčka, céčka" |
| **Bláznivá noc** | 1988 | Another film soundtrack, continuation of successful collaboration with film |
| **Mikrofon je můj** | 1989 | Album full of dance hits |

### Key Singles of the 80s

- **"Nonstop"** (1985) — the most famous song by Michal David, an absolute classic of Czech pop. The song became synonymous with an entire era and remains the most played Michal David hit at concerts.
- **"Poupata"** — an emotional ballad that showed a different side of Michal David. Proof that he can create not only dance hits but also deeply emotional songs.
- **"Céčka, céčka"** — a disco classic that got all of Czechoslovakia dancing.
- **"Každý mi tě, brácho, závidí"** — a socially-themed hit with an infectious chorus.
- **"Discopříběh"** — the title song from the film of the same name, which became a cult classic.
- **"Decibely lásky"** — another film hit that confirmed Michal David's dominance on the pop scene.

### Collaborations with Other Artists

In the 80s, Michal David didn't write only for himself. As a songwriter and producer, he created hits for a number of other artists:

- **Stanislav Hložek** — "Holky z naší školky" and others
- **Petr Kotvald** — joint projects with Hložek
- **Hana Zagorová** — several joint recordings
- **Sagvan Tofi** — film soundtracks

## The Transitional Period — the 90s

The fall of the communist regime in 1989 brought fundamental changes to the Czech music scene. The opening of the market to Western music and the arrival of new genres meant a decline in popularity for many artists from the 80s era. Michal David, however, managed to adapt.

### Albums of the 90s

| Album | Year | Note |
|-------|-----|----------|
| **Michal David** | 1991 | A new beginning in the post-revolution period |
| **Bláznivá zpráva** | 1993 | Experiments with a new sound |
| **Největší hity** | 1995 | First major compilation |
| **Další hity** | 1998 | Continuation of the compilation series |

### Sound Evolution

In the 90s, Michal David experimented with his sound. While the 80s were defined by synthesizers and drum machines, in the 90s he added more live instruments, guitar parts, and more modern arrangements to his production. Lyrically, he shifted toward more personal and mature themes.

## The New Millennium — 2000–2015

The entry into the new millennium brought Michal David a second wave of popularity. Nostalgia for the 80s began to grow, and with it interest in his work. At the same time, he released new albums that proved his creative potential was not exhausted.

### New Millennium Albums

| Album | Year | Note |
|-------|-----|----------|
| **O2** | 2002 | Modern pop with elements of electronica |
| **Tsunami** | 2008 | Commercially successful album |
| **Bláznivá noc 2** | 2012 | Return to film collaboration |
| **Vždycky jsem to já** | 2014 | One of the most successful albums of the new era |

### Compilations and Best-Of Albums

Compilations of Michal David's hits form a significant part of his discography. Among the most notable are:

- **Největší hity 1&2** — a two-part compilation covering the entire career
- **Gold** — a premium edition with remastered recordings
- **Disco hity** — focused on dance productions of the 80s
- **Balady** — a selection of the most beautiful slow songs

## The Contemporary Era — 2016 to Today

Michal David remains an active creator in the new millennium. He releases new singles, collaborates with younger artists, and his work adapts to modern trends while maintaining his characteristic sound.

### Recent Work

- New singles released on streaming platforms
- Collaboration with the younger generation of Czech musicians
- Participation in television projects and shows
- Continued concert activity at the highest level

## Michal David as a Film Songwriter

Film work forms an inseparable part of Michal David's discography. His collaboration with Czech cinema produced some of the most essential hits.

### Filmography — Music

| Film | Year | Key Songs |
|------|-----|---------------|
| **Discopříběh** | 1987 | Nonstop, Céčka céčka, Discopříběh |
| **Bláznivá noc** | 1988 | Bláznivá noc |
| **Decibely lásky** | 1989 | Decibely lásky |
| **Discopříběh 2** | 1991 | New versions of classic hits |

The 1987 film Discopříběh became a cult work of Czechoslovak cinema. The story of a young DJ, for whom Michal David wrote the soundtrack, reached millions of viewers, and the songs from the film became evergreens.

## Discography in Numbers

- **Studio albums:** over 15 titles
- **Compilations:** more than 20 releases
- **Singles:** hundreds of released singles
- **Records sold:** millions of copies in Czechoslovakia and the Czech Republic
- **Songs for other artists:** dozens of hits
- **Film soundtracks:** 4 or more feature films

## Where to Hear Michal David's Hits Live in 2026

Michal David's discography is impressive on paper, but the full dimension of his work can only be felt at a live concert. In 2026, you have several opportunities:

- **Hradní Oldies Festival** — May 16, 2026, Slezskoostravský hrad, Ostrava. Michal David will perform alongside Village People, East 17, and Tublatanka. Tickets from 999 CZK at [akceostrava.cz](/akce).
- **Summer open-air concerts** — Michal David traditionally tours summer festivals and city celebrations across the Czech Republic.
- **Autumn indoor tour** — indoor concerts offer perfect sound and a complete setlist.

## Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)

### How many albums has Michal David released?

Michal David has released over 15 studio albums and more than 20 compilations. The total number including singles, EPs, and special editions is significantly higher.

### Which song by Michal David is the most successful?

The most successful and best-known song is "Nonstop" from 1985. The song remains the most played Michal David hit to this day and one of the most recognizable Czech pop songs ever.

### Does Michal David write songs for other artists?

Yes, Michal David is a prolific songwriter and producer. In the 80s, he wrote hits for Stanislav Hložek, Petr Kotvald, and other artists. He continues this work to this day.

### Where can I listen to Michal David's discography online?

Michal David's work is available on all major streaming platforms — Spotify, Apple Music, YouTube Music, and others. Older albums are gradually being digitized and made available.

### Is Michal David planning a new album?

Michal David regularly releases new singles and works on further projects. Information about exact plans for a new studio album is shared on his official channels.

### Where can I see Michal David live nearest to Ostrava?

The main opportunity in 2026 is the Hradní Oldies Festival at Slezskoostravský hrad on May 16. Tickets and details can be found at [akceostrava.cz](/akce).

body:
# Tublatanka — The Slovak Rock Legend That Conquered Czechoslovakia

Few Slovak bands have left as deep a mark on Czech and Slovak music history as Tublatanka. The band, which was formed in Bratislava in 1982, became one of the most significant rock groups in all of Czechoslovakia within just a few years. Their hits "Pravda zvíťazí" and "Dnes" transcended the boundaries of musical genres and became anthems of an entire generation — especially in the context of the Velvet Revolution. In 2026, Tublatanka continues its concert activity and is headed to Ostrava.

## Formation and First Steps (1982–1985)

Tublatanka was formed in 1982 in Bratislava. The founder and key figure of the band is guitarist and singer Matúš Ďurinda, who shaped the sound and direction of the group from the very beginning.

### Beginnings in Bratislava Clubs

In its early years, Tublatanka moved through the Bratislava club scene and built up its fan base. The band played energetic rock with melodic elements, which distinguished it from the harder rock and metal groups of the era. Ďurinda's ability to write catchy choruses while maintaining rock authenticity proved to be key to future success.

### Sonic Identity

Tublatanka's sound combined hard rock guitar riffs with pop-rock melodies and prominent keyboard textures. This combination appealed to a wide audience — the band wasn't too heavy for mainstream listeners but at the same time had enough energy and authenticity for rock fans.

## Breakthrough and Golden Era (1985–1992)

The second half of the 80s brought Tublatanka massive success. The band became one of the most popular groups in Czechoslovakia, and their concerts filled the largest halls in both parts of the federation.

### Key Albums

| Album | Year | Key Tracks |
|-------|-----|-----------------|
| **Tublatanka** | 1985 | First album, foundation of the fan base |
| **Skúsime to cez noc** | 1986 | Commercial breakthrough |
| **V auťáku** | 1987 | Hit album with dance elements |
| **Žily** | 1988 | One of the band's strongest albums |
| **Pravda víťazí** | 1989 | The album that defined an era |

### Hits Everyone Knows

Tublatanka's repertoire contains dozens of songs, but several of them have become true evergreens:

- **"Pravda zvíťazí"** — the most significant song by Tublatanka. In the context of the Velvet Revolution in 1989, it became an unofficial anthem of the fight for freedom. The lyrics about the victory of truth over lies resonated with millions of people across Czechoslovakia.
- **"Dnes"** — an energetic rocker with an optimistic message that is a staple of the concert repertoire.
- **"Žily"** — a darker, more intense track with a prominent guitar riff.
- **"Skúsime to cez noc"** — the hit that catapulted Tublatanka into the mainstream.
- **"Neplačem"** — an emotional ballad showing the more sensitive side of the band.
- **"Poď so mnou"** — another classic with an unmistakable melody.

## Tublatanka and the Velvet Revolution

The role of Tublatanka in the events of 1989 cannot be overstated. The song "Pravda zvíťazí" became one of the symbols of the Velvet Revolution — people sang it in squares, at demonstrations, and at happenings across Czechoslovakia.

### Music as an Instrument of Change

At a time when the entire society was rebelling against the normalization regime, Tublatanka provided the soundtrack to the revolutionary events. "Pravda zvíťazí" was not originally written as a political song, but its universal message about the victory of truth predestined it to become an anthem of an entire generation.

### Concerts During the Revolution

In November and December 1989, Tublatanka played at a number of concerts connected with revolutionary events. These performances are among the most emotional moments in the history of Czechoslovak music — the energy of an audience that was just gaining its freedom created an atmosphere that cannot be described in words.

## Band Members — Who Makes Up Tublatanka

### Matúš Ďurinda — The Heart and Soul of the Band

Matúš Ďurinda is the founder, guitarist, singer, and main songwriter of Tublatanka. His charismatic vocal delivery and guitar technique define the band's sound. Ďurinda is considered one of the most talented Slovak rock musicians — his ability to combine heavy guitar riffs with melodic singing is unique in Slovak rock.

### Lineup Changes

Throughout its more than forty-year history, Tublatanka has undergone a number of personnel changes. The only constant has been Matúš Ďurinda, who has held the band together and ensured the continuity of its sound and vision. The current lineup includes experienced musicians who share Ďurinda's passion for rock and live performance.

## Discography — Album Overview

| Album | Year |
|-------|-----|
| Tublatanka | 1985 |
| Skúsime to cez noc | 1986 |
| V auťáku | 1987 |
| Žily | 1988 |
| Pravda víťazí | 1989 |
| Nebo a peklo | 1991 |
| Nahá | 1993 |
| Volám o pomoc | 1995 |
| Najväčšie hity | 1997 |
| Skúška sirén | 2002 |
| Tublatanka 30 | 2012 |

## Tublatanka in Ostrava — 2026 Concerts

For Czech fans of Tublatanka, 2026 is an exceptional year. The band is headed to Ostrava as part of the stellar lineup of the Hradní Oldies Festival.

### Hradní Oldies Festival — May 16, 2026

Tublatanka will perform on May 16, 2026 at Slezskoostravský hrad as part of the Hradní Oldies Festival. They will share the stage with Michal David, Village People, and East 17. It is a unique opportunity to see the Slovak rock legend in the setting of a historic castle with a view of Ostrava.

**Tickets:**

| Category | Price |
|-----------|------|
| Standard | 999 CZK |
| VIP standing | 1,490 CZK |
| VIP terrace | 2,990 CZK |

Tickets are available at [akceostrava.cz](/akce).

### Why See Tublatanka Live

Tublatanka on stage is an experience that cannot be replicated by a studio recording. The energy that Matúš Ďurinda and his bandmates generate on stage is literally electrifying. Their concerts are a mix of the greatest hits, rock energy, and authentic connection with the audience.

What you can expect:

- **Greatest hits** — "Pravda zvíťazí", "Dnes", "Žily", "Skúsime to cez noc", and more
- **Rock energy** — Tublatanka plays loud, hard, and with full commitment
- **Professional sound** — decades of experience are reflected in precise live sound
- **Audience interaction** — Ďurinda is a showman who knows how to engage the entire crowd

## Tublatanka vs. Other Slovak Rock Bands

The Slovak rock scene is rich and diverse. How does Tublatanka compare to other legends?

| Band | Genre | Era | Characteristics |
|--------|------|-----|-----------------|
| **Tublatanka** | Hard rock / pop-rock | 80s–present | Melodic rock with anthemic choruses |
| **Elán** | Pop-rock | 80s–2010s | More poetic lyrics, lighter sound |
| **Team** | Pop-rock | 80s–90s | More sophisticated pop-rock |
| **Horkýže Slíže** | Punk rock | 90s–present | Satirical lyrics, punk energy |
| **Desmod** | Pop-rock | 2000s–present | Modern pop-rock for a new generation |

## Tublatanka's Legacy in Czech and Slovak Culture

Tublatanka is not just a band — it is a cultural phenomenon. Their influence on the Czech and Slovak rock scene is undeniable and transcends the realm of music.

- **Inspiration for generations of musicians** — dozens of current bands cite Tublatanka as their role model
- **Symbol of freedom** — "Pravda zvíťazí" remains a symbol of the fight for democracy
- **Czech-Slovak bond** — Tublatanka is one of the bands that most strongly connects Czech and Slovak music fans
- **Living legend** — unlike many bands of the 80s, Tublatanka still actively performs

## Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)

### When was Tublatanka formed?

Tublatanka was formed in 1982 in Bratislava. The founder of the band is guitarist and singer Matúš Ďurinda, who has been its only permanent member since the beginning.

### What is Tublatanka's most famous hit?

The most famous song is "Pravda zvíťazí", which became a symbol of the Velvet Revolution in 1989. Other iconic hits include "Dnes", "Žily", and "Skúsime to cez noc".

### Where can I see Tublatanka in Ostrava in 2026?

Tublatanka will perform on May 16, 2026 at the Hradní Oldies Festival at Slezskoostravský hrad in Ostrava. Tickets are on sale at [akceostrava.cz](/akce).

### Does Tublatanka still actively perform?

Yes, Tublatanka under the leadership of Matúš Ďurinda still actively performs across the Czech Republic and Slovakia. The band plays a mix of their greatest hits and newer tracks.

### How much do tickets to Tublatanka in Ostrava cost?

At the Hradní Oldies Festival, tickets range from 999 CZK (Standard) to 1,490 CZK (VIP standing) to 2,990 CZK (VIP terrace). Details at [akceostrava.cz](/akce).

title: Tublatanka's Greatest Hits — Pravda víťazí, Dnes, Žerucha, and More
metaDescription: Tublatanka's greatest hits: Pravda víťazí, Dnes, Žerucha, and other legendary songs of the Slovak rock band. A complete overview of hits and their stories.
excerpt: Tublatanka is one of the most important bands on the Slovak rock scene. Over more than forty years of existence, it has created dozens of songs that have become part of the cultural heritage not only of Sl...
body:
# Tublatanka's Greatest Hits — Pravda víťazí, Dnes, Žerucha, and More

Tublatanka is one of the most important bands on the Slovak rock scene. Over more than forty years of existence, it has created dozens of songs that have become part of the cultural heritage not only of Slovakia but of all of Central Europe. Their hits still play at concerts, on the radio, and at festivals — and Czech audiences have embraced them just as warmly as Slovak ones. In this article, we'll look at Tublatanka's greatest hits, their stories, and the reasons why this music endures across generations.

## Tublatanka — A Brief Look at History

The band Tublatanka was formed in 1982 in Bratislava. Behind its founding stands guitarist and singer Matúš Ďurinda, who became the iconic face of the group for decades. Tublatanka quickly gained popularity thanks to its rougher sound, which stood out from the Czechoslovak pop-rock production of the time. The band was not afraid to experiment with hard rock elements and lyrics carrying a strong social charge.

In the 80s, Tublatanka became one of the most popular bands in Czechoslovakia. Their concerts sold out the largest halls, and albums sold in tens of thousands of copies. After the dissolution of Czechoslovakia in 1993, the band maintained a strong position on both sides of the border and continues to perform to this day.

## Tublatanka's Greatest Hits

### Pravda víťazí

The song "Pravda víťazí" is without a doubt Tublatanka's most famous hit and one of the most important songs in Slovak rock. It was created during the period of the Velvet Revolution and became an unofficial anthem of the fight for freedom and democracy. The lyrics carry a powerful message about truth, justice, and human dignity.

"Pravda víťazí" transcended the boundaries of an ordinary rock song and became a symbol of an entire generation. At Tublatanka concerts, it is always the highlight of the evening — the moment when the entire audience sings together and the atmosphere reaches its peak. The song has not lost its relevance even after decades, because its message is timeless.

### Dnes

"Dnes" is among the most lyrical and melodic tracks in Tublatanka's repertoire. The song captures the moment of the present, the importance of living in the current moment. The melodic line combined with Matúš Ďurinda's emotional singing creates a track that touches listeners on a deeply personal level.

At concerts, "Dnes" is often placed in the calmer part of the program, where it serves as an emotional counterweight to the more energetic rock numbers. For many fans, it is this song that best captures the artistic depth of Tublatanka.

### Žerucha

"Žerucha" represents the more energetic side of Tublatanka's work. The song with its prominent guitar riff and catchy chorus has become one of the band's concert evergreens. Its rhythmic drive and straightforward energy make it an ideal number for live performances, where the audience dances and sings from the first notes.

### Skúsime to cez noc

Another classic Tublatanka hit that combines rock energy with melodic accessibility. "Skúsime to cez noc" is a song that appeals to audiences with its immediacy and vitality. At concerts, it works as a reliable atmosphere igniter.

### Poď so mnou

"Poď so mnou" is one of the most-played Tublatanka songs on the radio. Its more pop-oriented sound and catchy chorus made it a hit known even to the wider public beyond the circle of dedicated fans. The song demonstrates Tublatanka's ability to move on the border between rock and pop without losing its authenticity.

### Other Notable Tracks

Besides the hits mentioned above, Tublatanka has a number of other significant songs in its repertoire:

- **Keď ťa stretnem** — A ballad with an emotional text about meeting and human relationships.
- **Tak stráž, anjelik, stráž** — A song with a spiritual dimension that is among the band's most beloved ballads.
- **Katarína** — An energetic rocker dedicated to a woman's name, a concert staple.
- **Pár dní** — A melodic track with a nostalgic touch.
- **V nás** — An anthem about belonging and the strength of the collective.

## Overview of Tublatanka's Most Important Hits

| Song | Album | Release Year | Character |
|-------|-------|------------|-----------|
| Pravda víťazí | Skúsme to cez noc | 1989 | Anthemic, socially engaged |
| Dnes | Tublatanka | 1985 | Lyrical, melodic |
| Žerucha | Žerucha | 1987 | Energetic, rock |
| Skúsime to cez noc | Skúsme to cez noc | 1989 | Direct, vital |
| Poď so mnou | Tublatanka II | 1986 | Pop-rock, catchy |
| Katarína | Žerucha | 1987 | Rock, concert staple |
| Tak stráž, anjelik, stráž | Skúsme to cez noc | 1989 | Balladic, spiritual |

## Why Tublatanka's Hits Endure

### Timeless Lyrics

Tublatanka's lyrics touch on universal human themes — freedom, love, truth, and the desire for an authentic life. Unlike many period hits that were tied to specific trends or events, Tublatanka's songs speak anew to each generation. "Pravda víťazí" has the same power today as it did in 1989.

### Quality Musical Craftsmanship

Matúš Ďurinda is not only a charismatic frontman but above all an exceptional guitarist and songwriter. The musical arrangements of Tublatanka's songs are sophisticated, with distinctive guitar riffs and melodies that are imprinted in memory on first listen. This craftsmanship quality distinguishes Tublatanka from the one-off hits of its era.

### Concert Energy

Tublatanka is primarily a live band. Their songs are designed to work on stage — with dynamic changes, space for audience interaction, and a buildup that leads to peak moments. Anyone who has experienced "Pravda víťazí" at a live concert will never forget that experience.

## Where to Hear Tublatanka's Hits Live in 2026

Tublatanka continues its active concert activity, and in 2026, it plans a number of performances in the Czech Republic. One of the most significant opportunities is the **Hradní Oldies Festival** at Slezskoostravský hrad in Ostrava, which takes place on **May 16, 2026**. At this festival, Tublatanka will perform alongside other legends — Village People, Michal David, and East 17.

A complete overview of events and concerts in Ostrava can be found on the portal [akceostrava.cz](/akce), where you can follow the current program and purchase tickets.

## Tublatanka vs. Other Slovak Rock Bands

| Band | Period of Greatest Fame | Typical Genre | Most Famous Hit |
|--------|----------------------|--------------|-----------------|
| Tublatanka | 1985–1992 | Hard rock / rock | Pravda víťazí |
| Elán | 1981–2000 | Pop-rock | Voda, čo ma drží nad vodou |
| Team | 1983–1995 | Pop-rock | Dávam ti slobodu |
| Banket | 1983–1990 | New wave / rock | Apokalypsa |

## FAQ — Frequently Asked Questions

### What is Tublatanka's biggest hit?

Tublatanka's biggest hit is unquestionably "Pravda víťazí" from 1989. The song became a symbol of the Velvet Revolution and remains the most played and best-known track by the band. At every concert, it is the peak moment of the program.

### Who is Tublatanka's frontman?

The frontman and founder of Tublatanka is guitarist and singer Matúš Ďurinda. He has been the key creative figure of the band since its founding in 1982 and is behind most of the group's greatest hits.

### How many albums has Tublatanka released?

Tublatanka has released over ten studio albums. Among the most important are the debut album "Tublatanka" (1985), "Žerucha" (1987), and "Skúsme to cez noc" (1989), which contain most of their best-known hits.

### Where can I see Tublatanka live in 2026?

Tublatanka will perform on May 16, 2026 at the Hradní Oldies Festival at Slezskoostravský hrad in Ostrava. Other concert dates can be found at [akceostrava.cz](/akce) and on the band's official website.

### Does Tublatanka sing in Czech or Slovak?

Tublatanka sings in Slovak, but thanks to the linguistic closeness of Czech and Slovak, the lyrics are comprehensible to Czech audiences without any difficulty. Many Czech fans sing Tublatanka's lyrics from memory.

## Conclusion

Tublatanka's hits have transcended the boundaries of genres, generations, and nations. From the anthemic "Pravda víťazí" through the lyrical "Dnes" to the energetic "Žerucha" — this band's work offers a broad spectrum of emotions and musical experiences. If you want to experience these songs live, don't hesitate to attend one of Tublatanka's concerts in 2026. Follow the current program at [akceostrava.cz](/akce) and don't miss the opportunity to be part of a legend.

body:
# Tublatanka Concerts 2026 — Where to See the Slovak Rock Legends

Tublatanka remains one of the most active Slovak rock bands on the live scene. 2026 brings fans several opportunities to see Matúš Ďurinda's legendary group live — from festival appearances to indoor concerts and special events. In this guide, you'll find a complete overview of Tublatanka's planned concerts in the Czech Republic, practical ticket information, and tips on how to make the most of the concert experience.

## Tublatanka in 2026 — What Fans Can Expect

Tublatanka enters 2026 in full force. Following a successful tour the previous year, the group continues its intensive concert activity and plans a number of performances across the Czech and Slovak Republics. Matúš Ďurinda and his bandmates prove that their energy and enthusiasm for live playing doesn't diminish with the years — quite the opposite.

2026 is also specific for Tublatanka in that the band is incorporating new material into its repertoire alongside proven hits like "Pravda zvíťazí", "Dnes", and "Žerucha". Fans can look forward to concerts that combine nostalgia with fresh impulses.

## Key Tublatanka Concerts in 2026

### Hradní Oldies Festival — Ostrava, May 16, 2026

Unquestionably one of Tublatanka's most significant appearances in 2026 is their participation in the **Hradní Oldies Festival** at Slezskoostravský hrad in Ostrava. The festival takes place on **May 16, 2026** and will offer a unique combination of domestic and international oldies scene legends.

Tublatanka will perform alongside names such as:

- **Village People** — the legendary American disco group
- **Michal David** — the king of Czech pop music
- **East 17** — the British boyband of the 90s

Slezskoostravský hrad provides an unforgettable setting for open-air concerts, and the festival atmosphere promises to be exceptional. For Tublatanka fans, it is the ideal opportunity to see the band in the context of an international program.

### Other Planned Appearances

Tublatanka traditionally includes concerts in Czech and Moravian cities in its calendar. In 2026, fans can look forward to stops as part of a tour covering Bohemia, Moravia, and Silesia. Specific dates and venues are being gradually announced on the band's official channels.

## Tickets for Tublatanka Concerts

### Hradní Oldies Festival — Price Categories

| Category | Price | What's Included |
|-----------|------|-------------|
| Standard admission | According to current offer | Entry to the entire festival |
| VIP | According to current offer | Priority entry, better position |

Tickets for the Hradní Oldies Festival and other events in Ostrava can be found on the portal [akceostrava.cz](/akce). We recommend purchasing in advance — events with Tublatanka in the lineup sell out quickly.

### Ticket Buying Tips

- **Buy early** — Tublatanka has a loyal fan base and tickets for their concerts disappear quickly, especially for festival appearances.
- **Watch for advance sales** — Registering for ticket seller newsletters will give you access to early-bird prices.
- **Compare categories** — For festival events, consider VIP tickets that offer better views and comfort.
- **Buy from verified sources** — Use official sales channels to avoid the risk of counterfeit tickets.

## What to Expect from a Tublatanka Concert

### Repertoire

A typical Tublatanka concert lasts approximately 90 to 120 minutes and includes a cross-section of the band's entire career. The setlist usually contains:

- **Opening block** — Energetic rock hits to warm up the audience
- **Middle section** — Ballads and more lyrical tracks including "Dnes"
- **Main block** — Greatest hits like "Žerucha", "Skúsime to cez noc", and "Poď so mnou"
- **Finale** — "Pravda zvíťazí" as the undisputed highlight of the concert

### Sound and Production

Tublatanka is known for quality live sound. Matúš Ďurinda places emphasis on the concert sound matching studio recordings while also having the energy and dynamics of a live performance. The band uses professional sound equipment, and at larger events also a light show.

### Atmosphere

Tublatanka concerts attract audiences of all ages. The core consists of fans who have been following the band since the 80s and 90s, but younger listeners who are discovering Tublatanka's music anew continue to appear. The atmosphere is always friendly and energetic — the audience sings, dances, and actively participates in the program.

## Practical Tips for Attending a Concert in Ostrava

### Getting to Slezskoostravský hrad

Slezskoostravský hrad is located in the center of Ostrava, in close proximity to the Ostravice river. It is easily accessible by public transport and on foot from the city center.

- **Public transport** — Take the tram to the Výstaviště stop, then 5 minutes on foot
- **By car** — Parking near the castle is limited; we recommend the public parking lot near Ostravar Aréna
- **On foot** — From the center of Ostrava (Masarykovo náměstí) it is approximately 15 minutes' walk

### Accommodation

For visitors traveling to the concert from a greater distance, Ostrava offers a wide range of accommodation options. In the city center and near Slezskoostravský hrad, you'll find hotels, guesthouses, and apartments in various price ranges.

### Weather and Clothing

The Hradní Oldies Festival takes place outdoors. May weather in Ostrava can be unpredictable — we recommend having a light jacket or raincoat ready. Comfortable footwear is a given for an all-evening standing event.

## Tublatanka at Festivals vs. Solo Concerts

| Aspect | Festival Appearance | Solo Concert |
|--------|----------------------|----------------|
| Performance length | 60–90 minutes | 90–120 minutes |
| Repertoire | Greatest hits | Cross-section of entire body of work |
| Atmosphere | Festival-like, diverse | More intimate, focused |
| Ticket price | Part of festival admission | Separate ticket |
| Other performers | Yes, rich program | No, focus on Tublatanka |
| Ideal for | Fans of multiple genres | Dedicated fans of the band |

## Other Events in Ostrava in May 2026

If you're planning a trip to Ostrava for the Hradní Oldies Festival, you can combine your stay with other events. The day before, **May 15, 2026**, **Kollárovci** will perform at Slezskoostravský hrad — another Slovak band with an enormous fan base. Tickets for the Kollárovci concert are available in categories Standard (890 CZK), VIP standing (990 CZK), and VIP terrace (2,390 CZK).

A complete overview of all events can be found at [akceostrava.cz](/akce).

## FAQ — Frequently Asked Questions

### Where will Tublatanka perform in 2026?

Tublatanka will perform, among other places, at the Hradní Oldies Festival at Slezskoostravský hrad in Ostrava on May 16, 2026. Other concert dates are being announced on an ongoing basis. Follow the current overview of concerts in Ostrava at [akceostrava.cz](/akce).

### How much do tickets to a Tublatanka concert cost?

Ticket prices vary depending on the type of event and category. For festival appearances, admission is part of the overall festival price. For standalone concerts, prices typically range from 500 to 1,500 CZK depending on the venue and category.

### How long does a Tublatanka concert last?

A solo Tublatanka concert usually lasts 90 to 120 minutes. At festivals, the performance is shorter — approximately 60 to 90 minutes — but all the more intense, as the band includes their greatest hits.

### What songs does Tublatanka play at concerts?

All the greatest hits are played at concerts — "Pravda zvíťazí", "Dnes", "Žerucha", "Skúsime to cez noc", "Poď so mnou", and more. The highlight of every concert is always "Pravda zvíťazí", which the audience sings together with the band.

### Can I bring children to the Hradní Oldies Festival?

Yes, the Hradní Oldies Festival is suitable for family visits as well. Oldies music appeals to a wide age range, and the atmosphere of the festival at Slezskoostravský hrad is friendly. However, we recommend considering hearing protection for smaller children.

### Is Slezskoostravský hrad wheelchair accessible?

Slezskoostravský hrad has partial wheelchair accessibility. For specific information about accessibility, we recommend contacting the event organizer in advance.

## Conclusion

2026 offers Tublatanka fans a number of opportunities to see the Slovak rock legend live. The Hradní Oldies Festival in Ostrava is one of the most enticing events of the season — the combination of a great lineup, a unique location at Slezskoostravský hrad, and May weather promises an unforgettable experience. Follow the current program at [akceostrava.cz](/akce) and secure your tickets early.

title: Slovak Rock — Tublatanka, Elán, and Other Bands You Must Know
metaDescription: Slovak rock from Tublatanka through Elán to modern bands. A complete guide to the Slovak rock scene, its history, and the most important bands.
excerpt: The Slovak rock scene is among the richest in all of Central Europe. From the emergence of the first rock bands in the 60s through the golden era of the 80s and 90s to the present — Slovakia has given th...
body:
# Slovak Rock — Tublatanka, Elán, and Other Bands You Must Know

The Slovak rock scene is among the richest in all of Central Europe. From the emergence of the first rock bands in the 60s through the golden era of the 80s and 90s to the present — Slovakia has given the world a number of exceptional groups that have won millions of fans across the former Czechoslovakia and beyond its borders. In this guide, we'll walk you through the history of Slovak rock, introduce the most important bands, and explain why this music still resonates with Czech audiences.

## The Roots of Slovak Rock

### 60s and 70s — First Steps

Slovak rock began to take shape in the 60s under the influence of the British and American rock wave. Bands like Prúdy, Collegium Musicum, and Fermáta brought to Slovakia a sound that combined foreign inspiration with domestic musical tradition. Marián Varga and his Collegium Musicum became pioneers of art rock in Central Europe, and their albums are still considered masterpieces.

In the 70s, the Slovak rock scene developed despite the political restrictions of normalization. Bands had to balance between artistic freedom and the ideological demands of the regime at the time. Nevertheless, a number of quality albums were produced, and the concert scene remained vibrant.

### 80s — The Golden Era

The eighties represent the absolute peak of Slovak rock. In this period, bands were formed that defined the sound of an entire generation and whose hits still resonate today. The two biggest names are without a doubt Tublatanka and Elán — bands that competed for audience favor and mutually pushed each other to better performances.

## Tublatanka — The Rough Side of Slovak Rock

Tublatanka was formed in 1982 in Bratislava and quickly became one of the most popular bands in Czechoslovakia. Guitarist and singer Matúš Ďurinda created a sound that was rougher and more rock-oriented than most of the production at the time. The band was not afraid of hard rock and lyrics with a clear social message.

### Sound and Style

Tublatanka was built on strong guitar riffs, energetic rhythm, and Ďurinda's characteristic voice. Unlike the more pop-rock Elán, Tublatanka moved closer to classic hard rock with influences from bands like Deep Purple or Whitesnake. This approach earned them the loyalty of rock purists while also creating melodies catchy enough for the mainstream.

### Key Albums and Hits

- **Tublatanka** (1985) — Debut album that introduced the band to a wide audience
- **Žerucha** (1987) — Album with one of the most famous rock riffs in Slovak music
- **Skúsme to cez noc** (1989) — Album containing the anthem "Pravda víťazí"

### Legacy for the Present

Tublatanka is still active and performs across Central Europe. In 2026, they will appear at the Hradní Oldies Festival at Slezskoostravský hrad in Ostrava (May 16), where they will offer a cross-section of their work alongside other legends.

## Elán — The Melodic Side of Slovak Rock

Elán is probably the most commercially successful Slovak band of all time. Under the leadership of charismatic frontman Jožo Ráž, Elán created dozens of hits that became part of the cultural consciousness of all of Czechoslovakia.

### Sound and Style

While Tublatanka relied on rock energy, Elán was built on melody, catchy choruses, and lyrical playfulness. Elán's songs were more accessible to a wider audience, which ensured the band massive popularity. Elán could be witty, romantic, and melancholic — often within a single album.

### Most Famous Hits

- **Voda, čo ma drží nad vodou** — One of the most played Slovak songs of all time
- **Odkedy ťa mám** — A romantic ballad that became a wedding classic
- **Nie sme zlí** — An energetic rocker with an unforgettable chorus
- **Ďakujem, že smiem** — An emotional ballad full of gratitude
- **Sestričky z Kramárov** — A playful song with typically Elán-esque humor

### Comparison of Elán and Tublatanka

| Aspect | Elán | Tublatanka |
|--------|------|------------|
| Founded | 1968 | 1982 |
| Frontman | Jožo Ráž | Matúš Ďurinda |
| Genre | Pop-rock, melodic rock | Hard rock, rock |
| Lyrics | Playful, romantic, witty | Socially engaged, direct |
| Commercial success | Massive | High |
| Current status | Inactive (tribute projects) | Active, performing |
| Most famous hit | Voda, čo ma drží nad vodou | Pravda víťazí |

## Other Slovak Rock Bands You Must Know

### Team

Team, led by Pavol Habera, is one of the longest-running Slovak rock bands. Their pop-rock sound with prominent melodies appealed especially to female audiences. Hits like "Dávam ti slobodu" and "Si pre mňa najkrajšia" are classics of Slovak music.

### Banket

The band Banket represented a more alternative branch of Slovak rock in the 80s. Their new wave-inspired sound and intelligent lyrics made them a cult favorite. The album "Apokalypsa" is considered one of the best Slovak rock albums ever.

### Horkýže Slíže

Horkýže Slíže brought punk-rock energy, humor, and unbridled spirit to the Slovak scene. Their satirical lyrics and wild concerts made them one of the most entertaining Slovak bands. For fans of the alternative scene, Horkýže Slíže are an absolute legend.

### Desmod

Desmod represents modern Slovak rock. The band of frontman Mário "Kuly" Kollár managed to appeal to a younger generation of listeners with melodic pop-rock and quality lyrics. Hits like "Hemeroidy" and "Molekuly" are proof that Slovak rock has something to offer in the 21st century as well.

### No Name

The Bratislava band No Name became one of the most successful Slovak pop-rock groups at the turn of the millennium. Their positive melodies and friendly concert atmosphere secured them a stable fan base in both the Czech Republic and Slovakia.

## Overview of Slovak Rock Bands by Period

| Period | Bands | Main Genre |
|--------|--------|-------------|
| 60s–70s | Prúdy, Collegium Musicum, Fermáta | Art rock, psychedelic |
| 80s | Tublatanka, Elán, Team, Banket | Hard rock, pop-rock, new wave |
| 90s | Horkýže Slíže, Para, Heľenine Oči | Punk-rock, alternative rock |
| 2000+ | Desmod, No Name, Korben Dallas | Pop-rock, indie rock |

## Why Czech Fans Love Slovak Rock

The relationship of Czech audiences to Slovak music is unique. Linguistic affinity allows full understanding of the lyrics without translation, the shared history of Czechoslovakia created a common cultural space, and geographic proximity facilitates mutual concert touring.

### Linguistic Closeness

Czech and Slovak are sufficiently close languages that Czech listeners understand Slovak lyrics without any difficulty. This is a huge advantage for Slovak bands — they can play in their mother tongue and still reach a binational audience.

### Shared History

Four decades of a shared state created cultural bonds that endure to this day. Generations of Czechs and Slovaks grew up on the same songs, watched the same music programs, and attended the same festivals. This shared experience is the foundation of the lasting popularity of Slovak rock in the Czech Republic.

### Ostrava as a Bridge

Ostrava plays a specific role in the relationship between Czech and Slovak rock. As a city close to the Slovak border, it is a natural place where both scenes meet. Slovak bands traditionally have a strong fan base here, and concerts by Slovak rock groups in Ostrava tend to sell out.

## Where to See Slovak Rock Live in Ostrava

2026 offers fans of Slovak rock in Ostrava a number of opportunities:

- **Hradní Oldies Festival** (May 16, 2026) — Tublatanka at Slezskoostravský hrad
- **Kollárovci** (May 15, 2026) — Slezskoostravský hrad, tickets from 890 CZK
- **Club concerts** — Slovak bands regularly perform at Barrák Music Club and other Ostrava clubs

Follow the complete overview of events at [akceostrava.cz](/akce).

## FAQ — Frequently Asked Questions

### What is the most famous Slovak rock band?

It depends on the criterion. In terms of commercial success, it is probably Elán; in terms of rock authenticity, Tublatanka. Both bands have an enormous fan base in both the Czech Republic and Slovakia, and their hits are known by practically everyone.

### Does Elán still perform?

Elán no longer actively performs in their full lineup. However, the band's legacy lives on through tribute projects and special concerts. By contrast, Tublatanka is still fully active and performs regularly.

### Where can I see Slovak rock bands in Ostrava?

Slovak rock bands perform in Ostrava at various venues — from Ostravar Aréna through Slezskoostravský hrad to clubs like Barrák Music Club. A current overview of concerts can be found at [akceostrava.cz](/akce).

### Do Czechs understand Slovak lyrics?

Yes, Czech and Slovak are mutually intelligible languages. Czech audiences understand Slovak lyrics without problems, and many fans know the lyrics to Slovak hits by heart.

### What is the difference between Tublatanka and Elán?

Tublatanka is a rougher, more rock-oriented band with hard rock elements and socially engaged lyrics. Elán is a more melodic, pop-rock group with more playful and romantic lyrics. Both bands are legends of Slovak rock but appeal to partially different audiences.

## Conclusion

Slovak rock is a rich and vibrant tradition that deserves the attention of every lover of quality music. From the pioneers of the 60s through the golden era of Tublatanka and Elán to modern bands like Desmod — the Slovak rock scene offers music for every taste. If you want to experience Slovak rock live, Ostrava is the ideal place — follow the program at [akceostrava.cz](/akce) and discover the magic of Slovak rock music with your own ears.

body:
# Tublatanka in Ostrava — Hradní Oldies Festival 2026

Slezskoostravský hrad will transform into the epicenter of nostalgic music on May 16, 2026. The Hradní Oldies Festival will bring to Ostrava a unique combination of domestic and international legends — from American Village People through Czech pop king Michal David and British East 17 to the Slovak rock icon Tublatanka. In this article, you'll find complete information about the festival, its program, tickets, and practical tips for visitors.

## What Is the Hradní Oldies Festival

The Hradní Oldies Festival is a music event focused on the golden era of popular music of the 70s, 80s, and 90s. The festival takes place in the unique setting of Slezskoostravský hrad, which provides an unforgettable atmosphere for open-air concerts. The event is organized by RESTARTSTAGE PRODUCTION, which is also behind the akceostrava.cz portal.

The 2026 edition brings an exceptionally strong lineup that combines world stars with domestic and Slovak legends. For fans of oldies music, it is one of the most significant events of the year in the entire Moravskoslezský region.

## Hradní Oldies Festival 2026 Lineup

### Village People

The American disco group Village People is one of the most iconic bands in music history. Their hits "Y.M.C.A.", "In the Navy", and "Macho Man" are global evergreens known by everyone regardless of age or musical preference. A Village People performance at Slezskoostravský hrad will undoubtedly be one of the highlights of the entire festival.

Village People are known for their visual show — costumes representing various American professions (cowboy, Native American, police officer, construction worker, soldier, and biker) are as iconic as their music. A Village People concert is always an interactive experience where the entire audience dances and sings.

### Michal David

Michal David needs no introduction in the Czech context. The king of Czech pop music and uncrowned ruler of the 80s and 90s charts will bring his energetic show full of immortal hits to Slezskoostravský hrad. "Nonstop", "Poupata", "Céčka, céčka", "Každý mi tě, brácho, závidí" — that's just a fraction of the songs the entire venue sings from the first note.

Michal David is a guarantee of quality entertainment. His concerts are characterized by high energy, professional production, and the ability to draw the audience into the action. At the Hradní Oldies Festival, he will be one of the main stars of the program.

### East 17

The British boyband East 17 was one of the biggest music phenomena in Europe in the 90s. Hits like "Stay Another Day", "House of Love", and "It's Alright" became the soundtrack of a generation of teenagers. East 17 at Slezskoostravský hrad will bring a dose of 90s nostalgia and prove that their music still works after three decades.

### Tublatanka

The Slovak rock legend Tublatanka will round out the festival lineup with a harder rock sound. Matúš Ďurinda and his band will bring hits like "Pravda zvíťazí", "Dnes", "Žerucha", and "Skúsime to cez noc". Tublatanka at the Hradní Oldies Festival represents an ideal connection of Slovak and Czech musical tradition, and their performance will be one of the emotional highlights of the evening.

## Lineup Overview

| Artist | Country | Genre | Greatest Hit |
|-----------|------|------|-------------|
| Village People | USA | Disco, pop | Y.M.C.A. |
| Michal David | Czech Republic | Pop, disco | Nonstop |
| East 17 | UK | Pop, R&B | Stay Another Day |
| Tublatanka | Slovakia | Rock, hard rock | Pravda víťazí |

## Practical Information About the Festival

### Date and Venue

- **Date:** May 16, 2026 (Saturday)
- **Venue:** Slezskoostravský hrad, Ostrava
- **Address:** Hradní 1, 710 00 Ostrava-Slezská Ostrava

### Tickets

Tickets for the Hradní Oldies Festival are available through the portal [akceostrava.cz](/akce). We recommend purchasing in advance — an event with this lineup will sell out quickly.

### Festival Grounds

Slezskoostravský hrad offers a unique setting for open-air events. The grounds are divided into several zones:

- **Main stage** — In front of the castle, where headliners perform
- **Gastro zone** — Refreshments and food stalls
- **Relaxation zone** — Seating and rest between performances

## Getting to Slezskoostravský hrad

### Public Transport

Slezskoostravský hrad is well accessible by tram. The nearest stop is Výstaviště, from which it is approximately 5 minutes on foot. From the main station Ostrava-Svinov, you can use a direct tram connection.

### By Car

Parking in the immediate vicinity of the castle is limited and fills up very quickly on festival day. We recommend:

- **Ostravar Aréna parking lot** — A large-capacity parking lot within walking distance
- **City center** — Parking garages in the center, from which it is 15–20 minutes on foot
- **Park & Ride** — Use outlying parking lots and transfer via public transport

### By Train

Ostrava is well accessible by train from across the Czech Republic and from Slovakia. From the main station Ostrava-Svinov, you can reach Slezskoostravský hrad by tram in approximately 20 minutes.

## What Else Does Ostrava Offer in May 2026

If you're planning a trip to the Hradní Oldies Festival, you can extend your stay in Ostrava with other events:

- **Kollárovci** (May 15, 2026) — Concert at Slezskoostravský hrad, the day before the Hradní Oldies Festival. Tickets: Standard 890 CZK, VIP standing 990 CZK, VIP terrace 2,390 CZK.
- **DJ BoBo** (October 17, 2026) — Concert at Ostravar Aréna for eurodance fans.
- **Fesťáček** (July 11, 2026) — A children's festival for families with kids.

A complete overview of all events in Ostrava can be found at [akceostrava.cz](/akce).

## A Weekend Full of Music — Kollárovci + Hradní Oldies Festival

A unique opportunity for fans is combining two consecutive events at Slezskoostravský hrad:

| Day | Event | Headliner | Genre |
|-----|------|-----------|------|
| Friday, May 15 | Kollárovci | Kollárovci | Folk, entertainment |
| Saturday, May 16 | Hradní Oldies Festival | Village People, Michal David, East 17, Tublatanka | Oldies, disco, rock |

This weekend offers two completely different musical experiences at the same location — from the energetic folk entertainment of Kollárovci to a nostalgic oldies marathon with international stars.

## Tips for Visiting the Festival

### What to Bring

- **Comfortable shoes** — You'll be standing and dancing for several hours
- **A light jacket or raincoat** — May weather in Ostrava can surprise you
- **Good spirits** — The oldies festival is about singing and dancing together

### What to Expect

- **Grounds opening** — Usually 2–3 hours before the first performance begins
- **Refreshments** — Available in the festival grounds, including food and drinks
- **Festival duration** — Expect an entire evening; the last performance usually ends around 11:00 PM

### Safety

The festival grounds are monitored by security services. Standard checks take place at entry. It is not recommended to bring your own alcohol or glass containers.

## FAQ — Frequently Asked Questions

### When does the Hradní Oldies Festival 2026 take place?

The Hradní Oldies Festival takes place on May 16, 2026 (Saturday) at Slezskoostravský hrad in Ostrava.

### Who performs at the Hradní Oldies Festival?

Performing at the festival are Village People, Michal David, East 17, and Tublatanka — a unique combination of domestic and international legends of oldies music.

### Where can I buy tickets for the Hradní Oldies Festival?

Tickets are available on the portal [akceostrava.cz](/akce). We recommend purchasing in advance due to the limited capacity of the grounds.

### Is the Hradní Oldies Festival suitable for children?

The festival is suitable for older children and teenagers who have a connection to oldies music. For the youngest children, we recommend rather Fesťáček (July 11, 2026), which is aimed directly at a children's audience.

### Does the festival take place even in rain?

Yes, the Hradní Oldies Festival takes place in all weather conditions. In case of bad weather, we recommend bringing a raincoat. Umbrellas may be prohibited for safety reasons in the front rows.

### What is the capacity of the grounds?

The capacity of the Slezskoostravský hrad grounds for concert events is limited. Therefore, we recommend purchasing tickets in advance — once sold out, no additional tickets will be available.

## Conclusion

The Hradní Oldies Festival 2026 at Slezskoostravský hrad is one of the most significant music events of the year in Ostrava. The combination of Village People, Michal David, East 17, and Tublatanka creates a lineup that will appeal to every lover of oldies music. The atmosphere of a historic castle, a May evening, and immortal hits — these are the ingredients of an unforgettable experience. Secure your tickets at [akceostrava.cz](/akce) and be there.

title: Kollárovci — The Story of a Legendary Slovak Band
metaDescription: Kollárovci: the story of a Slovak family band, their rise, biggest hits, and concerts in Ostrava. May 15, 2026 at Slezskoostravský hrad.
excerpt: Kollárovci are a phenomenon unmatched on the Slovak music scene. This family band from eastern Slovakia has built one of the largest and most loyal fan bases in Central Europe in a remarkably short time...
body:
# Kollárovci — The Story of a Legendary Slovak Band

Kollárovci are a phenomenon unmatched on the Slovak music scene. This family band from eastern Slovakia has built one of the largest and most loyal fan bases in Central Europe in a remarkably short time. Their concerts regularly sell out within hours, their YouTube channel boasts hundreds of millions of views, and their energetic stage performances are second to none. In this article, we will trace the story of Kollárovci — from humble beginnings through a meteoric rise to their current position as one of the most popular Slovak bands.

## Beginnings — A Family That Plays Together

### Roots in Eastern Slovakia

The story of Kollárovci begins in eastern Slovakia, a region with a rich musical tradition. The Kollár family grew up surrounded by music — singing and playing instruments were a natural part of family life. This authenticity and naturalness later became a key factor in their success with audiences.

Unlike bands that form through music clubs or classified ads, Kollárovci are a genuine family band. The brothers grew up together, learned to play together, and built their musical career together. This family dimension lends their performances a sincerity and cohesion that audiences feel instantly.

### First Steps on the Stage

Kollárovci started out at local events, weddings, and celebrations in the eastern Slovak region. Their ability to entertain audiences and create an unforgettable atmosphere quickly built them a regional reputation. The turning point came with the internet — particularly YouTube, where they began sharing videos of their performances.

## Rise to Popularity

### YouTube as a Springboard

Kollárovci were among the first Slovak bands to fully harness the potential of the internet and social media. Their YouTube videos reached millions of viewers not only in Slovakia and Czechia, but also in the Slovak and Czech diaspora worldwide. The authentic energy of their performances worked just as well on screen as it did live.

The Kollárovci YouTube channel became one of the most-watched music channels in Slovakia. Videos with millions of views attracted the attention of media, promoters, and event organizers — and Kollárovci became a nationwide phenomenon practically overnight.

### From Local Band to National Star

The transition from a regional band to a nationally recognized name happened unusually fast for Kollárovci. The combination of viral videos, positive reviews, and word-of-mouth created an avalanche of interest that the band managed to handle professionally. Instead of small venues, they began filling large halls and festival stages.

## The Musical Style of Kollárovci

### Modern Folk Music

Kollárovci operate at the intersection of traditional Slovak folk music and modern pop-folk production. Their sound combines authentic folk elements — cimbalom, violin, accordion — with modern rhythm and production. The result is music that is both traditional and contemporary, accessible to a wide age range of listeners.

### Repertoire

The Kollárovci repertoire includes:

- **Traditional Slovak folk songs** — In modern arrangements that breathe new life into them
- **Original compositions** — Original songs rooted in folk tradition
- **Covers of popular hits** — Well-known songs in the Kollárovci style, adding surprising moments to concerts
- **Romani songs** — Kollárovci also draw from the Romani musical tradition, which is an integral part of eastern Slovak culture

### Live Sound

Kollárovci are above all a live band. Unlike many contemporary performers who rely on playback and pre-recorded tracks, Kollárovci play live, and their musical proficiency is undeniable. Every band member is an excellent instrumentalist, and vocal harmonies are one of the pillars of their sound.

## Why Kollárovci Are So Popular

### Authenticity

In an era of highly stylized, producer-driven music, Kollárovci offer an authentic experience. Their music comes from a real tradition, their stage presence is sincere and natural. Audiences appreciate that Kollárovci are themselves on stage — without pretense or posturing.

### Concert Energy

Kollárovci concerts are renowned for their energy. The band can keep the audience moving throughout performances that often last over three hours. Dancing, singing, audience interaction — Kollárovci create an atmosphere at their concerts that nobody wants to leave.

### Cross-Generational Appeal

The music of Kollárovci appeals to all generations. Older listeners appreciate the return to folk tradition, the middle generation enjoys the energetic performances, and younger audiences are drawn to the dynamism and accessibility of their music. At Kollárovci concerts, you will find families, couples, and groups of friends of all ages.

### Fan Community

Kollárovci have built an extraordinarily loyal fan community. This community is active on social media, shares concert videos, and organizes group trips to the band's performances. The Kollárovci fan base functions almost like a fan club with its own identity and traditions.

## Kollárovci and Czech Audiences

### Popularity in Czechia

Kollárovci have also gained enormous popularity in the Czech Republic, especially in Moravia and Silesia. Their concerts in Czech cities regularly sell out, and the Czech fan base continues to grow. Linguistic proximity and cultural affinity make it easy for Czech audiences to embrace Slovak folk music.

### Ostrava — A Second Home

Ostrava is among the cities where Kollárovci have the strongest fan base in the Czech Republic. The proximity to the Slovak border, a sizable Slovak community in the region, and the folk music tradition in the Moravian-Silesian Region create ideal conditions for Kollárovci performances. Their Ostrava concerts are among the most attended musical events in the city.

## Kollárovci at Slezskoostravský hrad — May 15, 2026

### Event Information

Kollárovci will perform on **May 15, 2026** at Slezskoostravský hrad in Ostrava. This is one of the band's most significant events in the Czech Republic in 2026 and an opportunity to experience their energetic performance in the unique setting of a historic castle.

### Tickets

| Category | Price | Description |
|-----------|------|-------|
| Standard | 890 CZK | General admission, standing in the main area |
| VIP Standing | 990 CZK | Priority entry, better position near the stage |
| VIP Terrace | 2,390 CZK | Exclusive spot on the terrace with a view, refreshments included |

Tickets are available at [akceostrava.cz](/akce). Given the high popularity of Kollárovci, we recommend purchasing well in advance.

### What to Expect

- **Concert length** — At least 2.5 to 3 hours of music and entertainment
- **Repertoire** — A cross-section of their entire body of work, from traditional folk songs to their own hits
- **Atmosphere** — Energetic, friendly, and highly interactive
