## FAQ — Frequently Asked Questions

### Where do Kollárovci come from?

Kollárovci come from eastern Slovakia. They are brothers who formed a family band rooted in the tradition of Slovak folk music combined with modern elements.

### How much do tickets cost for the Kollárovci concert in Ostrava?

Tickets for the May 15, 2026 concert at Slezskoostravský hrad are available in three categories: Standard for 890 CZK, VIP Standing for 990 CZK, and VIP Terrace for 2,390 CZK. You can purchase them at [akceostrava.cz](/akce).

### How long does a Kollárovci concert last?

Kollárovci concerts typically last 2.5 to 3 hours, sometimes even longer. The band is known for their long performances, maintaining their energy from the first minute to the last.

### Are Kollárovci concerts suitable for children?

Yes, Kollárovci concerts are suitable for the whole family. The atmosphere is friendly and positive, and the music appeals to all age groups. For smaller children, we recommend hearing protection and access to a rest area.

### Do Kollárovci play live?

Yes, Kollárovci are primarily a live band. Unlike many contemporary performers, they play completely live without playback. Their instrumental and vocal proficiency is one of the main reasons their concerts are so impressive.

### Can I combine the Kollárovci concert with the Hradní Oldies Festival?

Absolutely. Kollárovci play on Friday, May 15, and the Hradní Oldies Festival (Village People, Michal David, East 17, Tublatanka) takes place on Saturday, May 16 — both at Slezskoostravský hrad. It is an ideal opportunity for a music-filled weekend in Ostrava.

## Conclusion

The story of Kollárovci is a story of talent, authenticity, and family unity. From a regional band in eastern Slovakia, they have become one of the biggest phenomena on the Central European music scene. Their concert at Slezskoostravský hrad on May 15, 2026 is an opportunity to experience this energy firsthand. Tickets from 890 CZK are available at [akceostrava.cz](/akce) — and given the band's popularity, we recommend not hesitating.

## Kollárovci Greatest Hits and Most Beautiful Songs

Kollárovci are among the most popular music groups to successfully bridge the Romani musical tradition with modern folk-pop, captivating hundreds of thousands of listeners across Czechia and Slovakia. Their YouTube channel boasts hundreds of millions of views, and their live concerts regularly sell out halls and summer amphitheaters. In this article, we will look at their greatest hits, most beautiful ballads, and songs you definitely should not miss.

### Who Are Kollárovci?

Kollárovci are a family band originally from eastern Slovakia. The Kollár brothers — along with other family members — have been playing and singing since a very young age. Their repertoire ranges from traditional Romani and Slovak folk songs through gospel to modern pop arrangements. It is precisely this ability to blend genres that has earned them an enormous fan base.

- **Origin:** Eastern Slovakia
- **Genre:** Folk, Romani music, gospel, pop
- **YouTube:** Hundreds of millions of views
- **Concert activity:** Dozens of performances per year in both Czech Republic and Slovakia

### Greatest Hits by Kollárovci

The following table summarizes the most well-known songs that have won listeners' hearts and are regularly performed at concerts.

| Song | Genre | Why You Know It |
|-------|------|---------------|
| Ja som žena | Folk-pop | An anthem of female strength, millions of views on YouTube |
| Cigánsky čardáš | Romani tradition | An energetic csárdás full of emotion and dance energy |
| Ej, husličky | Folk ballad | A Slovak folk classic in a modern arrangement |
| Hej, sokoly | Folk | A Slavic folk song everyone knows |
| Ďakujem ti, Bože | Gospel | A spiritual song with beautiful harmony |
| Zem spieva | Folk-pop | A celebration of nature and the Slovak countryside |
| Pod tým naším okienkom | Folk | A traditional melody with a new arrangement |

### Most Beautiful Ballads and Slow Songs

Kollárovci excel not only in fast csárdás numbers but also in emotive ballads. It is the slow songs that showcase the power of their vocal harmonies — the family's multi-part singing creates a sound that is hard to replicate.

- **Mama** — a touching song dedicated to mothers that has moved thousands of viewers to tears at live concerts
- **Modlitba** — a spiritual ballad combining Romani melody with gospel depth
- **Láska nebeská** — a romantic composition with orchestral arrangement
- **Keď sa ráno zobudím** — a calm morning song full of gratitude

These ballads shine brightest at a live concert, where the acoustics and atmosphere amplify every note.

### Concert Hits — What Will They Play Live?

At Kollárovci concerts, you can expect a cross-section of their entire body of work. A typical setlist includes:

1. **Opening energy block** — csárdás numbers and dance pieces that get the whole audience moving
2. **Ballad middle section** — emotive songs that make time stand still
3. **Slovak and Romani folk songs** — traditional repertoire that bridges generations
4. **Gospel section** — spiritual songs with powerful multi-part vocals
5. **Final ride** — greatest hits and sing-alongs with the audience

If you want to see Kollárovci live, you do not have to travel far. The group regularly performs in Ostrava as well — current dates and tickets can be found on the [our events](/akce) page.

### Why Are Kollárovci So Popular?

The Kollárovci phenomenon rests on several pillars:

- **Authenticity** — they are not a product of the music industry but a family band rooted in tradition
- **YouTube strategy** — they were among the first on the Slovak scene to bet on music videos and online distribution
- **Genre diversity** — they appeal to fans of folk music as well as lovers of pop and gospel
- **Family chemistry** — sibling harmony in singing is unbeatable
- **Concert energy** — their live performances are full of emotion, humor, and audience interaction

### Kollárovci and the Romani Musical Tradition

The work of Kollárovci is deeply rooted in the Romani musical tradition of eastern Slovakia. Romani music is characterized by:

- Distinctive rhythms and tempo changes
- Emotive vocal delivery
- Use of violin, double bass, and guitar
- Improvisation and spontaneity in live performance

Kollárovci respect this tradition while also pushing it forward — adding modern production, electronic instruments, and pop melodies. The result is a sound that is unique and instantly recognizable.

### Where to Listen to Kollárovci Hits?

| Platform | What You Will Find |
|-----------|-----------|
| YouTube | Music videos, live recordings, full concerts |
| Spotify | Studio recordings, playlists |
| Apple Music | Complete discography |
| Live concerts | The most authentic experience — concert schedule at [akceostrava.cz](/akce) |

### FAQ — Most Frequent Questions About Kollárovci Hits

**What is the biggest hit by Kollárovci?**
Among the most listened to are "Ja som žena" and "Cigánsky čardáš," both of which have tens of millions of views on YouTube.

**Do Kollárovci sing in Czech?**
They predominantly sing in Slovak and Romani, but their repertoire also includes Czech folk songs and songs understandable to Czech audiences.

**How many songs do Kollárovci have?**
The Kollárovci discography comprises hundreds of recordings — from studio albums to live recordings and special projects.

**Are Kollárovci suitable for family audiences?**
Absolutely. Their concerts are attended by families with children as well as seniors — the repertoire is diverse and the atmosphere friendly.

**Where can I see Kollárovci live in 2026?**
Kollárovci are planning a number of concerts across Czechia and Slovakia. In Ostrava, they will perform on May 15 at Slezskoostravský hrad — tickets and other events can be found at [akceostrava.cz](/akce).

### Conclusion — Hits You Must Hear Live

Kollárovci hits sound great through speakers and headphones, but only a live concert reveals their full power. The energy of the audience, multi-part harmonies bouncing off castle walls or hall ceilings, and spontaneous moments — all of this makes a Kollárovci concert an unforgettable experience. If you have only known their songs from YouTube so far, it is time to change that and go to a concert.

### FAQ — Frequently Asked Questions

title: Slovak Folk Music — A Guide to the Genre and Concerts
metaDescription: A guide to Slovak folk music — history, artists, instruments, and where to see folk live in Ostrava. Kollárovci, Kandráčovci, and more.
excerpt: Slovak folk music is one of the richest musical traditions in Central Europe. From highland Wallachian melodies through spirited csárdás dances to moving Romani ballads — Slovak folk is a genre full of emoti...
body:
## Slovak Folk Music — A Guide to the Genre and Concerts

Slovak folk music is one of the richest musical traditions in Central Europe. From highland Wallachian melodies through spirited csárdás dances to moving Romani ballads — Slovak folk is a genre full of emotion, stories, and generational heritage. In recent years, this genre has experienced an unexpected renaissance thanks to bands like Kollárovci, who connect tradition with modern production and reach millions of listeners online. In this article, we will explore the roots of Slovak folk music, its main currents, instruments, and artists — and show you where you can experience it live.

### History of Slovak Folk Music

Slovak folk music has evolved over centuries and reflects the diverse cultural mosaic of the Carpathian region. Key stages include:

- **Middle Ages and Early Modern Period** — pastoral songs, ritual chants, work songs tied to the agricultural cycle
- **18th–19th Century** — flourishing of folk singing and dance, emergence of regional styles (Podpoľanie, Detva, Šariš, Zemplín)
- **Era of Collectors** — Béla Bartók and other ethnomusicologists recorded Slovak melodies for future generations
- **20th Century** — folk music became part of national identity; professional folklore ensembles were founded
- **21st Century** — a renaissance driven by YouTube, fusion genres, and new bands

### Regional Styles of Slovak Folk

Slovakia is musically extraordinarily diverse. Each region has its own specific style:

| Region | Characteristics | Typical Instruments |
|--------|----------------|-----------------|
| **Podpoľanie** | Slow, melancholic melodies | Fujara, whistle |
| **Šariš** | Energetic, danceable | Violin, cimbalom |
| **Zemplín** | Romani influence, emotive | Violin, double bass |
| **Detva** | Pastoral singing | Fujara, bagpipes |
| **Záhorie** | Moravian influence, cheerful | Clarinet, cimbalom |
| **Sub-Carpathian** | Carpathian folk | Trembita, violin |

### Key Instruments of Slovak Folk Music

Slovak folk is characterized by unique instruments that have no counterpart in other European traditions:

- **Fujara** — a monumental shepherd's flute, inscribed on the UNESCO list as a Masterpiece of the Oral and Intangible Heritage of Humanity. It measures up to 180 cm and produces a deep, meditative sound.
- **Cimbalom** — a hammered string instrument, the heart of every folk band. Characterized by a bell-like tone.
- **Gajdy** — Slovak bagpipes, typical of pastoral music.
- **Violin** — the foundation of both Romani and non-Romani folk bands.
- **Double bass** — the rhythmic foundation that keeps the tempo.
- **Clarinet** — common in western Slovak folk.

### Slovak Folk Today — Who Is Shaping the Genre?

The contemporary Slovak folk scene is more vibrant than ever. Key artists can be divided into several streams:

**Traditional folk:**
- Folklore ensembles (Lúčnica, SĽUK) — preserving authentic tradition
- Regional bands — playing at weddings, feasts, and festivals

**Modern folk and fusion:**
- **Kollárovci** — Romani folk with pop and gospel elements, hundreds of millions of YouTube views
- **Kandráčovci** — energetic folk-pop from eastern Slovakia
- **IMT Smile** — pop-rock with folk roots

**Romani folk:**
- Kollárovci, Gipsy.cz, and other bands bring the Romani musical tradition to a broad audience
- Romani folk from Slovakia is characterized by emotive singing, distinctive rhythms, and improvisation

### The Connection Between Slovak and Czech Folk

Czech and Slovak folk music share common roots — Moravian folk is a natural bridge between the two traditions. For Czech audiences, Slovak folk is understandable and familiar:

- Linguistic proximity makes it easy to understand the lyrics
- The shared history of Czechoslovakia means a shared repertoire
- Moravian folk songs often transition smoothly into Slovak melodies
- Czech fans of Slovak folk form a large and growing group

### Where to Experience Slovak Folk Live in Ostrava?

Thanks to its location at the crossroads of Moravia and Silesia, Ostrava is a natural meeting point for Czech and Slovak folk. In 2026, it offers several opportunities:

| Date | Event | Venue | Details |
|-------|------|-------|--------|
| May 15, 2026 | **Kollárovci** | Slezskoostravský hrad | Romani folk, tickets from 890 CZK |
| May 16, 2026 | **Hradní Oldies Festival** | Slezskoostravský hrad | Mix of genres including Tublatanka |

Tickets and the complete program can be found at [akceostrava.cz](/akce).

### Why Is Slovak Folk So Popular?

The popularity of Slovak folk has grown sharply in recent years. The reasons are clear:

1. **Authenticity** — in an era of artificial music, people seek real emotions and stories
2. **The YouTube effect** — bands like Kollárovci managed to reach millions of people online
3. **Generational transmission** — grandparents sang folk songs to their children, who now search for them on the internet
4. **Fusion genres** — modern production makes folk accessible to younger audiences
5. **Festival culture** — folklore festivals are social events, not just musical showcases
6. **Nostalgia** — Slovak folk songs evoke memories of childhood, the countryside, and family gatherings

### Fujara — The Slovak National Instrument

The fujara deserves special attention. This unique instrument:

- Was inscribed on the UNESCO list in 2005
- Measures 150–180 cm and is played standing up
- Originally served mountain shepherds as a meditative instrument
- Produces a deep, overtone-rich sound reminiscent of natural harmony
- Today it is also used in modern music and fusion projects

### FAQ — Most Frequent Questions About Slovak Folk Music

**What is Slovak folk?**
Slovak folk is a genre rooted in the folk musical tradition of Slovakia — it encompasses pastoral songs, dance csárdás numbers, Romani ballads, and modern folk-pop projects.

**Who are the most famous Slovak folk artists?**
Among the most popular are Kollárovci, Kandráčovci, IMT Smile, and professional ensembles such as Lúčnica and SĽUK.

**Where can I hear Slovak folk live in Czechia?**
Slovak folk bands regularly perform in Czechia. In Ostrava, Kollárovci will perform on May 15, 2026 at Slezskoostravský hrad — tickets at [akceostrava.cz](/akce).

**What is a fujara?**
The fujara is a traditional Slovak shepherd's flute measuring up to 180 cm, inscribed on the UNESCO list. It is a symbol of Slovak folk music.

**What is the difference between Slovak and Moravian folk?**
Both genres share common roots, but Slovak folk is more diverse due to Romani influence and specific regional styles (fujara, csárdás). Moravian folk is closer to the Czech environment.

**Is Slovak folk suitable for young audiences?**
Absolutely — modern artists like Kollárovci or Kandráčovci appeal to all generations by connecting tradition with pop production.

### Conclusion — A Genre That Never Dies

Slovak folk music is living proof that tradition does not have to be dusty. Thanks to new artists, YouTube, and festival culture, Slovak folk is experiencing a golden era. If you want to get to know this genre live, Ostrava in 2026 offers excellent opportunities — start with the Kollárovci concert at Slezskoostravský hrad and discover the magic of Slovak music with your own ears. Follow [akceostrava.cz](/akce) for the latest concert offerings.

title: East 17 — The Story of a British Boyband Legend from the 90s
metaDescription: East 17 — the story of a British boyband from the 90s. Hits Stay Another Day, House of Love, and a concert in Ostrava on May 16, 2026 at the Hradní Oldies Festival.
excerpt: The nineties were the golden era of boy bands — and one of the brightest stars of that period was East 17. Unlike the polished pop of rivals Take That, the lads from east London bet on a rougher image, street style, and hits...
body:
## East 17 — The Story of a British Boyband Legend from the 90s

The nineties were the golden era of boy bands — and one of the brightest stars of that period was East 17. Unlike the polished pop of rivals Take That, the lads from east London bet on a rougher image, street style, and hits that combined pop with R&B and hip-hop. Their song "Stay Another Day" became one of the most famous Christmas hits of all time. In this article, we will look at the story of East 17 — from the formation of the band through mega-hits to their current comeback tour, which is bringing them to Ostrava.

### The Formation of East 17 — Lads from Walthamstow

East 17 were formed in 1991 in the Walthamstow district of London's East End — hence their name (postal code E17). The founding lineup consisted of:

- **Tony Mortimer** — main songwriter and rapper
- **Brian Harvey** — lead vocalist
- **John Hendy** — vocalist
- **Terry Coldwell** — vocalist and dancer

The band was discovered and managed by producer Tom Watkins, who had previously been behind the success of Pet Shop Boys. From their first single, it was clear that East 17 would be different — while Take That targeted teenage girls with polished pop, East 17 brought the rough sound of the streets, baggy clothing, and rap passages.

### East 17's Greatest Hits

East 17 released a series of hits in the 90s that topped charts across Europe:

| Song | Year | UK Position | Note |
|-------|-----|-------------|----------|
| **House of Love** | 1992 | #10 | Breakthrough single, defined the band's sound |
| **Deep** | 1993 | #5 | Dark, R&B-flavored hit |
| **It's Alright** | 1993 | #3 | Summer anthem with ragga influence |
| **Around the World** | 1994 | #3 | Worldwide hit |
| **Steam** | 1994 | #7 | Energetic dance track |
| **Stay Another Day** | 1994 | **#1** | Christmas classic, the band's most famous song |
| **Let It Rain** | 1995 | #10 | Ballad hit |
| **If You Ever** (feat. Gabrielle) | 1996 | #2 | Duet with Gabrielle |

### Stay Another Day — The Song That Changed Everything

"Stay Another Day" is without a doubt the most famous song by East 17. Released in November 1994, it reached number one on the UK singles chart and became the Christmas number one hit. What makes this song exceptional:

- **Emotive lyrics** — Tony Mortimer wrote it as a tribute to his brother, who took his own life
- **Orchestral arrangement** — unlike typical East 17 production, it is a ballad with orchestra
- **Iconic music video** — band members in white parkas amid a snowy landscape
- **Timelessness** — the song returns to the charts every year in the pre-Christmas period

### East 17 vs. Take That — The Rivalry of the 90s

The nineties were defined by the rivalry of two British boy bands, which divided an entire generation:

| | East 17 | Take That |
|---|---------|-----------|
| **Style** | Street, hip-hop, R&B | Polished pop, dance |
| **Image** | Baggy clothes, caps, toughness | Elegance, choreography |
| **Main star** | Brian Harvey | Robbie Williams / Gary Barlow |
| **Sound** | Darker, more experimental | Melodic, mainstream |
| **Christmas hit** | Stay Another Day | Back for Good |

Both bands had millions of fans, and their rivalry was the main topic of the British music press. While Take That successfully returned after their breakup as mainstream giants, East 17 maintained cult status and a loyal fan base.

### Breakup and Comebacks

The story of East 17 after the golden era of the 90s was not straightforward:

- **1997** — Brian Harvey leaves the band after controversial statements
- **1997–1999** — the band continues as E-17 without Harvey
- **1999** — official breakup
- **2006** — first reunion in the four-member lineup
- **2010s** — a series of comebacks in various lineups
- **2020s** — East 17 continue touring and travel across Europe with nostalgia tours

The current version of East 17 focuses on live concerts and retro festivals — and it is at one such festival that you can see them in Ostrava.

### East 17 in Ostrava — Hradní Oldies Festival 2026

East 17 will perform on **May 16, 2026** at the **Hradní Oldies Festival** at Slezskoostravský hrad in Ostrava. The festival is organized by RESTARTSTAGE PRODUCTION and will offer a unique combination of legends from the 80s and 90s:

| Artist | Genre | Biggest Hit |
|-----------|------|-------------|
| **Village People** | Disco | Y.M.C.A. |
| **Michal David** | Pop, disco | Nonstop, Céčka |
| **East 17** | Pop, R&B | Stay Another Day |
| **Tublatanka** | Rock | Pravda víťazí |

### Tickets for the Hradní Oldies Festival

| Category | Price |
|-----------|------|
| **Standard** | 999 CZK |
| **VIP Standing** | 1,490 CZK |
| **VIP Terrace** | 2,990 CZK |

Tickets are on sale at [akceostrava.cz](/akce). With four artists at one festival, it represents an exceptional value for the experience.

### What to Expect from a Live East 17 Performance?

East 17 concerts are an energetic ride full of nostalgia:

- **Greatest hits** — Stay Another Day, House of Love, It's Alright, Deep, and more
- **Authentic 90s sound** — the band preserves the original arrangements
- **Audience interaction** — fans sing along to every lyric
- **Visual style** — the street aesthetic the band never abandoned
- **Performance length** — approx. 45–60 minutes as part of the festival program

### The Legacy of East 17 in British Pop Culture

East 17 left an indelible mark on British music history. They proved that a boyband does not have to be polished and prefabricated — they brought hip-hop and R&B elements into pop before it became the norm. Stay Another Day remains one of the most-played Christmas songs in the history of British music, and the band remains an icon of 90s retro culture.

### FAQ — Frequently Asked Questions About East 17

**Who are East 17?**
East 17 are a British boyband from the 90s, known for hits like Stay Another Day, House of Love, and It's Alright. They come from east London.

**Where can I see East 17 in Czechia in 2026?**
East 17 will perform on May 16, 2026 at the Hradní Oldies Festival at Slezskoostravský hrad in Ostrava. Tickets at [akceostrava.cz](/akce).

**How much do tickets cost for East 17 in Ostrava?**
Tickets for the Hradní Oldies Festival (where East 17 will perform) cost from 999 CZK (Standard) to 2,990 CZK (VIP Terrace).

**What is East 17's most famous hit?**
Without a doubt, "Stay Another Day" from 1994 — a Christmas number one in Britain and one of the most iconic songs of the 90s.

**Are East 17 still active?**
Yes, East 17 continue touring and regularly perform at festivals and retro events across Europe.

**Who else is performing at the Hradní Oldies Festival?**
In addition to East 17, Village People, Michal David, and Tublatanka will appear at the festival.

### Conclusion

East 17 are more than just a 90s boyband — they are a symbol of an era that shaped popular music for entire generations. Their hits sound just as fresh after thirty years, and their live concerts prove that the energy of those east London lads has not faded. If you want to experience Stay Another Day under the open sky at a historic castle, May 16, 2026 is your day. Tickets for the Hradní Oldies Festival are available at [akceostrava.cz](/akce).

title: East 17 Greatest Hits — Stay Another Day, House of Love, and More
metaDescription: East 17 greatest hits: Stay Another Day, House of Love, It's Alright, and more. A complete guide to the discography of the British boyband legend.
excerpt: British pop group East 17 is one of the most successful boy bands of the nineties. The quartet from east London — Tony Mortimer, Brian Harvey, John Hendy, and Terry Coldwell — burst onto the scene in 1...
body:
## East 17 Greatest Hits — Stay Another Day, House of Love, and More

British pop group East 17 is one of the most successful boy bands of the nineties. The quartet from east London — Tony Mortimer, Brian Harvey, John Hendy, and Terry Coldwell — burst onto the scene in 1992 and within a few years sold over 18 million records worldwide. Their music combined pop with elements of new jack swing, hip-hop, and R&B, which set them distinctly apart from rivals Take That. In this article, we will go through their greatest hits that still resonate with fans across Europe — and which you will hear at the [Hradní Oldies Festival 2026 in Ostrava](/akce).

### How East 17 Changed the Boyband Scene

In the early nineties, the British pop scene was dominated by Take That with their polished image and choreographed performances. East 17 offered an alternative — a street style, baggy clothes, baseball caps, and a rough sound inspired by American R&B. This allowed them to reach a different segment of the audience and carve out their own niche in a crowded genre.

The band's main songwriter was Tony Mortimer, who wrote most of the group's hits. It was his ability to combine catchy melodies with lyrics about real life on London housing estates that gave East 17 an authenticity that has survived decades.

### Complete Overview of East 17's Greatest Hits

| Song | Year Released | Highest UK Position | Album |
|-------|-----------|---------------------|-------|
| House of Love | 1992 | 10 | Walthamstow |
| Deep | 1993 | 5 | Walthamstow |
| It's Alright | 1993 | 3 | Walthamstow |
| Around the World | 1994 | 3 | Steam |
| Steam | 1994 | 7 | Steam |
| Stay Another Day | 1994 | 1 | Steam |
| Let It Rain | 1995 | 10 | Up All Night |
| If You Ever (ft. Gabrielle) | 1996 | 2 | Up All Night |
| Hey Child | 1996 | 3 | Up All Night |
| Each Time | 1998 | 2 | Resurrection |

### Stay Another Day — A Christmas Hit That Transcended the Genre

If there is one song that defines East 17, it is undoubtedly **Stay Another Day**. Released in November 1994, this emotive ballad became the Christmas number one in the United Kingdom — beating both Mariah Carey and East 17's own rivals from Take That.

Tony Mortimer wrote the song as a tribute to his brother, who took his own life. The lyrics are deeply personal and urgent — a plea to a loved one to stay just one more day. It is precisely this sincerity, combined with a majestic orchestral arrangement and the iconic snow parkas in the video, that created one of the most powerful moments in nineties British pop.

Stay Another Day sold millions of copies across Europe and remains one of the most-played Christmas hits on British radio to this day. In the Czech Republic, the song became known through MTV and music television channels that played it in heavy rotation.

### House of Love — Where the Legend Began

**House of Love** was one of East 17's first singles and immediately showed the direction the band would take. An energetic track with a prominent bass riff and Brian Harvey's rap passages set the bar for the entire debut album Walthamstow. The song reached number ten in the UK charts and introduced the band to a wide audience.

### It's Alright — A Nineties Anthem

**It's Alright** is perhaps the most optimistic song in the East 17 repertoire. The positive message — "it's alright, it's alright" — combined with a catchy melody and dance rhythm created an anthem that worked in clubs, on the radio, and in stadiums. The song reached number three in the British charts and became one of the defining hits of 1993.

### Deep — The Dark Side of East 17

While most East 17 hits were melodic and accessible, **Deep** revealed a different side of the band. Darker production with a prominent beat and urgent vocals created an atmosphere more reminiscent of R&B than traditional pop. The song proved that East 17 were not a one-dimensional boyband but a group capable of genre diversity.

### Around the World and Steam — The Second Album Era

The second album **Steam** (1994) brought a shift in both production and maturity. **Around the World** with its oriental samples and a cinematic video shot on several continents showed the band's ambition to transcend the boundaries of British pop. The title track **Steam** then offered a raw, synthetic sound with a prominent beat — ideal material for clubs and concerts.

### If You Ever — A Duet with Gabrielle

The collaboration with British singer Gabrielle on the single **If You Ever** (1996) is one of the most beautiful moments in East 17's career. The emotive duet reached number two in the UK charts and showed that the band could handle more sophisticated territory. The combination of Gabrielle's velvety voice with East 17's harmonies created a timeless ballad.

### Each Time — A Comeback Hit

After a turbulent period that included Brian Harvey's departure and media scandals, East 17 returned in 1998 with the album **Resurrection** and the single **Each Time**. The song reached number two in the British charts and confirmed that the band's fan base had remained loyal.

### East 17 Discography in Numbers

- **18 million** records sold worldwide
- **18 singles** in the British Top 40
- **1 Christmas number one** (Stay Another Day)
- **4 studio albums** in the nineties
- **2 BRIT Awards** nominations
- Active in **more than 30 countries** worldwide

### Where to Hear East 17 Hits Live in 2026

East 17 are returning to the Czech Republic in 2026 — specifically to Ostrava. At the [Hradní Oldies Festival](/akce) at Slezskoostravský hrad on May 16, 2026, they will play a set full of their greatest hits. Alongside them, Village People, Michal David, and Tublatanka will perform — making it one of the biggest oldies events of the year in the Czech Republic.

Tickets for the Hradní Oldies Festival are available in several categories — from standard admission at 999 CZK through VIP Standing at 1,490 CZK to the VIP Terrace at 2,990 CZK. The complete offering can be found at [akceostrava.cz](/akce).

## Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)

### What is East 17's greatest hit?

East 17's greatest hit is **Stay Another Day** — a Christmas number one from 1994 that sold millions of copies across Europe and remains one of the most-played Christmas hits in the United Kingdom to this day.

### How many records did East 17 sell?

East 17 sold over 18 million records worldwide, making them one of the most successful British boyband groups of the nineties.

### Who wrote most of East 17's hits?

The main songwriter of East 17 was **Tony Mortimer**, who wrote most of the group's hits including Stay Another Day, House of Love, and It's Alright.

### Where can I see East 17 live in 2026?

East 17 will perform at the **Hradní Oldies Festival** at Slezskoostravský hrad in Ostrava on May 16, 2026. Tickets are available at [akceostrava.cz](/akce).

### Are East 17 still active?

Yes, East 17 are still active and regularly perform at festivals and retro events across Europe. The current lineup consists of Terry Coldwell and John Hendy along with other members. The band plays their greatest hits from the nineties live.

body:
## East 17 in Ostrava — Hradní Oldies Festival 2026

British boyband legend East 17 is heading to Ostrava. On May 16, 2026, they will perform at the Hradní Oldies Festival at Slezskoostravský hrad — one of the most atmospheric concert locations in the Czech Republic. Fans can look forward to the hits Stay Another Day, House of Love, It's Alright, and other songs that defined the nineties. In this article, you will find everything you need — from ticket information and practical advice to an overview of what to expect from the East 17 concert in Ostrava.

### Why the Arrival of East 17 in Ostrava Is Exceptional

East 17 rarely perform in the Czech Republic. In recent years, the group has focused their European tours mainly on the United Kingdom, Germany, and Scandinavia. The performance at the Hradní Oldies Festival is therefore one of the few opportunities to see the British band live on Czech soil in 2026.

The combination of East 17 with other legends — Village People, Michal David, and Tublatanka — also creates a lineup unmatched in the Czech festival landscape. It is a unique intersection of British, American, Czech, and Slovak pop history on one stage.

### Hradní Oldies Festival — What to Know

The Hradní Oldies Festival takes place in the courtyard of Slezskoostravský hrad, which is among the most sought-after concert venues in the Moravian-Silesian Region. The historic castle setting creates an unforgettable atmosphere appreciated by both artists and audiences.

**Basic festival information:**

| Detail | Info |
|------|--------|
| Date | May 16, 2026 (Saturday) |
| Venue | Slezskoostravský hrad, Ostrava |
| Lineup | Village People, Michal David, East 17, Tublatanka |
| Time | 3:00 PM – 11:00 PM |
| Organizer | RESTARTSTAGE PRODUCTION |

### Ticket Categories and Prices

The Hradní Oldies Festival offers three ticket categories that differ in price and comfort. The choice depends on your preferences — do you want to be in the crowd and experience the energy to the fullest, or do you prefer comfort with premium service?

| Category | Price | What It Includes |
|-----------|------|------------|
| Standard | 999 CZK | Festival entry, access to the main stage |
| VIP Standing | 1,490 CZK | Access to the VIP zone with a better view of the stage |
| VIP Terrace | 2,990 CZK | Access to a covered terrace with an exclusive view |

Tickets are available at [akceostrava.cz](/akce). We recommend buying in advance — the Hradní Oldies Festival with this lineup will likely sell out quickly.

### What to Expect from the East 17 Performance

East 17 are known for energetic live performances that combine singing, dancing, and audience interaction. The current band lineup — with Terry Coldwell and John Hendy — keeps the spirit of the nineties alive and plays the complete set of greatest hits at concerts.

**Expected East 17 setlist at the Hradní Oldies Festival:**

- House of Love
- Deep
- It's Alright
- Around the World
- Steam
- Let It Rain
- If You Ever
- Hey Child
- Each Time
- **Stay Another Day** (closing song)

The exact setlist may vary, but these songs form the backbone of every East 17 performance. Stay Another Day traditionally closes the concert, creating an emotive moment when the entire audience sings together.

### Slezskoostravský hrad — Practical Information for Visitors

Slezskoostravský hrad is located in the center of Ostrava, close to the Ostravice river. Transport access is excellent — the castle is reachable by tram, bus, and car.

**How to get to Slezskoostravský hrad:**

- **Public transport** — The nearest tram stop is "Výstaviště," approximately a 10-minute walk away. Buses stop at the "Slezskoostravský hrad" stop.
- **By car** — Parking is available near the castle, but capacity is limited. We recommend arriving early or using P+R near tram stops.
- **On foot** — From the center of Ostrava, the castle is approximately a 20-minute walk across the bridge over the Ostravice.

### What to Bring to the Festival

May weather in Ostrava can be variable. The festival takes place outdoors, so we recommend:

- **A rain poncho or light jacket** — May evenings can be cool
- **Comfortable shoes** — you will be standing for several hours
- **Cash and card** — festival refreshment stands usually accept both
- **A phone charger / power bank** — for taking photos and recording videos
- **Earplugs** — if you prefer hearing protection in the front rows

### Other Artists at the Hradní Oldies Festival 2026

East 17 are not the only draw of the festival. The lineup includes four exceptional artists:

**Village People** — American disco legends known for hits Y.M.C.A., In the Navy, and Macho Man. Their show is always spectacular and interactive — count on dancing.

**Michal David** — The king of Czech pop music of the eighties and nineties. Hits like Nonstop, Poupata, and Céčka, céčka are known by every Czech. Michal David guarantees a roaring party.

**Tublatanka** — A Slovak rock legend with hits Pravda víťazí, Žeravé uhlíky, and Dnes. Their concerts are energetic and appeal to fans across generations.

### Why Not Buy a Ticket Right Away

There are several reasons not to delay purchasing tickets:

- **Limited capacity** — The courtyard of Slezskoostravský hrad holds a limited number of spectators. Once tickets sell out, there will be no more.
- **VIP categories sell fastest** — If you are interested in the VIP Terrace at 2,990 CZK, act as soon as possible.
- **Unique lineup** — The combination of four legends at one festival may not be repeated.
- **May date** — Ideal timing for an open-air event — neither too hot nor too cold.

Tickets can be purchased at [akceostrava.cz](/akce).

## Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)

### When will East 17 perform at the Hradní Oldies Festival?

East 17 will perform at the Hradní Oldies Festival on **May 16, 2026** at Slezskoostravský hrad in Ostrava. The festival runs from 3:00 PM to 11:00 PM.

Tickets are available in three categories — Standard at 999 CZK, VIP Standing at 1,490 CZK, and VIP Terrace at 2,990 CZK. Purchase is possible at [akceostrava.cz](/akce).

### Who else is performing at the Hradní Oldies Festival 2026?

Alongside East 17, **Village People**, **Michal David**, and **Tublatanka** will perform. It is a unique mix of British, American, Czech, and Slovak pop and rock classics.

### Is the Hradní Oldies Festival suitable for children?

The festival is suitable for all age groups. The music is accessible and the atmosphere at Slezskoostravský hrad is family-friendly. Children under a certain age typically have free entry — check the event page for exact conditions.

### Will there be food and drinks at the festival?

Yes, the Hradní Oldies Festival will have food and drink stalls available. Details about the gastro zone will be published before the event.

title: Boyband Concerts in the Czech Republic 2026 — East 17, Backstreet Boys, and More
metaDescription: Boyband concerts in Czechia 2026: East 17, Backstreet Boys, and more. A complete overview of boyband performances in the Czech Republic, tickets, and tips for fans.
excerpt: The boyband mania of the nineties never truly ended — it just transformed. In 2026, several legendary boy bands that defined the pop culture of an entire generation are returning to the Czech Republic...
body:
## Boyband Concerts in the Czech Republic 2026 — East 17, Backstreet Boys, and More

The boyband mania of the nineties never truly ended — it just transformed. In 2026, several legendary boy bands that defined the pop culture of an entire generation are returning to the Czech Republic. From British East 17 through American giants Backstreet Boys to newer formations — the offering of boyband concerts in Czechia this year is exceptionally rich. In this guide, you will find a complete overview, ticket tips, and everything you need to know as a fan of boyband music.

### The Boyband Phenomenon — Why It Survived Decades

The concept of a boyband — a vocal group of young men combining singing, dancing, and an attractive image — has existed since the sixties (The Jackson 5, The Osmonds). The peak, however, came in the nineties when boy bands dominated charts worldwide.

Key reasons for the enduring popularity of boy bands:

- **Nostalgia** — The generation that grew up on 90s hits now has purchasing power and a desire for experiences from their youth.
- **Song quality** — The best boyband hits are perfectly crafted pop songs that hold up even after thirty years.
- **Live show** — Boy bands offer a complete experience — singing, dancing, choreography, audience interaction.
- **Social phenomenon** — Attending a boyband concert is a social event that people attend in groups of friends.
- **Cross-generational appeal** — Mothers take their daughters to concerts, thereby expanding the fan base.

### Overview of Boyband Concerts in Czechia 2026

| Artist | Date | Venue | Event Type |
|-----------|-------|-------|----------|
| East 17 | May 16, 2026 | Slezskoostravský hrad, Ostrava | Hradní Oldies Festival |
| Backstreet Boys | 2026 (TBA) | Prague / O2 arena | Solo concert |
| Caught in the Act | 2026 (TBA) | Czech Republic | Nostalgia tour |
| New Kids on the Block | 2026 (TBA) | European tour (Czech Republic TBC) | The Block Revisited Tour |

*Note: Dates and venues marked TBA will be specified. Follow the current offering at [akceostrava.cz](/akce) for events in Ostrava.*

### East 17 at the Hradní Oldies Festival in Ostrava

The confirmed boyband star of summer 2026 in Czechia is **East 17**. The British quartet from east London will perform on May 16 at the Hradní Oldies Festival at Slezskoostravský hrad in Ostrava. The festival offers a unique lineup — alongside East 17, Village People, Michal David, and Tublatanka will perform.

**What makes East 17 exceptional among boy bands:**

- Street image vs. polished competition
- Original songwriting (Tony Mortimer as main songwriter)
- Fusion of pop, R&B, and hip-hop
- Christmas mega-hit Stay Another Day
- 18 million records sold

Tickets for the Hradní Oldies Festival are available from 999 CZK (Standard) through 1,490 CZK (VIP Standing) to 2,990 CZK (VIP Terrace). The complete offering can be found at [akceostrava.cz](/akce).

### The Biggest Boy Bands of the Nineties — Where Are They Now

#### Backstreet Boys

The best-selling boyband of all time with over 100 million records sold. Hits Everybody (Backstreet's Back), I Want It That Way, and As Long As You Love Me are immortal. Backstreet Boys never officially disbanded and regularly release new albums and tour. In 2026, they continue their worldwide tour, which should also visit Central Europe.

#### Take That

British rivals of East 17 who experienced a second wave of massive success after their 2006 reunion. Gary Barlow, Howard Donald, and Mark Owen continue as a trio, and their concerts in the United Kingdom regularly sell out stadiums. A European tour with a Czech stop is among the possibilities for 2026.

#### Boyzone, *NSYNC, and Westlife

Other boy bands with the potential to perform in Czechia in 2026 include Irish Boyzone (No Matter What, Words), American *NSYNC (Bye Bye Bye, It's Gonna Be Me), and Irish Westlife (You Raise Me Up, Flying Without Wings). All three formations are active to varying degrees, and their European tours are not ruled out.

### Why Attend a Boyband Concert in 2026

- **Professionalism** — Scene veterans have thousands of concerts behind them and know how to entertain an audience.
- **Hit flood** — At a boyband concert, you sing along to the entire set, not just three songs.
- **Atmosphere** — Audiences at nostalgia concerts are relaxed and ready to have fun.
- **Affordability** — Nostalgia concerts tend to be more affordable than tours by current megastars.
- **Shared experience** — An ideal opportunity for a trip with friends who grew up on the same music.

### How to Choose the Right Boyband Concert

- **Band lineup** — The more original members, the more authentic the experience.
- **Event type** — A solo concert offers a longer set; a festival brings intense hits plus the bonus of other artists.
- **Location** — Open-air (Slezskoostravský hrad) vs. indoor concert (O2 arena) — both have their charm.
- **Price** — A festival ticket for 999 CZK for four artists can be a better deal than a solo concert.

### Boyband Comparison — Quick Overview

| Boyband | Records Sold | Peak Era | Style | Active in 2026 |
|---------|-----------------|-------------|------|----------------|
| Backstreet Boys | 100+ mil. | 1997–2001 | Pop | Yes |
| East 17 | 18 mil. | 1992–1997 | Pop/R&B | Yes |
| Take That | 45+ mil. | 1992–1996, 2006+ | Pop | Yes (trio) |
| *NSYNC | 70+ mil. | 1998–2002 | Pop/dance | Partially |
| Boyzone | 25+ mil. | 1994–2000 | Pop/ballads | Occasionally |
| Westlife | 55+ mil. | 1999–2012, 2018+ | Pop/ballads | Yes |
| New Kids on the Block | 80+ mil. | 1988–1994 | Pop/dance | Yes |

### Tips for Fans — How to Get the Most Out of a Boyband Concert

- **Prepare a playlist** — Before the concert, listen to the greatest hits so you know the lyrics by heart.
- **Dress comfortably** — You will be dancing and jumping; comfortable shoes are essential.
- **Arrive early** — The best spots near the stage fill up fast.
- **Charge your phone** — You will want to record Stay Another Day or Y.M.C.A.
- **Enjoy it** — Leave the cynicism at home. A boyband concert is about pure joy from music.

## Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)

### Which boyband is performing in Czechia in 2026?

The confirmed boyband concert in the Czech Republic for 2026 is the performance of **East 17** at the Hradní Oldies Festival in Ostrava on May 16. Additional boyband concerts (Backstreet Boys, Take That) are expected — follow the current offering.

### Where can I buy tickets for a boyband concert in Ostrava?

Tickets for the Hradní Oldies Festival with East 17 can be purchased at [akceostrava.cz](/akce). Prices start at 999 CZK for standard.

### Are boyband concerts suitable for children?

Yes, boyband concerts are suitable for all age groups. The music is positive, the atmosphere friendly, and for children it is a great first concert experience.

### Do boy bands sing live at concerts?

Most contemporary boy bands sing live at concerts with playback tracks for the instrumental parts. East 17, Backstreet Boys, and Take That are known for quality live vocals.

### How much does a VIP ticket cost for the Hradní Oldies Festival?

VIP Standing costs 1,490 CZK and VIP Terrace costs 2,990 CZK. The standard ticket is 999 CZK.

body:
## Hradní Oldies Festival 2026 — Complete Festival Guide

On May 16, 2026, the courtyard of Slezskoostravský hrad in Ostrava will transform into the epicenter of nostalgic music. The Hradní Oldies Festival brings a lineup that spans four decades of pop and rock history — Village People, Michal David, East 17, and Tublatanka. It is one of the most significant oldies events of the year in the Czech Republic, and this guide will help you enjoy it to the fullest. From tickets to transport and tips on what to bring — everything in one place.

### Basic Festival Information

| Detail | Info |
|------|--------|
| Name | Hradní Oldies Festival 2026 |
| Date | May 16, 2026 (Saturday) |
| Venue | Slezskoostravský hrad, Ostrava |
| Time | 3:00 PM – 11:00 PM |
| Organizer | RESTARTSTAGE PRODUCTION |
| Lineup | Village People, Michal David, East 17, Tublatanka |

### Why Is the Hradní Oldies Festival 2026 Exceptional

Ostrava hosts dozens of concerts and festivals each year, but the Hradní Oldies Festival 2026 stands out for several reasons.

First — **an international lineup on one stage**. American disco legend Village People, British boyband East 17, Slovak rock icon Tublatanka, and Czech pop king Michal David. Four countries, four genre styles, four generations of hits. You will not find such a combination anywhere else on the Czech festival map.

Second — **the location**. Slezskoostravský hrad is not just a concert venue — it is a historic landmark with an atmosphere no multipurpose arena can match. Stone walls, a castle courtyard, and a city view create a backdrop that elevates the musical experience to something extraordinary.

Third — **affordability**. Four international and domestic stars for admission starting at 999 CZK — that is a very attractive offer in the context of current concert ticket prices.

### Tickets — Categories, Prices, and Where to Buy

| Category | Price | Description |
|-----------|------|-------|
| Standard | 999 CZK | Basic festival entry, access to the main stage |
| VIP Standing | 1,490 CZK | Access to a designated VIP zone with a better view |
| VIP Terrace | 2,990 CZK | Exclusive covered terrace with the best view of the stage |

**Where to buy tickets:** At [akceostrava.cz](/akce) — online purchase with instant e-ticket delivery.

**Recommendation:** The VIP Terrace usually sells out first. If you prefer comfort and an exclusive view, do not leave the purchase to the last minute. Standard tickets offer great value for money — four artists for less than a thousand crowns.

### Festival Lineup — Who Will Perform

#### Village People — Y.M.C.A., In the Navy, Macho Man

American disco formation Village People is one of the most recognizable groups in the history of popular music. Their costumed performances — the Indian, construction worker, police officer, cowboy, soldier, and biker — are iconic and instantly recognizable. Hits Y.M.C.A. and In the Navy transcended the disco genre and became universal party anthems known by everyone regardless of age or musical preference.

The famous choreography to Y.M.C.A. — where audience members form the letters with their arms — is one of the most well-known moments in the history of concert entertainment.

#### Michal David — Nonstop, Poupata, Céčka céčka

Michal David is the most successful Czech pop singer of the eighties and nineties. Songs like Nonstop, Poupata, Céčka céčka, and Každý mi tě brácho závidí are known by every Czech. At the Hradní Oldies Festival, he will present a cross-section of his career — and he guarantees that there will be dancing from the first note to the last.

#### East 17 — Stay Another Day, House of Love, It's Alright

British boyband East 17 will bring to Ostrava the hits that defined the nineties. Stay Another Day — the 1994 Christmas number one — is one of the most emotive pop ballads of the decade. House of Love and It's Alright are energetic tracks ideal for a festival setting.

At the Hradní Oldies Festival, they will play a set full of their greatest hits — for Czech fans, this is one of the few opportunities to see the band live on home soil.

#### Tublatanka — Pravda víťazí, Žeravé uhlíky, Dnes

Slovak rock legend Tublatanka is among the most significant bands on the Czechoslovak music scene. Their song Pravda víťazí became the anthem of the Velvet Revolution in 1989 and still resonates as a symbol of freedom. Žeravé uhlíky, Dnes, and V tejto krajine are also songs that transcended the boundaries of music and became part of the social consciousness.

Tublatanka bring rock energy to the festival — the ideal complement to the disco of Village People, the pop of Michal David, and the boyband sound of East 17.

### Getting to Slezskoostravský hrad

#### Public Transport

Slezskoostravský hrad is excellently accessible by public transport:

- **Trams** — "Výstaviště" stop (lines 1, 2, 11, 12), approximately a 10-minute walk to the castle.
- **Buses** — "Slezskoostravský hrad" stop — directly at the entrance.
- **From the main train station** — By tram approximately 15 minutes to the Výstaviště stop.

#### By Car

- **Parking** — There are paid parking lots near the castle, but capacity is limited. For large events, we recommend parking farther from the center and using public transport.
- **Navigation** — Enter "Slezskoostravský hrad" or the address Hradní 1, Ostrava-Slezská Ostrava.

#### By Train

Direct connections from Prague (approx. 3.5 hours), from Brno (approx. 1.5 hours), from Bratislava (approx. 3 hours). From Ostrava-Svinov or Ostrava main station, take a tram to the castle.

### What to Bring to the Festival — Checklist

- Ticket (electronic or printed)
- ID card
- Comfortable shoes (you will be standing for 8 hours)
- Light jacket or sweatshirt (May evenings in Ostrava can be cool)
- Rain poncho (in case of rain — open-air event)
- Power bank and charging cable
- Cash and payment card
- Earplugs (optional, for the front rows)
- Sunglasses and sunscreen (the afternoon program starts at 3:00 PM)

### Weather, Refreshments, and Accommodation

May in Ostrava offers average temperatures of 15–20°C during the day and 8–12°C in the evening. We recommend layers of clothing and a rain poncho in case of rain.

| Time | Expected Temperature | Recommendation |
|-----|-------------------|------------|
| 3:00 PM (opening) | 17–20°C | Light clothing, sunglasses |
| 7:00 PM (evening program) | 13–16°C | Sweatshirt or light jacket |
| 10:00 PM (closing) | 9–12°C | Jacket, long pants |

Food and drink stalls will be available on-site — grilled specialties, street food, beer, wine, and coffee. If you are traveling from another city, we recommend booking accommodation in advance — hotels in the center (Imperial, Park Inn, Mercure) are within 20 minutes of the castle.

### Safety and Festival Rules

- Bringing your own alcohol, glass containers, pyrotechnics, or dangerous objects into the festival is not permitted.
- A security check takes place at the entrance.
- Medical services will be present on-site.

## Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)

### When does the Hradní Oldies Festival 2026 take place?

The festival takes place on **Saturday, May 16, 2026** from 3:00 PM to 11:00 PM at Slezskoostravský hrad in Ostrava.

### How much do tickets cost for the Hradní Oldies Festival?

Standard 999 CZK, VIP Standing 1,490 CZK, VIP Terrace 2,990 CZK. Tickets are available at [akceostrava.cz](/akce).

### Who is performing at the Hradní Oldies Festival 2026?

Village People, Michal David, East 17, and Tublatanka — four legends from four countries on one stage.

### How do I get to Slezskoostravský hrad?

By public transport (tram to Výstaviště stop, bus to Slezskoostravský hrad stop), by car (limited parking), or by train to Ostrava and then by public transport.

### Is the festival suitable for families with children?

Yes, the festival is suitable for all age groups. The atmosphere at Slezskoostravský hrad is friendly and safe.

### What if it rains?

The festival takes place regardless of weather. We recommend having a rain poncho. VIP Terrace holders have access to a covered area.

body:
## Hradní Oldies Festival 2026 — Lineup, Schedule and Timetable

Hradní Oldies Festival 2026 offers eight hours of music at Slezskoostravský hrad in Ostrava. Four artists — Village People, Michal David, East 17 and Tublatanka — will deliver a programme from 15:00 to 23:00. In this article you will find a detailed introduction to each performer, the expected timetable, a rundown of the hits you will hear, and practical festival information.

### Festival Timetable

The festival runs from the afternoon until late evening. The eight-hour programme gives each performer plenty of room for a full set. There are breaks between performances for stage changeovers and refreshments.

| Time | Programme |
|-----|---------|
| 15:00 | Venue opens, visitor entry |
| 15:30–16:45 | Tublatanka |
| 16:45–17:15 | Break / stage changeover |
| 17:15–18:30 | East 17 |
| 18:30–19:00 | Break / stage changeover |
| 19:00–20:30 | Michal David |
| 20:30–21:00 | Break / stage changeover |
| 21:00–22:30 | Village People |
| 22:30–23:00 | Festival finale |

*Note: The timetable shown is indicative and may change. The final running order will be published before the event on the organiser's website.*

### Tublatanka — Slovak Rock Legend Opens the Festival

Slovak band Tublatanka kicks off the festival programme and sets the bar high. Founded in 1982 in Bratislava, the group ranks among the most important rock acts in Czechoslovak music history. Frontman Maťo Ďurinda is a charismatic singer and guitarist whose voice is instantly recognisable.

#### History and Significance of Tublatanka

Tublatanka was formed in 1982 and their song **Pravda víťazí** became the unofficial anthem of the Velvet Revolution in 1989.

#### Expected Hits in Tublatanka's Setlist

- **Pravda víťazí** — anthem of freedom and revolution
- **Žeravé uhlíky** — emotive power ballad
- **Dnes** — optimistic eighties anthem
- **V tejto krajine** — a song about homeland and identity
- **Skúsime to cez noc** — energetic rocker

**What to expect from the performance:** Energetic concerts with emphasis on guitar sound. Maťo Ďurinda communicates with the audience in both Slovak and Czech. An ideal festival opener.

### East 17 — British Boyband Brings Nineties Hits

Second on the bill are East 17 — a British pop group that sold 18 million records in the nineties and delivered a string of hits that still echo across European airwaves today.

#### History and Significance of East 17

East 17 were formed in 1991 in Walthamstow, east London. Unlike the polished Take That, they opted for a street image and a sound inspired by R&B and hip hop.

#### Expected Hits in East 17's Setlist

- **House of Love** — debut hit that launched their career
- **Deep** — dark R&B track with an urgent beat
- **It's Alright** — positive anthem, ideal for a festival setting
- **Around the World** — cinematic pop with oriental influences
- **Steam** — energetic single from their second album
- **Let It Rain** — melodic hit with a catchy chorus
- **If You Ever** — emotive ballad (originally a duet with Gabrielle)
- **Each Time** — comeback single from 1998
- **Stay Another Day** — iconic ballad, traditionally the closing song of the set

**What to expect from the performance:** An energetic hit-packed set with choreography and audience interaction. Stay Another Day traditionally closes the concert with an emotional moment as the entire audience sings along.

### Michal David — The King of Czech Pop at His Best

Michal David headlines the Czech portion of the programme and his performance guarantees a great time. As the most successful Czech pop singer of the eighties and nineties, he boasts a catalogue of hits unrivalled in Czech music.

#### History and Significance of Michal David

Michal David is the most successful Czech pop singer of the eighties and nineties. The film "Discopříběh" (1987) featuring his music became a cult classic and every Czech knows its songs.

#### Expected Hits in Michal David's Setlist

- **Nonstop** — the most famous Czech disco song of all time
- **Poupata** — romantic ballad with an immortal melody
- **Céčka, céčka** — energetic hit from Discopříběh
- **Každý mi tě, brácho, závidí** — friendship anthem
- **Decibely lásky** — dance classic
- **Discopříběh** — title song of the cult film
- **Bláznivá noc** — party hymn

**What to expect from the performance:** An energetic show full of audience interaction. Michal David is a guarantee that people will dance and sing non-stop.

### Village People — Grand Headliner Closes the Festival

American disco act Village People close the festival programme and bring a show that is one of the most famous in the history of popular music.

#### History and Significance of Village People

Village People were formed in 1977 in New York. Each member represents a different American archetype — a Native American, construction worker, police officer, cowboy, soldier and biker. The hit **Y.M.C.A.** sold over 12 million copies and is one of the most recognisable songs on the planet.

#### Expected Hits in Village People's Setlist

- **Y.M.C.A.** — the most famous disco song in history; the audience forms the letters with their arms
- **In the Navy** — second biggest hit, an energetic anthem
- **Macho Man** — masculine anthem with an iconic riff
- **Go West** — optimistic song later popularised by Pet Shop Boys
- **Can't Stop the Music** — title song from the film
- **San Francisco** — a celebration of the city and its culture

**What to expect from the performance:** An interactive costumed show — you will dance, sing and form the letters Y-M-C-A with your arms. The ideal closing act of the festival.

### Artist Comparison — What Each One Brings

| Artist | Genre | Era | Biggest Hit | Show Energy |
|-----------|------|-----|-------------|-------------|
| Tublatanka | Rock | 80s–90s | Pravda víťazí | Rock, intense |
| East 17 | Pop/R&B | 90s | Stay Another Day | Dance, emotive |
| Michal David | Disco pop | 80s–90s | Nonstop | Party, interactive |
| Village People | Disco | 70s–80s | Y.M.C.A. | Spectacular, participatory |

### Tickets — Category Overview

| Category | Price | Benefits |
|-----------|------|--------|
| Standard | 999 CZK | Festival entry, access to the main stage |
| VIP Standing | 1 490 CZK | Reserved VIP zone with a better view |
| VIP Terrace | 2 990 CZK | Covered terrace, exclusive view, premium comfort |

Purchase tickets at [akceostrava.cz](/akce).

### Tips for the Best Festival Experience

- **Arrive from the start** — Tublatanka at 15:30 is worth it.
- **Plan refreshments for the breaks** — Thirty-minute pauses are ideal for food and drinks.
- **Bring layers** — The festival lasts 8 hours and the temperature will change significantly.
- **Charge your phone** — A power bank is a must for 8 hours of photos.
- **Learn the Y.M.C.A. choreography** — You will need it.

### Getting to the Festival

The tram stop "Výstaviště" is a 10-minute walk from the castle; the bus stop "Slezskoostravský hrad" is right next to the venue. The festival ends at 23:00 — night public transport services are running.

## Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)

### What time does the Hradní Oldies Festival start?

The festival starts at **15:00** when the venue opens. The first performance (Tublatanka) begins at approximately 15:30. The festival ends at 23:00.

### In what order do the artists perform?

The expected order is: Tublatanka, East 17, Michal David, Village People. The final running order will be confirmed by the organiser before the event.

### How much is the cheapest ticket?

A Standard ticket costs **999 CZK** and includes entry to the entire festival with all four artists. Purchase at [akceostrava.cz](/akce).

### Can I bring my own food and drinks?

Bringing your own alcoholic beverages and glass containers to the festival is not permitted. Refreshments are available at stalls within the venue.

### Is the festival covered?

The festival takes place in the open air in the courtyard of Slezskoostravský hrad. Covered space is available only for VIP Terrace ticket holders.

### How long does the entire festival last?

The festival lasts **8 hours** — from 15:00 to 23:00. Each of the four artists has ample room for a full set.

body:
## Hradní Oldies Festival Tickets — Prices, Categories and Where to Buy

On 16 May 2026, Slezskoostravský hrad will become the epicentre of nostalgic entertainment. Hradní Oldies Festival brings legendary Village People, Michal David, East 17 and Tublatanka to a single stage — and you can be there. All you need to do is choose the right ticket category and secure your spot in time. In this article you will find a complete overview of prices, differences between categories and practical tips on how to buy your ticket at the best price.

### Why Hradní Oldies Festival Is the Event of the Year

Ostrava has been experiencing a boom in retro events in recent years, but Hradní Oldies Festival 2026 raises the bar even higher. Artists from different decades and genres meet in one place — from American disco through British pop to Slovak rock and Czech hits. You will not easily find such a combination anywhere else in the Czech Republic.

Moreover, the Slezskoostravský hrad venue itself gives the entire event an unrepeatable atmosphere. Historic scenery, open sky and sound reverberating off castle walls — that is an experience no arena can replicate.

### Ticket Categories and Prices Overview

The festival offers three price categories that differ in comfort level and position within the venue. Each has its own specifics — let us look at them in detail.

| Category | Price | What It Includes |
|-----------|------|-------------|
| Standard | 999 CZK | Entry to the main area in front of the stage, standing |
| VIP Standing | 1 490 CZK | Reserved VIP zone closer to the stage, better view |
| VIP Terrace | 2 990 CZK | Exclusive terrace with seating, premium stage view |

### Standard — 999 CZK

The basic ticket at 999 CZK gets you into the main festival area. This is standing room in the open castle grounds with a direct view of the stage. For most visitors it is the ideal choice — the atmosphere in the crowd during hits like Y.M.C.A. or Disco příběh is simply unforgettable.

What to expect from the Standard category:

- Entry to the main festival zone
- Access to refreshments and festival stalls
- Freedom to move around the venue
- An authentic festival experience right in the middle of the action

### VIP Standing — 1 490 CZK

If you want to be closer to the stage while enjoying a bit more space, VIP Standing is the right choice. For 1 490 CZK you get access to a reserved zone with fewer spectators and a better view of the performers.

This category is popular among those who want a combination of live atmosphere and greater comfort. You do not need to arrive hours early to secure a good spot — the VIP zone guarantees you a quality position.

### VIP Terrace — 2 990 CZK

For those who want to experience the festival at the highest level, there is the VIP Terrace at 2 990 CZK. This is an exclusive space with seating and a premium view of the entire stage. An ideal choice for couples, groups of friends, or anyone who prefers comfort.

The VIP Terrace offers:

- Reserved seating with a view
- Exclusive space with a limited number of places
- A calmer atmosphere for full enjoyment of the music
- Premium service

### Who Performs at Hradní Oldies Festival

The festival lineup is assembled to appeal to the widest possible range of retro music fans. Each performer brings their own unique energy to the stage.

| Artist | Genre | Biggest Hits |
|-----------|------|---------------|
| Village People | Disco, pop | Y.M.C.A., In the Navy, Macho Man |
| Michal David | Pop, disco | Disco příběh, Nonstop, Pár přátel |
| East 17 | Pop, R&B | Stay Another Day, House of Love |
| Tublatanka | Rock, pop-rock | Pravda víťazí, Dnes, Žerucha |

Village People are undoubtedly the main draw — an American disco legend whose Y.M.C.A. is one of the most recognisable songs of all time. Michal David will then ensure that Czech and Slovak audiences sing from the first minute to the last. East 17 bring nineties British pop and Tublatanka add rock energy from Slovakia.

### Where and How to Buy Tickets

Tickets for Hradní Oldies Festival 2026 are available online through the akceostrava.cz portal. We recommend not waiting — events of this format in Ostrava sell out quickly.

The purchase process is simple:

1. Visit the page [akceostrava.cz/akce](/akce)
2. Select Hradní Oldies Festival
3. Choose your desired ticket category
4. Complete the order and payment
5. You will receive your ticket electronically by email

### Practical Tips for Visitors

To enjoy the festival to the fullest, we have prepared several recommendations:

- **Arrive early** — the Slezskoostravský hrad venue has limited capacity and entrance gates may fill up
- **Dress for the weather** — May weather in Ostrava can be changeable; bring a light jacket
- **Parking** — there are paid car parks near the castle; consider public transport or walking from the centre
- **Refreshments** — there will be stalls in the venue, but bring your own basic water supply

### Comparison with Other Events in Ostrava

Hradní Oldies Festival is not the only retro event in Ostrava in 2026. The day before, on 15 May, Kollárovci perform at Slezskoostravský hrad, and in autumn — on 17 October — DJ BoBo brings his spectacular show to Ostravar Aréna. For nostalgia lovers, this year in Ostrava is packed.

### Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)

**How much is the cheapest ticket for Hradní Oldies Festival?**
The basic Standard ticket costs 999 CZK and includes entry to the main festival area.

**What is the difference between VIP Standing and VIP Terrace?**
VIP Standing (1 490 CZK) offers a reserved zone closer to the stage — it is still standing. VIP Terrace (2 990 CZK) provides exclusive seating with a premium view.

**Where does Hradní Oldies Festival take place?**
The festival takes place at Slezskoostravský hrad in Ostrava on 16 May 2026.

**Can I buy a ticket on site?**
We recommend buying tickets in advance online at [akceostrava.cz/akce](/akce). Availability on site is not guaranteed.

**Is the festival suitable for children?**
The festival is primarily aimed at an adult audience, but children accompanied by parents are welcome. Specific conditions can be found on the event page.

**Who performs at the festival?**
The headliners are Village People, Michal David, East 17 and Tublatanka.
