body:
## Best Oldies Festivals in the Czech Republic 2026 — Overview and Comparison

2026 is an exceptional year for retro music fans in the Czech Republic. Festivals and concert events celebrating the golden eras of pop, disco, rock and dance music are taking place across the country. Nostalgia is stronger than ever — and organisers are responding with ambitious lineups packed with international and domestic legends. We have prepared a comprehensive overview of the most interesting oldies festivals happening in the Czech Republic this year.

### Why Oldies Festivals Are Booming

There are several reasons. The generation born between the 1960s and 1980s now forms the economically most powerful segment of the population — and they enjoy spending on experiences that remind them of their youth. At the same time, quality pop, rock and disco from past decades are also winning over younger audiences who discover these genres on social media.

Festival organisers are responding to this demand with increasingly inventive concepts. Instead of one-off concerts, all-day events are emerging with rich programmes, refreshments, entertainment and an atmosphere that transports audiences back several decades.

### Hradní Oldies Festival — Ostrava, 16 May 2026

Undoubtedly one of the most striking retro events of the year. Hradní Oldies Festival takes place at **Slezskoostravský hrad** and offers a unique combination of international and domestic stars.

**Lineup:**
- **Village People** — American disco legend (Y.M.C.A., In the Navy)
- **Michal David** — king of the Czech pop scene (Disco příběh, Nonstop)
- **East 17** — British boyband of the 90s (Stay Another Day)
- **Tublatanka** — Slovak rock stalwart (Pravda víťazí)

**Ticket prices:**

| Category | Price |
|-----------|------|
| Standard | 999 CZK |
| VIP Standing | 1 490 CZK |
| VIP Terrace | 2 990 CZK |

What makes Hradní Oldies Festival exceptional is the location itself. Slezskoostravský hrad provides an atmosphere that no arena or standard festival site can replicate. A concert under the open sky surrounded by historic walls — that is an experience in itself.

[Buy tickets for Hradní Oldies Festival here](/akce).

### Retro Music Festival Prague

Prague traditionally offers a range of retro events, with the most prominent being summer open-air concerts in parks and on the riverbank. Retro Music Festival focuses mainly on the Czech and Slovak pop scene of the 80s and 90s and is popular among families and couples looking for easy-going cultural entertainment.

A typical lineup includes artists such as Petra Janů, Michal David, Helena Vondráčková or a Karel Gott tribute show. The atmosphere is pleasant but lacks the international dimension offered by, for example, the Hradní Oldies Festival in Ostrava.

### Brno Retro Summer

Brno enters the game with summer events at BVV (Brno Exhibition Centre) and the amphitheatre at Kraví hora. Brno's retro events traditionally focus on 80s rock and pop with a dash of Czech new wave. Compared to Prague, the atmosphere is more intimate and authentic.

### Comparison of Key Oldies Festivals 2026

| Parameter | Hradní Oldies Festival (Ostrava) | Retro Music Festival (Prague) | Retro Summer (Brno) |
|----------|----------------------------------|------------------------------|-------------------|
| Date | 16 May 2026 | Summer 2026 | Summer 2026 |
| Location | Slezskoostravský hrad | Various locations | BVV, Kraví hora |
| International stars | Village People, East 17 | Usually not | Occasionally |
| Czech stars | Michal David, Tublatanka | Yes, plenty | Yes |
| Price range | 999–2 990 CZK | 500–1 500 CZK | 400–1 200 CZK |
| Unique atmosphere | Historic castle, open-air | Urban locations | Exhibition centre, park |

### What to Consider When Choosing a Festival

Every oldies festival has its own specific atmosphere and target audience. When choosing, consider the following criteria:

- **Lineup** — are you looking for specific artists, or is the overall atmosphere more important?
- **Location** — open-air at a historic site versus indoor concert versus city park
- **Price category** — from a few hundred to thousands of crowns, depending on the level of comfort desired
- **Transport accessibility** — consider distance, parking and accommodation options
- **Side programme** — some festivals also offer gastronomy, retro markets or dance zones

### Oldies Festivals as Part of a Broader Trend

Retro festivals are not an isolated phenomenon — they fit into a global trend where nostalgia plays an ever-greater role in the entertainment industry. In the United Kingdom, formats such as Let's Rock are thriving; in Germany, Schlagermove and similar events are successful. The Czech Republic is adopting this trend and adapting it to the local context.

Interestingly, oldies festivals in the Czech Republic appeal not only to those who remember the era but also to Generation Z. Hits from the 80s and 90s spread via TikTok and Instagram, where young people discover songs they previously knew only from their parents' stories. Different generations meet at festivals — and that is precisely what creates the unique atmosphere.

### What Lies Ahead for the Oldies Scene

The retro festival trend in the Czech Republic is likely to deepen. Organisers are introducing new formats — themed decade nights, combinations of live performances and DJ sets, fusions of music with gastronomy and fashion of the era.

For 2026 it is clear that Ostrava is becoming one of the key cities for retro music in the Czech Republic. The combination of Hradní Oldies Festival at Slezskoostravský hrad, the Kollárovci concert and DJ BoBo's autumn show at Ostravar Aréna make it a destination that nostalgia fans cannot ignore.

### Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)

**Which oldies festival in the Czech Republic in 2026 has the best international lineup?**
Hradní Oldies Festival in Ostrava — with Village People and East 17 it offers the strongest international cast of any Czech retro event in 2026.

**How much do tickets to oldies festivals cost?**
Prices range from approximately 400 CZK for basic entry to smaller events up to 2 990 CZK for VIP categories at larger festivals such as Hradní Oldies Festival.

**Are oldies festivals suitable for a younger audience?**
Absolutely. Retro music appeals across generations and the atmosphere at these events tends to be friendly and safe.

**Where can I buy tickets for Hradní Oldies Festival?**
Tickets are available on the [akceostrava.cz/akce](/akce) portal — RESTARTSTAGE PRODUCTION handles online sales.

**Are there more retro events in Ostrava in 2026?**
Yes — besides Hradní Oldies Festival, Ostrava also hosts the Kollárovci concert at Slezskoostravský hrad (15 May) and DJ BoBo at Ostravar Aréna (17 October).

title: 80s and 90s Music — The Best Hits and Why They Still Fill Concert Halls
metaDescription: 80s and 90s music — the best hits that still fill concert halls. Why retro pop, disco and dance are making a comeback and where to hear them live in Ostrava.
excerpt: There are songs that outlive generations. Songs that make everyone nod their head automatically, even if they hear them for the first time in twenty years. Music from the 80s and 90s has this power like no other era...
body:
## 80s and 90s Music — The Best Hits and Why They Still Fill Concert Halls

There are songs that outlive generations. Songs that make everyone nod their head automatically, even if they hear them for the first time in twenty years. Music from the 80s and 90s has this power like no other era — and 2026 confirms it in full force. Retro concerts and festivals are selling out, oldies playlists have billions of streams, and artists from these decades are returning to stages in grand style. Why is this happening and which hits are behind it?

### The Eighties — The Decade That Changed Pop

The eighties brought a revolution in popular music production. Synthesisers, drum machines and new recording technologies opened the door to sounds that had never existed before. Synth-pop, new wave, power ballads and modern disco emerged — genres that defined an entire era and whose influence we still feel today.

The most important 80s hits that still resonate:

| Song | Artist | Year | Genre |
|---------|-----------|-----|------|
| Y.M.C.A. | Village People | 1978* | Disco |
| Take On Me | a-ha | 1985 | Synth-pop |
| Livin' on a Prayer | Bon Jovi | 1986 | Rock |
| Never Gonna Give You Up | Rick Astley | 1987 | Dance-pop |
| Disco příběh | Michal David | 1987 | Pop, disco |
| Pravda víťazí | Tublatanka | 1989 | Rock |
| Sweet Dreams | Eurythmics | 1983 | Synth-pop |
| Total Eclipse of the Heart | Bonnie Tyler | 1983 | Power ballad |

*Village People debuted in the late 70s, but their hits defined the disco era that flowed seamlessly into the 80s.

What makes eighties music so unforgettable:

- **Melodic simplicity** — catchy choruses that get stuck in your head
- **Production innovation** — synthesisers gave music a futuristic sound
- **Visual identity** — the birth of MTV linked music with visuals like never before
- **Emotional range** — from the euphoria of dance tracks to the melancholy of power ballads

### The Nineties — The Golden Age of Dance and Pop

If the eighties laid the foundations, the nineties built an empire on them. Eurodance, Brit-pop, grunge, R&B and hip-hop — everyone could find their genre. But it is the dance and pop music of the 90s that holds the strongest position in the retro concert context.

Nineties hits that everyone knows:

| Song | Artist | Year | Genre |
|---------|-----------|-----|------|
| Everybody | DJ BoBo | 1994 | Eurodance |
| Stay Another Day | East 17 | 1994 | Pop |
| Somebody Dance with Me | DJ BoBo | 1992 | Eurodance |
| Hyper Hyper | Scooter | 1994 | Happy hardcore |
| Blue (Da Ba Dee) | Eiffel 65 | 1998 | Eurodance |
| Nonstop | Michal David | 1990 | Pop |
| Macarena | Los del Río | 1993 | Dance-pop |
| What Is Love | Haddaway | 1993 | Eurodance |

The nineties were the golden age of dance hits that worked on dance floors, on the radio and in stadiums. Their strength lies in energy — just a few bars and the whole venue is on its feet.

### Why Retro Hits Still Fill Concert Halls

The phenomenon of old music's popularity has several explanations, ranging from neuroscience to sociology.

**Nostalgia as an emotion.** Music we heard during key periods of life — typically between the ages of 12 and 22 — is inscribed into memory deeply and permanently. When we hear it again, we are transported back to a time of carefree living. This effect is well-documented and psychologists call it the "nostalgia bump".

**Quality of the songs.** Hits from the 80s and 90s went through a strict filter — without streaming platforms, only truly exceptional songs made it to radio stations and charts. Today's listener therefore gets a concentrate of the best from the entire decade.

**Community and shared experience.** At retro concerts, the entire venue sings along. Everyone knows the lyrics, everyone knows the choreography. This collective experience creates a sense of belonging that is increasingly rare in an age of individualised music consumption through headphones.

**Cross-generational appeal.** Parents take their children to concerts by the artists they grew up with. Children discover this music through TikTok and viral trends. A unique bridge between generations is formed.

### Where to Hear 80s and 90s Hits Live in Ostrava

Ostrava offers several opportunities to enjoy retro hits live in 2026:

- **Hradní Oldies Festival** (16 May, Slezskoostravský hrad) — Village People, Michal David, East 17, Tublatanka. Tickets from 999 CZK. [Buy tickets](/akce).
- **Kollárovci** (15 May, Slezskoostravský hrad) — Slovak phenomenon with a retro repertoire
- **DJ BoBo Arena Tour** (17 October, Ostravar Aréna) — spectacular show with hits Everybody, Chihuahua, Freedom and more. Tickets from 975 CZK. [Buy tickets](/akce).

### Retro Music in Numbers

The power of retro music is best illustrated by concrete data:

- The Spotify playlist "80s Hits" has over 15 million followers
- Y.M.C.A. by Village People has surpassed 1 billion streams on streaming platforms
- Average attendance at retro festivals in Europe grew by 40% between 2020 and 2025
- DJ BoBo has played over 3,000 concerts throughout his career — and demand is still growing

### The Influence of the 80s and 90s on Contemporary Music

The retro sound is not only influencing the concert scene — it is also permeating current pop production. Artists such as The Weeknd, Dua Lipa and Miley Cyrus openly draw from the synth-pop and disco of the eighties. Producers are returning to analogue synthesisers and retro sonic textures.

This trend confirms that music from the 80s and 90s is not merely a nostalgic affair — it is a living source of inspiration that shapes the sound of 2026 as well.

### Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)

**What are the most famous 80s hits?**
Among the best-known are Y.M.C.A. (Village People), Take On Me (a-ha), Sweet Dreams (Eurythmics), Livin' on a Prayer (Bon Jovi) and the Czech classic Disco příběh (Michal David).

**Why is 90s music still popular?**
A combination of the nostalgia effect, high song quality and cross-generational transmission — 90s hits are spreading via social media to new listeners.

**Where can I hear retro hits live in Ostrava?**
In 2026 Ostrava offers Hradní Oldies Festival (16 May), the Kollárovci concert (15 May) and DJ BoBo at Ostravar Aréna (17 October). Tickets at [akceostrava.cz/akce](/akce).

**Is retro music suitable for young listeners?**
Absolutely — hits from the 80s and 90s appeal to all age groups. Different generations regularly meet at retro festivals.

**Which 90s genre is most popular today?**
Eurodance is experiencing the greatest renaissance — artists such as DJ BoBo and Scooter continue to sell out arenas across Europe.

title: Retro Festivals and Concerts in Ostrava — A Nostalgia Guide
metaDescription: Retro festivals and concerts in Ostrava 2026 — complete guide. Hradní Oldies Festival, DJ BoBo, Kollárovci and more events for nostalgia fans.
excerpt: In 2026, Ostrava is establishing itself as one of the most prominent spots on the retro music map of the Czech Republic. The combination of unique venues — from the historic Slezskoostravský hrad to the modern Ostravar Arén...
body:
## Retro Festivals and Concerts in Ostrava — A Nostalgia Guide

In 2026, Ostrava is establishing itself as one of the most prominent spots on the retro music map of the Czech Republic. The combination of unique venues — from the historic Slezskoostravský hrad to the modern Ostravar Aréna — and ambitious lineups with international stars makes the Moravian-Silesian capital a destination worth the trip. This guide will help you navigate the retro event offering and plan your nostalgic year.

### Why Ostrava Is Ideal for Retro Music

Ostrava has something that other Czech cities lack — raw authenticity. The city's industrial heritage creates a contrast with nostalgic music that works surprisingly well. Moreover, Ostrava has the infrastructure to host events of various formats and sizes.

Key factors:

- **Slezskoostravský hrad** — an open-air location with an unrepeatable atmosphere for festivals and concerts under the open sky
- **Ostravar Aréna** — a modern hall with a capacity of over 10,000 for large arena shows
- **Transport accessibility** — proximity to the Polish and Slovak borders expands the audience to include international visitors
- **Affordability** — compared to Prague, Ostrava offers comparable experiences at significantly lower accommodation and service prices
- **RESTARTSTAGE PRODUCTION** — professional production that attracts world-class stars to Ostrava

### Calendar of Retro Events in Ostrava 2026

Let us go through the most important retro events of the year in chronological order.

| Date | Event | Venue | Ticket Prices |
|-------|------|-------|----------------|
| 15 May | Kollárovci | Slezskoostravský hrad | See akceostrava.cz |
| 16 May | Hradní Oldies Festival | Slezskoostravský hrad | 999–2 990 CZK |
| 17 October | DJ BoBo Arena Tour | Ostravar Aréna | 975–3 990 CZK |

### Hradní Oldies Festival — 16 May 2026

The main retro event of the spring. Four artists representing different decades and genres of retro music will perform at Slezskoostravský hrad.

**Village People** bring American disco in its purest form. Y.M.C.A. and In the Navy are among the most recognisable songs in the history of popular music — and their live rendition set against a Gothic castle will be unforgettable.

**Michal David** needs no introduction. The king of the Czech pop scene, whose hits Disco příběh, Nonstop and Pár přátel are known by literally every Czech. At an open-air event of this kind, his set of hits will work perfectly.

**East 17** represent nineties British pop. Stay Another Day is one of the most emotional ballads of the entire decade and their performance at the castle will add a melancholic mood that contrasts with the disco energy of the rest of the programme.

**Tublatanka** bring Slovak rock. Pravda víťazí became the anthem of the Velvet Revolution and its power has not faded even after decades.

Tickets: Standard 999 CZK, VIP Standing 1 490 CZK, VIP Terrace 2 990 CZK. [Buy at akceostrava.cz](/akce).

### Kollárovci — 15 May 2026

The day before Hradní Oldies Festival, Kollárovci perform at the same venue — Slezskoostravský hrad. This Slovak musical family has built a huge fan base in recent years thanks to energetic live performances and a repertoire that blends folk, pop and retro hits.

For visitors travelling from further away, combining both days is ideal — arrive on Friday for Kollárovci and continue on Saturday with Hradní Oldies Festival.

### DJ BoBo Arena Tour — 17 October 2026

The autumn peak of the retro season in Ostrava. Swiss showman DJ BoBo brings his legendary show to Ostravar Aréna, packed with pyrotechnics, dancers, visual effects and of course hits like Everybody, Chihuahua, Freedom and Somebody Dance with Me.

DJ BoBo concerts are known as some of the most elaborate shows in the entire genre. It is not just about the music — it is a complete visual and emotional experience.

**Price categories:**

| Category | Price |
|-----------|------|
| Tribune | 975 CZK |
| VIP Tribune | 2 490 CZK |
| Golden Seating | 3 990 CZK |

[Tickets for DJ BoBo in Ostrava](/akce).

### How to Plan a Retro Weekend in Ostrava

Ostrava is not just about concerts — the city offers a range of other experiences that you can combine with a retro event visit.

**May weekend (15–16 May):**

- Friday: arrival, Kollárovci at Slezskoostravský hrad
- Saturday: tour of Dolní oblast Vítkovice, Hradní Oldies Festival
- Sunday: walk through the centre, Stodolní ulice, departure

**October weekend (17 October):**

- Saturday: city tour, shopping, DJ BoBo at Ostravar Aréna
- Sunday: ZOO Ostrava or Landek Park, departure

### Tips for Visitors from Other Cities

If you are travelling to Ostrava specifically for a retro event, here are some practical recommendations:

- **Transport** — the train journey from Prague takes approximately 3.5 hours, from Brno just under 2 hours. Both concert venues are easily accessible by public transport.
- **Accommodation** — book in advance, especially for the May date when two events take place on consecutive days. Affordable hotels can be found near the centre.
- **Parking** — parking capacity near Slezskoostravský hrad is limited; prefer public transport. The situation at Ostravar Aréna is better.
- **Dining** — Ostrava offers excellent gastronomy. Try local restaurants in the city centre or on Stodolní ulice.

### Ostrava versus Prague — Where Are Retro Concerts Better?

The eternal debate. Prague has more events, but Ostrava offers specifics that the capital does not:

| Aspect | Ostrava | Prague |
|--------|---------|-------|
| Unique venues | Slezskoostravský hrad, industrial spaces | Halls, parks |
| Audience atmosphere | Warmer, more energetic | Larger, but more reserved |
| Price level | Lower (accommodation, food, services) | Significantly higher |
| International lineups | Village People, DJ BoBo, East 17 | Comparable |
| Transport from PL/SK | Excellent | Limited |

For retro music fans from Moravia, Silesia, Poland and Slovakia, Ostrava is clearly the logical choice.

### The Future of the Retro Scene in Ostrava

RESTARTSTAGE PRODUCTION, the organiser of key retro events in Ostrava, is pointing in a clear direction — Ostrava aims to become a retro music destination of Central European significance. The combination of quality venues, professional production and the city's strategic location creates ideal conditions for this.

For nostalgia fans it is clear — you cannot miss 2026 in Ostrava.

### Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)

**What retro events are taking place in Ostrava in 2026?**
Kollárovci (15 May), Hradní Oldies Festival with Village People and Michal David (16 May) and DJ BoBo Arena Tour (17 October).

**Where can I buy tickets for retro concerts in Ostrava?**
Tickets for all RESTARTSTAGE PRODUCTION events are available at [akceostrava.cz/akce](/akce).

**How much is the cheapest ticket for a retro event in Ostrava?**
The lowest price is 975 CZK for a tribune seat at DJ BoBo and 999 CZK for Standard at Hradní Oldies Festival.

**Is it better to go to Hradní Oldies Festival or DJ BoBo?**
It depends on your preferences — Hradní Oldies Festival offers genre variety under the open sky, while DJ BoBo delivers a spectacular indoor show. Ideally, visit both.

**Can multiple retro events in Ostrava be combined in one weekend?**
Yes — on 15 and 16 May, Kollárovci and Hradní Oldies Festival take place at the same venue, making the May weekend ideal for retro fans.

title: Fesťáček Ostrava 2026 — Complete Guide to the Children's Festival
metaDescription: Fesťáček Ostrava 2026 in Bělský les — programme, performers, tickets at 599 CZK. Karol a Kvído, Štístko a Poupěnka, Míša Růžičková and more.
excerpt: Looking for the ideal summer event for the whole family? Fesťáček Ostrava 2026 is the answer. This children's festival, held on 11 July in the beautiful setting of Bělský les, will offer a full day of music, ...
body:
## Fesťáček Ostrava 2026 — Complete Guide to the Children's Festival

Looking for the ideal summer event for the whole family? Fesťáček Ostrava 2026 is the answer. This children's festival, held on 11 July in the beautiful setting of Bělský les, will offer a full day of music, fun and unforgettable experiences for the youngest — and their parents too. In this guide you will find everything you need to know: from the programme and performers through practical information to tips on how to make the most of the day.

### Basic Information about Fesťáček 2026

| Parameter | Detail |
|----------|--------|
| Date | 11 July 2026 (Friday) |
| Venue | Bělský les, Ostrava |
| Time | 08:30–18:00 |
| Ticket price (3rd wave) | 599 CZK |
| Target group | Families with children, pre-school and school age |

Fesťáček is one of the largest children's festivals in the Moravian-Silesian Region. Its concept is simple — an entire day dedicated to children, with performances by the most popular children's artists, interactive zones and an atmosphere that both young and old will enjoy.

### Who Performs at Fesťáček

The Fesťáček 2026 lineup is assembled from the biggest stars of the Czech children's scene. Each performer brings their own unique style and energy to the stage.

#### Karol a Kvído

A phenomenon of Czech children's entertainment in recent years. Karol a Kvído combine music with education — their songs are catchy, the lyrics educational and the performances interactive. Children love them and parents appreciate that their little ones learn something along the way. At Fesťáček you can look forward to their biggest hits and plenty of singing together.

#### Štístko a Poupěnka

A legendary duo from television screens. Štístko a Poupěnka are among the most enduring certainties of the Czech children's scene — their fairy-tale world, full of songs and stories, has fascinated children for many years. The live show is full of colours, dancing and interaction with the audience. For many children, meeting Štístko and Poupěnka is the highlight of the day.

#### Bibi Bum

An energetic show that gets even the shyest children moving. Bibi Bum is built on rhythmic music, dancing and singing together. Their performances are dynamic, loud and immensely entertaining — exactly what a children's festival needs.

#### Míša Růžičková

A beloved face of the Czech children's scene. Míša Růžičková brings a fairy-tale atmosphere, beautiful melodies and a gentle approach to the youngest audience members. Her performance is ideal even for the very smallest children who are just getting their bearings at the festival.

### What a Day at Fesťáček Looks Like

The festival starts at 08:30 and ends at 18:00 — that is nearly ten hours of entertainment. The day is structured so that children have plenty of stimulation but also space for rest.

Typical schedule of the day:

- **08:30–09:30** — venue opens, settling in, children's zones
- **09:30–12:00** — morning performances on the main stage
- **12:00–13:30** — lunch break, free play in the grounds
- **13:30–16:00** — afternoon programme, main stars
- **16:00–18:00** — closing programme, farewell

The exact performance schedule will be published before the event on the organiser's website.

### Why Bělský les Is the Ideal Venue

Bělský les is one of Ostrava's most popular recreational areas. For a children's festival, this location is absolutely perfect — for several reasons:

- **Nature** — the festival takes place in greenery, surrounded by trees and lawns. Children have space to run and play.
- **Shade** — in July you will appreciate the shade of the trees, which is absent from typical festival grounds
- **Safety** — an enclosed venue in a quiet area with no car traffic
- **Accessibility** — Bělský les is easily reachable by public transport and by car from across Ostrava

### Tickets — Price and Where to Buy

Tickets for Fesťáček 2026 are currently in the 3rd wave at **599 CZK**. The price is per person (adults and children above a certain age — conditions can be found on the event page).

We recommend buying tickets as early as possible — previous editions sold out and with each new wave the price rises.

Buy tickets online at [akceostrava.cz/akce](/akce).

### Practical Tips for Parents

Fesťáček is an all-day event and proper preparation will save you a lot of hassle. Here are our tried-and-tested tips:

**What to bring:**

- Sun hat and sunscreen — July sun can be intense
- Plenty of water — although there will be refreshments in the grounds, your own supplies always come in handy
- Spare clothes for children — children will get dirty at the festival, that is a law of nature
- Snacks — especially for the youngest who have special dietary needs
- A rain poncho — the weather can surprise
- A blanket or mat — for resting on the grass between performances

**What to leave at home:**

- Stress and rush — Fesťáček is about relaxation, not about catching every performance
- Large luggage — travel light; you will be moving around the grounds a lot

**Additional recommendations:**

- Arrive early — car parks and entrance gates can fill up
- Agree on a meeting point with your children — in case you get separated in the grounds
- Charge your phone — you will want to take photos and videos
- Consider ear protection for the youngest children — amplified music can be loud

### Who Is Fesťáček Suitable For

The festival is primarily designed for families with children aged approximately 2–10. In practice, however, older children and certainly parents, grandparents and other accompanying adults will enjoy it too.

| Age Group | Suitability | What Will Engage Them |
|----------------|----------|--------------|
| 0–2 years | With reservations | Atmosphere, but it can be noisy |
| 2–5 years | Ideal | Štístko a Poupěnka, Míša Růžičková |
| 5–8 years | Ideal | Karol a Kvído, Bibi Bum, everything |
| 8–12 years | Suitable | Interactive zones, Bibi Bum |
| Adults | Yes | Children's joy, atmosphere, relaxation |

### Fesťáček in the Context of Other Summer Events in Ostrava

Summer 2026 in Ostrava offers a diverse range of events for different target groups. If you are planning a summer trip to Ostrava with your family, Fesťáček is the ideal starting point — but far from the only option.

In May, the festival season is preceded by events at Slezskoostravský hrad — the Kollárovci concert (15 May) and Hradní Oldies Festival (16 May). In autumn, DJ BoBo performs at Ostravar Aréna (17 October). For families with older children, these events may be an interesting addition.

### Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)

**How much does a ticket to Fesťáček 2026 cost?**
The ticket is currently in the 3rd wave at 599 CZK. The price may increase in subsequent sales waves.

**Where does Fesťáček take place?**
In Bělský les in Ostrava — one of the most beautiful recreational areas of the city.

**How long does Fesťáček last?**
The festival starts at 08:30 and ends at 18:00 — nearly ten hours of programme.

**Who performs at Fesťáček?**
Karol a Kvído, Štístko a Poupěnka, Bibi Bum and Míša Růžičková — the cream of the Czech children's scene.

**Is Fesťáček suitable for very young children?**
For children from 2 years the festival is ideal. For younger children, consider whether they might be bothered by louder amplified music.

**Where can I buy tickets?**
Tickets are available online at [akceostrava.cz/akce](/akce).

**Are there refreshments in the grounds?**
Yes, there will be refreshment stalls in the grounds. We still recommend bringing your own water and snacks, especially for the youngest children.

**How do I get to Bělský les?**
Bělský les is accessible by public transport and by car. Exact transport and parking information will be published before the event on the organiser's website.

title: Children's Festivals in Ostrava and Surroundings 2026 — Guide for Parents
metaDescription: Complete overview of children's festivals in Ostrava and surroundings 2026. Fesťáček, family events, dates, ticket prices and tips for parents in one place.
keywords: things to do with kids Ostrava
excerpt: In recent years, Ostrava has transformed into a city that thinks of families with children. Every year new festivals and events tailored for the youngest keep appearing — from fairy-tale performances to music programmes...
body:
## Children's Festivals in Ostrava and Surroundings 2026 — Guide for Parents

In recent years, Ostrava has transformed into a city that thinks of families with children. Every year new festivals and events tailored for the youngest keep appearing — from fairy-tale performances through music programmes to sports and educational activities in the open air. 2026 will be no exception. We have prepared a complete overview of children's festivals planned in Ostrava and the nearby area so you can plan family weekends well in advance.

### Why Ostrava?

The Moravian-Silesian capital offers a unique combination of large outdoor spaces, good transport accessibility and a tradition of cultural events. The city actively supports family programmes and many festivals take place in parks, at castles or in natural areas where children can play freely. For parents from across the region — from Opava through Frýdek-Místek to Havířov — Ostrava is a natural entertainment hub.

### Calendar of Children's Festivals 2026

| Date | Festival | Venue | Price From |
|---|---|---|---|
| 11 July 2026 | Fesťáček | Bělský les, Ostrava | 599 CZK (3rd wave) |
| Summer 2026 | Fairy-Tale Forest | various locations | free / low entry |
| Summer 2026 | Children's Day in Ostrava | Komenského sady | free |
| Autumn 2026 | Family Day at the Castle | Slezskoostravský hrad | according to programme |

The main event of the year is undoubtedly **Fesťáček in Bělský les**, held on 11 July 2026. The festival brings the biggest children's stars of the Czech scene to one place — from Karol a Kvído through Štístko a Poupěnka to Bibi Bum and Míša Růžičková. Details can be found in our [Fesťáček programme](/akce/festacek).

### What to Consider When Choosing a Festival

Every family has different needs. Before deciding which festival to attend, consider the following criteria:

- **Children's age** — some festivals are more suitable for toddlers, others for pre-schoolers or school-age children
- **Type of programme** — musical, theatrical, sports or combined
- **Facilities** — availability of toilets, changing tables, shade and refreshments
- **Transport accessibility** — parking, public transport, distance from the stop
- **Programme length** — all-day festivals require different preparation than two-hour events
- **Price** — some events are free, others require tickets bought in advance

### Fesťáček — The Biggest Children's Festival in the Region

In a short time, Fesťáček has established itself as the most popular children's festival in the Moravian-Silesian Region. It takes place in the natural grounds of Bělský les, which offers plenty of space, shade and grassy areas for the youngest. The programme features performances by stars that children know from television and YouTube — Karol a Kvído, Štístko a Poupěnka, Bibi Bum and Míša Růžičková.

Tickets in the current 3rd wave cost **599 CZK** and are available on the [AKCE OSTRAVA](/akce) portal. We recommend not waiting — previous editions sold out.

### Slezskoostravský hrad — A Backdrop for Family Events

Slezskoostravský hrad is one of the most attractive venues for family events in all of Ostrava. In 2026 a range of events will take place here — from the Kollárovci concert (15 May) through Hradní Oldies Festival (16 May) to seasonal family programmes. The castle offers:

- **Historic setting** that children love
- **Large grounds** with plenty of room for free movement
- **Permanent attractions** — climbing wall, mini-golf, maze
- **Refreshments** right in the castle grounds
- **Good accessibility** by public transport and car

### Tips for Parents — How to Survive a Children's Festival

A family festival can be a great experience but also a demanding marathon. Here are some tried-and-tested tips:

1. **Arrive on time** — the best spots near the stage fill up quickly, plus you will avoid queues at the entrance
2. **Pack light but smart** — a blanket to sit on, sunglasses, sun hat, sunscreen and plenty of fluids
3. **Have a Plan B** — if the child tires of the main programme, explore the side attractions
4. **Charge your phone** — you will be photographing, filming and possibly navigating
5. **Set a meeting point** — it is easy to get separated in a larger venue
6. **Expect queues** — for toilets, refreshments and attractions

### Comparison of Children's Festivals in Ostrava

| Criterion | Fesťáček | Fairy-Tale Forest | Children's Day |
|---|---|---|---|
| Size | large (thousands of visitors) | medium | medium |
| Price | 599 CZK | free / low | free |
| Age group | 2–10 years | 3–8 years | 3–12 years |
| Main programme | children's star concerts | fairy tales, theatre | sports, competitions |
| Length | all day | morning | afternoon |
| Refreshments | yes, stalls | limited | yes |

### How to Get to Festivals in Ostrava

Most large festivals take place at locations accessible by public transport. Bělský les, where Fesťáček is held, is reachable by bus. Slezskoostravský hrad lies near the centre and a tram stop. If you are driving, expect parking to be limited on event days — we recommend arriving early or using park-and-ride facilities.

### What Parents Who Have Attended Festivals Say

Feedback from previous editions of children's festivals in Ostrava is unequivocally positive. Parents particularly appreciate:

- **Programme quality** — well-known performers, professional sound
- **Safety** — fenced grounds, medical service
- **Accessibility** — both in terms of price and transport
- **Atmosphere** — a friendly environment where children feel at ease

### Tickets and Practical Information

Most tickets for children's festivals in Ostrava can be purchased online in advance. The advantage of early purchase is not only a lower price (wave pricing system) but also the certainty of getting in. A complete overview of family [events in Ostrava](/akce) can be found on the AKCE OSTRAVA portal operated by RESTARTSTAGE PRODUCTION.

### Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)

**What children's festivals are taking place in Ostrava in 2026?**
The main children's festivals include Fesťáček in Bělský les (11 July), family events at Slezskoostravský hrad and a number of other smaller events throughout the summer. A current overview can be found at [AKCE OSTRAVA](/akce).

**How much do tickets to Fesťáček 2026 cost?**
Tickets for Fesťáček are currently in the 3rd wave at 599 CZK. The price increases with each subsequent wave, so we recommend buying as soon as possible.

**Are children's festivals in Ostrava suitable for toddlers?**
Yes, most festivals — including Fesťáček — are designed for children from approximately 2 years of age. The grounds have grassy areas and plenty of space for the youngest. We always recommend checking the specific event conditions.

**Where can I find a complete overview of family events in Ostrava?**
A complete overview of concerts, festivals and family events in Ostrava can be found on the [AKCE OSTRAVA](/akce) portal operated by RESTARTSTAGE PRODUCTION.

**Can I bring my own food and drinks to the festival?**
Conditions vary by festival. At most open-air events you can bring your own non-alcoholic beverages and snacks. Details are listed in each event's terms and conditions.

title: Fesťáček — Programme, Attractions and Tips for Parents

body:
## Fesťáček — Programme, Attractions and Tips for Parents

Fesťáček ranks among the most sought-after children's festivals in the Moravian-Silesian Region. It takes place on **11 July 2026** in the natural grounds of **Bělský les in Ostrava** and promises a full day of fun for the whole family. In one place you will find the biggest children's stars of the Czech scene, dozens of attractions and thousands of families with children. In this article you will find everything you need to know — from the programme through practical tips to answers to the most common questions.

### When and Where Fesťáček Takes Place

- **Date:** 11 July 2026 (Friday)
- **Venue:** Bělský les, Ostrava
- **Ticket price:** from 599 CZK (current 3rd wave)
- **Tickets:** [AKCE OSTRAVA](/akce/festacek)

Bělský les is an ideal location for a summer festival — it offers mature trees that provide natural shade, grassy areas for rest and enough space so that even with high attendance you do not feel cramped.

### Performers — Who You Will See at Fesťáček

The organisers from RESTARTSTAGE PRODUCTION have prepared a stellar lineup that covers the tastes of children from toddlers to school-age kids:

| Performer | Profile |
|---|---|
| **Karol a Kvído** | The most popular children's duo in the Czech Republic — educational songs, interactive show |
| **Štístko a Poupěnka** | Iconic characters from Czech Television, loved by generations of pre-schoolers |
| **Bibi Bum** | Energetic musical show full of dance and fun |
| **Míša Růžičková** | Singer and presenter, queen of children's events |

Each performer brings something different to the stage — from gentler fairy-tale songs to dance hits that will not let even the parents stand still.

### Karol a Kvído — The Festival's Main Draw

Karol a Kvído are a phenomenon of the current Czech children's scene. Their YouTube channel is among the most viewed in the Czech Republic — not just in the children's category but overall. Songs like "Hlavně že se máme rádi" and "Zvířátka" have hundreds of millions of views and have become the childhood soundtrack of an entire generation.

### Štístko a Poupěnka — A Classic That Never Disappoints

Štístko a Poupěnka are synonymous with quality children's entertainment in the Czech Republic. Their programmes on Czech Television are watched by millions of children and live performances are always sold out. At Fesťáček, the youngest visitors in particular can look forward to it — the programme is ideal for children aged 2 to 6.

### Bibi Bum and Míša Růžičková — Energy and Dance

Bibi Bum bring a modern approach to children's entertainment — dynamic songs, dance choreography and interactive elements that draw the entire audience into the action. Míša Růžičková then rounds out the lineup with her charm and experience from television and the festival scene. Together they ensure there is no weak link in the programme.

### Attractions and Side Programme

Fesťáček is not just about music. The Bělský les grounds transform for one day into a children's paradise full of activities:

- **Bouncy castles and inflatable attractions** — a classic that children love
- **Creative workshops** — painting, modelling, making small souvenirs
- **Fairy-tale trails** — interactive routes through the grounds with tasks and rewards
- **Sports zone** — ball games, obstacle courses
- **Face painting** — face art with fairy-tale motifs
- **Food zone** — refreshment stalls, children's menu, ice cream, drinks

The programme runs all day, so even when your favourite star is not performing, the children will not stop.

### Practical Tips for Visitors

#### What to Bring

- **Sun hat and sunscreen** — July sun in Ostrava can surprise you
- **Blanket or mat** — for sitting by the stage and resting in the shade
- **Plenty of fluids** — your own water bottle will save queuing at stalls
- **Spare shoes or sandals** — the terrain is grassy but can be wet after rain
- **Rain poncho** — in case of a summer shower
- **Small backpack** — free hands are priceless at a festival
- **Cash and card** — not all stalls accept card payments

#### Transport and Parking

Bělský les is accessible by bus from the centre of Ostrava. If you are driving, expect limited parking near the grounds — organisers usually arrange temporary parking areas, but capacity is limited. We recommend arriving early or using public transport.

#### What Age Children for Fesťáček

The festival is ideal for children aged **2 to 10**. The youngest will appreciate Štístko a Poupěnka, school-age kids will be thrilled by Karol a Kvído. The programme is designed so that every age group finds something for them.

### Tickets — Wave Sales System

Tickets for Fesťáček are sold in waves, with the price rising with each wave:

| Wave | Price | Availability |
|---|---|---|
| 1st wave | lowest price | sold out |
| 2nd wave | discounted price | sold out |
| **3rd wave** | **599 CZK** | **currently on sale** |
| 4th wave | higher price | coming soon |

The current 3rd wave offers tickets at **599 CZK**. We recommend not delaying your purchase — previous editions sold out and the price will rise with the next wave. Buy tickets at [AKCE OSTRAVA](/akce/festacek).

### What to Do If It Rains

Summer festivals carry one risk — the weather. Fesťáček takes place in all weather conditions (unless the organisers decide otherwise for safety reasons). Bělský les offers natural shelter under the trees, but in case of rain we recommend:

- Bring a rain poncho (an umbrella obstructs other people at a festival)
- Wear waterproof footwear
- Bring spare clothes for the children
- Monitor the weather forecast and the organisers' social media

### Other Family Events in Ostrava 2026

If Fesťáček excites you, Ostrava offers a number of other family events in 2026. **Kollárovci** perform at Slezskoostravský hrad (15 May) and **Hradní Oldies Festival** takes place (16 May). In October, **DJ BoBo** appears at Ostravar Aréna with his spectacular show, which will captivate older children and teenagers too. A complete overview of [events in Ostrava](/akce) can be found on the AKCE OSTRAVA portal.

### Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)

**When does Fesťáček 2026 take place?**
Fesťáček takes place on 11 July 2026 in the Bělský les grounds in Ostrava.

**How much do tickets to Fesťáček cost?**
The 3rd sales wave is currently running at 599 CZK. The price increases with each subsequent wave. Buy tickets at [AKCE OSTRAVA](/akce/festacek).

**Who performs at Fesťáček?**
Karol a Kvído, Štístko a Poupěnka, Bibi Bum and Míša Růžičková will perform at Fesťáček 2026.

**Is Fesťáček suitable for toddlers?**
Yes, the festival is suitable for children from approximately 2 years of age. The youngest visitors will particularly enjoy the Štístko a Poupěnka performance and the inflatable attractions.

**How do I get to Bělský les?**
Bělský les is accessible by bus from the centre of Ostrava. You can park near the grounds by car, but capacity is limited — we recommend arriving early.

**Does Fesťáček take place even in rain?**
Yes, the festival takes place in all weather conditions. In case of extreme conditions, the organisers may adjust the programme. Follow current information on RESTARTSTAGE PRODUCTION's social media.

title: Best Family Events in Ostrava 2026 — Where to Go with Kids

body:
## Best Family Events in Ostrava 2026 — Where to Go with Kids

The question "Where to go with the kids?" plagues parents every weekend. In Ostrava and surroundings the answer is easier than it seems — the city offers a diverse range of family events, festivals and concerts in 2026 that will entertain children and adults alike. From spring events at Slezskoostravský hrad through summer festivals in nature to autumn spectacular shows at Ostravar Aréna. We have prepared a selection of the very best.

### Overview of Top Family Events 2026

| Date | Event | Venue | For Whom | Price From |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 15 May | Kollárovci | Slezskoostravský hrad | whole family | according to sales |
| 16 May | Hradní Oldies Festival | Slezskoostravský hrad | families, nostalgia | according to sales |
| 11 July | Fesťáček | Bělský les | children 2–10 years | 599 CZK |
| 17 October | DJ BoBo — Arena Tour | Ostravar Aréna | families, teenagers | according to sales |

Tickets for all listed events can be found on the [AKCE OSTRAVA](/akce) portal.

### Spring — Slezskoostravský hrad Comes Alive

The spring season in Ostrava traditionally starts at Slezskoostravský hrad, one of the most iconic places in the city. The castle offers a unique atmosphere — historic walls, sprawling grounds and views of the city create a backdrop that no modern concert hall can match.

#### Kollárovci — 15 May 2026

Slovak band Kollárovci rank among the most popular family performers in Central Europe. Their concerts are renowned for their great atmosphere, live music and energy that sweeps the audience across generations. At Slezskoostravský hrad they will play their beloved programme full of folk hits, modern arrangements and entertainment for the whole family.

Why bring the kids:
- **Live band** — children will see real musicians, not playback
- **Dance atmosphere** — people dance from the first minute at Kollárovci
- **Outdoor setting** — the castle offers space for movement and relaxation
- **Friendly audience** — Kollárovci attract families, not wild parties

#### Hradní Oldies Festival — 16 May 2026

The day after Kollárovci, the programme continues with Hradní Oldies Festival. This festival is ideal for parents who want to combine their love of 70s, 80s and 90s music with a family outing. While the adults enjoy nostalgic hits, children can explore the castle grounds with its attractions.

The castle offers year-round:
- Climbing wall
- Mini-golf
- Maze
- Historical exhibitions
- Cafe and refreshments

### Summer — Fesťáček in Bělský les

The peak of the summer season for families with children is **Fesťáček** — the biggest children's festival in the Moravian-Silesian Region. It takes place on **11 July 2026** in the natural grounds of Bělský les and offers a full day of music, attractions and fun.

Stars that children know from their screens will take the stage:

- **Karol a Kvído** — phenomenon of the Czech children's scene with millions of fans
- **Štístko a Poupěnka** — iconic duo from Czech Television
- **Bibi Bum** — modern children's music show
- **Míša Růžičková** — singer and presenter

Tickets are in the current 3rd wave at **599 CZK** — we recommend not waiting, as the price rises with each wave. The detailed [Fesťáček programme](/akce/festacek) can be found on our portal.

### Autumn — DJ BoBo at Ostravar Aréna

Who says arena concerts are not for families? **DJ BoBo** and his Arena Tour 2026 prove the opposite. The Swiss eurodance legend's show is a visual spectacle — dozens of dancers, LED walls, lasers and pyrotechnics create an experience that will amaze adults and older children alike.

**DJ BoBo** performs in Ostrava on **17 October 2026** at [Ostravar Aréna](/akce/dj-bobo-arena-tour-2026-ostrava) at the address Ruská 3077/135. The hall holds approximately 12,000 spectators and offers comfortable facilities — from seating to refreshment stalls.

Why bring teenagers:
- **Visual show** — DJ BoBo is known for spectacular productions
- **Hits they know** — Everybody, Freedom and Chihuahua are known to the young generation thanks to social media
- **Safe environment** — an arena concert with organised entry and seating

### Things to Do with Kids in Ostrava — Tips Beyond Festivals

Ostrava offers families much more than just festivals. You can head out for activities outside the main event dates too:

- **ZOO Ostrava** — one of the most beautiful zoos in the Czech Republic, open year-round
- **Svět miniatur** — interactive exhibition for curious children
- **Dolní oblast Vítkovice** — industrial heritage site with a programme for families
- **Landek Park** — mining museum with an adventure exhibition
- **Aquapark Ostrava** — water fun in any weather

### How to Plan a Family Year in Ostrava

The proven approach is to plan the main events at the beginning of the year and buy tickets in presale. Not only will you save — Fesťáček for example offers a wave system with rising prices — but you will also secure a place at events that regularly sell out.

Recommended schedule:

1. **January–February** — follow announcements of new events, buy tickets in the first wave
2. **March–April** — plan spring outings to the castle and into nature
3. **May** — Kollárovci and Hradní Oldies Festival at Slezskoostravský hrad
4. **June** — preparation for Fesťáček, check tickets
5. **July** — Fesťáček in Bělský les (11 July)
6. **August** — summer open-air events, swimming pools
7. **September** — back to school, cultural season starts
8. **October** — DJ BoBo at Ostravar Aréna (17 October)

### Family Event Budget — How Much to Expect

The family culture budget is an important factor. Here is an indicative overview of costs for a family of four:

| Item | Approximate Price |
|---|---|
| Tickets (2 adults + 2 children) | 1,200–4,000 CZK |
| Parking | 0–200 CZK |
| Refreshments on site | 300–800 CZK |
| Attractions and souvenirs | 200–500 CZK |
| **Total per event** | **1,700–5,500 CZK** |

Tip — follow [AKCE OSTRAVA](/akce) for current offers and discounted presale tickets.

### Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)

**What are the best family events in Ostrava in 2026?**
The main family events include Kollárovci at Slezskoostravský hrad (15 May), Hradní Oldies Festival (16 May), Fesťáček in Bělský les (11 July) and DJ BoBo at Ostravar Aréna (17 October). A complete overview can be found at [AKCE OSTRAVA](/akce).

**How much does a family trip to a festival in Ostrava cost?**
It depends on the specific event. As a rough guide, expect 1,700 to 5,500 CZK for a family of four including tickets, refreshments and parking.

**Are events in Ostrava suitable for small children?**
Yes, especially Fesťáček is designed for children from 2 years. Events at Slezskoostravský hrad are suitable for all age groups. For arena concerts we recommend considering the child's age — loud music may not be suitable for the youngest.

**Where can I buy tickets for family events in Ostrava?**
Tickets for events organised by RESTARTSTAGE PRODUCTION can be purchased on the [AKCE OSTRAVA](/akce) portal. We recommend buying in presale at discounted prices.

**Where to go in Ostrava with kids in bad weather?**
In bad weather we recommend Aquapark Ostrava, Svět miniatur, Dolní oblast Vítkovice or indoor playgrounds. Arena concerts (e.g. DJ BoBo) take place indoors regardless of the weather.

title: Karol a Kvído, Štístko a Poupěnka — Children's Stars at Fesťáček

body:
## Karol a Kvído, Štístko a Poupěnka — Children's Stars at Fesťáček

Fesťáček 2026 brings a lineup to Ostrava that children's festivals could only dream of a few years ago. On one stage on **11 July in Bělský les**, the four biggest names in Czech children's entertainment will gather — Karol a Kvído, Štístko a Poupěnka, Bibi Bum and Míša Růžičková. Who are these performers, how did they win over millions of young fans, and what can you expect from their shows? Here is the complete guide to the stars of Fesťáček.

### Karol a Kvído — The YouTube Phenomenon That Conquered the Czech Republic

If you have a child aged 3 to 8 at home, Karol a Kvído need no introduction. Their YouTube channel is among the most-watched in the Czech Republic — not just in the children's category but overall. Songs like "Hlavně že se máme rádi", "Zvířátka" and "Barvičky" have hundreds of millions of views and have become the childhood soundtrack of an entire generation.

#### What Makes Karol a Kvído Exceptional

- **Educational content** — songs teach colours, numbers, animals and basic social behaviour
- **High production quality** — music videos are professionally shot with colourful sets
- **Interactive concerts** — live performances are full of games, quizzes and singing together
- **Authenticity** — Karol and Kvído come across naturally and children respond to them spontaneously

#### What to Expect from the Fesťáček Performance

Live concerts by Karol a Kvído are an experience for children and parents alike. Expect:

- An interactive show with the audience — children answer, sing, dance
- The biggest hits performed live
- Colourful costumes and sets
- Meeting favourite characters
- Performance length approximately 45–60 minutes

Tip for parents — get to the stage area early. Karol a Kvído are the festival's main draw and spots in the front rows fill up quickly.

### Štístko a Poupěnka — Generation After Generation

Štístko a Poupěnka are a household name in Czech children's culture. Their programme on Czech Television has been watched by children for many years and the characters have become icons of pre-school entertainment. Unlike newer YouTube stars, Štístko a Poupěnka have the backing of public-service television and a time-tested concept.

#### Duo Profile

| Characteristic | Detail |
|---|---|
| Known from | Czech Television — Déčko |
| Target group | 2–6 years |
| Style | Fairy-tale songs, nursery rhymes, short stories |
| Live show | Interactive, slower pace, ideal for the youngest |

#### Why Štístko a Poupěnka Are Important for Fesťáček

While Karol a Kvído appeal to a broader age group, Štístko a Poupěnka are the anchor for the very youngest. Children aged 2 to 4 react to them with enthusiasm because they know them from their daily television viewing. For many toddlers, Fesťáček may be their very first festival — and Štístko with Poupěnka will make sure they remember it with a smile.

What to expect:
- A calmer atmosphere suitable for the youngest audience
- Well-known songs and nursery rhymes
- Characters in costumes that children recognise instantly
- Short interactive segments with simple games

### Bibi Bum — The New Wave of Children's Entertainment

Bibi Bum represents the modern current in Czech children's music. The project is built on energetic songs with catchy melodies and dance choreographies that children quickly pick up. In the era of TikTok and short videos, Bibi Bum perfectly understands how to reach today's youngest generation.

#### What Bibi Bum Brings to the Festival

- **High energy** — performances are dynamic from the first to the last minute
- **Dance elements** — simple choreographies that children can master at first try
- **Modern sound** — production matches current trends, not outdated children's standards
- **Visual appeal** — colourful costumes, props and stage design

Bibi Bum is ideal for children aged 4 to 9 who want more than just sitting and listening — they want to move, dance and be part of the show.

### Míša Růžičková — A Versatile Artist

Míša Růžičková is a name parents know from television screens and from previous festival editions. As a singer, presenter and entertainer rolled into one, she brings professionalism and charm to the stage, instantly winning over audiences of all ages.

At Fesťáček you can expect from Míša Růžičková:

- Songs performed live with professional backing
- Witty presenter segments between programme blocks
- Interaction with children in the audience
- Positive energy and experience from dozens of festival performances

### Performer Comparison — Who Is Right for Which Child

| | Karol a Kvído | Štístko a Poupěnka | Bibi Bum | Míša Růžičková |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Ideal age** | 3–8 years | 2–6 years | 4–9 years | 3–8 years |
| **Style** | educational pop | fairy-tale songs | dance pop | pop, presenting |
| **Energy** | medium/high | calm | high | medium |
| **Interactivity** | very high | medium | high | high |
| **Known from** | YouTube | Czech Television | YouTube, social media | TV, festivals |

### How to Plan Your Fesťáček Day by Your Child's Age

For families with **toddlers (2–3 years)**:
- Priority is Štístko a Poupěnka
- Plan breaks for rest and snacks
- Use quieter zones of the grounds away from the main stage
- Do not be afraid to leave early if the child gets tired

For families with **pre-schoolers (4–6 years)**:
- Start with Štístko a Poupěnka, continue with Karol a Kvído
- Try creative workshops between concerts
- Let the children dance at Bibi Bum
- A full day is realistic if you alternate activity and rest

For families with **school-age children (7–10 years)**:
- The main targets will be Karol a Kvído and Bibi Bum
- The sports zone and attractions will fill gaps in the programme
- Older children can stay until the end of the programme

### Tickets for Fesťáček 2026

Tickets for Fesťáček are currently in the **3rd wave at 599 CZK**. Admission includes access to all performances and the side programme. We recommend buying as soon as possible — previous waves are already sold out and the price rises with each subsequent wave.

Buy tickets on the [AKCE OSTRAVA](/akce/festacek) portal, operated by RESTARTSTAGE PRODUCTION.

### Other Events in Ostrava for Families

Fesťáček is not the only opportunity to enjoy a great family event in Ostrava. In the 2026 calendar you will find:

- **Kollárovci** at Slezskoostravský hrad — 15 May
- **Hradní Oldies Festival** — 16 May
- **DJ BoBo** at Ostravar Aréna — 17 October

Follow the complete overview of [events in Ostrava](/akce) on the AKCE OSTRAVA portal.

### Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)

**Who performs at Fesťáček 2026?**
Karol a Kvído, Štístko a Poupěnka, Bibi Bum and Míša Růžičková will perform at Fesťáček 2026.

**Is Karol a Kvído suitable for a two-year-old?**
Karol a Kvído are ideal for children from 3 years and up. For two-year-olds we rather recommend Štístko a Poupěnka, who have a calmer programme adapted for the youngest.

**How much is admission to Fesťáček?**
The current 3rd wave offers tickets at 599 CZK. Admission includes access to all performances and side attractions. Buy tickets at [AKCE OSTRAVA](/akce/festacek).

**When and where does Fesťáček take place?**
Fesťáček takes place on 11 July 2026 in the Bělský les grounds in Ostrava.

**Will there be other attractions at Fesťáček besides concerts?**
Yes — Fesťáček offers bouncy castles, creative workshops, fairy-tale trails, a sports zone, face painting and a food zone with refreshments.
